review_title: Ahmad Earl Grey Tea, 100TB ~ The Most Exquisite Earl Grey I've Ever Tasted
review_text:
I first came across Ahmad Earl Grey Tea a number of years ago, while I was visiting a friend who lives out in Seaford, Long Island (NY). I was waiting for her to meet me in a mall, there, & I just happened into a Mideastern grocery-deli to browse and look at the great variety of exotic foods & spices they had for sale.

By & by, I started getting thirsty & asked the shopkeeper whether he had cups of hot tea for sale. He showed me the varieties of tea they had & I chose Ahmad Earl Grey Tea, since Earl Grey has been my favorite ever since I was introduced to it back in the late '70s by an English friend of mine, who would prepare it with milk or cream & sugar or honey.

Well, I had had excellent cups of Earl Grey tea in the past -- Twinings, Bigelow, Island-Rose & others -- but nothing I had previously tasted had fully prepared me for the sublimely fragrant, yet delicate & nuanced bouquet of aroma & flavor that literally "took me by surprise" upon my first sip of Ahmad Earl Grey Tea... aroma & flavor that was not just a simple one-dimensional taste but one that seemed to unfold as you drank it, revealing delightfully unexpected subtleties, layers & shades of enchantingly fragrant aromas & flavors.

Surprisingly, the high quality of the tea leaves & the bergamot used to impart the flavor were clearly distinguishable to me in the tea's unmistakably delightful yet delicate aroma, not only when opening the box of 100 tea bags, or the individual envelopes containing the bags of tea... but quite detectable, in the tea bag itself, even after having made a cup of tea or two, making me wonder whether they might have discovered some very effective, secret manner of infusing the bergamot aroma into the tea leaves, or the tea bag, such that the aroma didn't dissipate after having made a cup of tea, or even while standing on the shelf of the grocery, as I've noticed sometimes happens with lesser grades of Earl Grey tea.

In any event, after such an incomparable experience with Ahmad Earl Grey Tea, it is the only Earl Grey tea that I choose to drink, unless I happen to be out somewhere where it is, unfortunately, not available.

It is at such times that I am so appreciative to have come across Ahmad Earl Grey Tea.

Though I often purchase it at Mideastern food markets, for instance in Astoria, Queens, where it is readily available at rock-bottom prices & fresh from the importers, nevertheless, for those of you who are too far away to do so, the fact that it is available here on Amazon.com is already quite a boon, especially if you order a number of packages of it to lessen the impact of the shipping charges.

If you do order Ahmad Earl Grey Tea & try it, I'm quite sure that you'll find that it soon becomes for you, as it has for me, "a most exquisite delight, definitely not to be missed!"
I agree that this jelly is delicious. I serve it spread on cornmeal crepes filled with goat cheese. Everyone always loves them

review_title: Buffy loves 'em, no sugar, last awhile

It is a little pricey but I have yet to find something less expensive with the same quality and taste. Overall, I was happy enough with them to buy again. I might try the vanilla (because of other positive reviews) but for sure I will order some more chocolate.

I must preface my review with the point that I'm not sure if these were out of date; I do not have the best of faith in the seller from whom I bought them. If they were not extremely old, however, I would say they are not very powerful or effective. When I have purchased other moth traps (I have tried four different brands) the pheromone pads always have a distinct odor immediately upon opening the packet of the most effective ones. These exuded no smell whatsoever. Also, the better ones begin catching moths immediately. A moth circled the vicinity of this trap for a good hour without feeling enticed enough to go inside. The next day, not one moth is in the trap. Finally, the sticky surface inside the trap is not the stickiest I've seen and does not cover the entire inside of the trap. Definitely would not recommend.
The packaging is so cute on all of Ashby's teas and the tea is exceptional. I would recommend this tea to anyone.
Great for Dieters. Better taste than baked chips. I like these with the regular pop chips for good flavor. Just right on saltiness. Target carries them also but not all flavors.

review_title: PB2 may be for you if...
review_text: You don't mind losing creaminess along with the fat. It smells great but tastes quite different than my usual natural peanut butter. I do enjoy adding it to protein shakes and in my oatmeal. I just find the texture undesirable as a dip or on a sandwich.
The blanched almonds were everything we had hoped they would be. Fresh-tasting, large, and perfectly blanched. We were able to make our favorite holiday treat using this product.

SUPER FAST SHIPPING!!!!! AMAZING!!!!! I have eaten Blue Plate Mayo for as long as I can remember.... I am so happy I can still enjoy its great taste even here in Virginia.... Thank You Amazon for allowing me to be able to purchase it through you and Thank You Blue Plate company for making it available on amazon now I can continue to have my Mayo of choice!!!

review_title: Really terrible! Try another flavor. Don't waste your money!
review_text: I am a big fan of Primal Strips. In fact, I loved the first flavor so much (Texas BBQ) I decided to buy others and really enjoyed it all with the exception of Mesquite Lime. Luckily, this was the last flavor I purchased and I was displeasantly surprised. I had a rough time even swallowing my first bite! Yes, it was that bad. The "lime" in this was horridly instense, very vinegar like. It is really all you can taste. So... if you like biting into a meaty-textured product with an overbearing flavor of vinegar, you may probably enjoy this flavor. Otherwise, if you are like me, stick to the other enjoyable, tasty flavors this wonderful vegan product has to offer.
Love this brand of bread. High in fiber, tastes great, makes the best toast. Like the wonderful breads you find in Europe
I've been a Zevia fan since it came on the market. The grape is the best flavor... it tastes like regular grape soda, not at all diet-like.

RiceSelect Organic Texmati has become the rice of choice in this house, and is consumed nearly every day. Wonderfully consistent texture, rich, nutty flavor, and it fluffs nicely. The packaging is convenient (and non-BPA).

To the 1-star reviewers, claiming that this product is GMO, 30 seconds of research would have shown you that it is NOT.

"...all RiceSelect products are produced from our non-GMO proprietary seed and is grown by our farmers under strict supervision in the United States. This complete control through the production process allows us to ensure that our products will not be co-mingled with other varieties that may have GMO traits."

I love this this rice. Would recommend it to anyone who likes brown rice!

We purchased a six box carton of the product. We were very disappointed with the product, as it tastes very stale and does not have the flavor that we remembered. It leaves a dry chalky aftertaste. We are feeding the boxes to the birds.

I have tried several of the flavors in this oatmeal line, and this chocolate oatmeal and the blueberry muffin flavor are to die for. However, WHY THE HELL IS IT SO EXPENSIVE HERE??? I can get it at Walmart for $1.39 box. I normally think Amazon has great prices, but this is ridiculous.

I bet this is the smallest package of chips that you can ever receive. Do NOT be deceived by the images that the seller shows you because the chips can is half the size of your hand! The chips taste OK, not too good.

review_title: Efagold Organic coconut oil, 32 ounce jar - good stuff
review_text: I don't know what effects I will experience, however, it tastes good. It arrived promptly, and I received two 32 ounce jars. I add it to my coffee each morning, and it gives the coffee a very nice rich flavor. I use it on my arms, my legs, my feet. It makes my skin soft, and in particular, my feet feels less dry even after just one application. I think it is better then the moisturing lotions you find in the stores. Amazing, that you can eat this stuff and use it for skincare too.
I would save your money on these. They look better than they are. Neither my husband nor myself cared for them. Kind of like chewing on a hard waffle. I won't be buying them again.
This rice is amazing! It smells like hot buttered popcorn when it's cooking and it tastes so god that it doesn't need any seasoning, sauce or anything else added to it.
This coffee will wake you up in the morning. If you like mild coffee, stay away. The package is easy to open and store.

I love the mild scent and silky feel of this product. I have short, dry hair and live in the southern California desert. I spend a lot of time outside and under the hot desert sun. This product has made a difference; my lackluster, spiky-looking short hair (it isn't supposed to be spiky) is tamed, shiny and smooth without my spending a lot of time with gels and a curling iron. It's a nice product to use and it does as it promises. I'm sure I'll be buying this product in the future.

I have been feeding my greyhounds these treats for years. My hounds can be a little finicky, but they love these treats. They are expensive, relative to other biscuits, but I find they are a good addition to their diet. The treats are easy on the teeth. Since they are protein treat, be careful not to overindulge your pet on a regular basis. They are great treats to take on walks as you can stuff them in your pocket easily. I have found prices can vary from vendor to vendor, so shop around.

I guess I shouldn't expect much in terms of taste from baby food but my son who was 8 months at the time just didn't like it at all (I bought every flavor and they all taste about the same). I tried it and it taste kinda pasty like flourlish in addition from being bland (the bland part I expect).
I have a cat with sugar diabetes and this is the only food he likes that is high in protein. Happy I can purchase it.

Amazon's packaging really bad, Frst time I ordered this - no packaging or protection for product, arrived torn all to pieces and product damaged. I called Amazon, said they would send again properly. Same thing the second shipment, no packaging or protection, just thrown in the box, arrived ripped all to pieces, and product all over box. Don't understand Amazon not caring how their product arrives.....

review_title: VERY flimsy containers, arrived all smashed up
review_text: I ordered one of these cases (packages of six) to give as Christmas presents to multiple people. Sadly, upon arrival, every single individual container was smashed open. The external box was not mangled - I have to believe this was a byproduct of the brittle, thin, flimsy individual plastic containers, in combination with having been shipped along with giant cashew tins in a large box with minimal packing materials. Although I think had the container packaging been sturdier this would not have been a problem. In any event, it was a disappointment that none of the packages arrived intact.

Westbrae Natural Vegetarian Organic Chili Beans are a delicious blend of kidney, black, and pinto beans. They make a colorful blend for chili. I used them in a coffee/cocoa vegan chili from Veg News Magazine (Our big love issue, June 2010) recipe. The beans are rich and flavorful. They're a perfect blend for meatless chilies, or whatever calls for a combination of beans. They'd be great in a vegetarian tamale pie topped with cornbread, or tucked into burritos/enchiladas. Beans are a natural source of protein. They're also superior to most meat substitutes. The World in your Kitchen: Vegetarian recipes from Africa, Asia and Latin America for Western kitchens has many bean-based dishes, as does Jamie's America: Easy Twists on Great American Classics, and More when Oliver hits the trail in Wyoming, and makes his own twist on fireside chili. Delicious, organic and nutritious!

Unbleached flour should be no more then $0.50 per pound.
Walmart sells gold medal brand for 2.38 per 5 pound bag.

I have several favorite recipes that use tomato paste, but they many of them only use a couple of tablespoon. With canned paste, I usually ended up throwing out the rest of the can after a week in the refrigerator made it taste like the can. I first used Amore paste when I lived in Europe and I loved it. It has a rich tomato flavor, no metal taste from the can and it keeps for weeks in the refrigerator. It's more expensive than the small cans, but considering that I don't throw any away, it's cheaper in the long run.

This stuff makes a great filling meal. I eat a lot of vegetable soup which I really like but it just isn't that filling. Now I just add a cup of tvp to my soup and it becomes a hearty stew. I also like adding this to my pasta sauce. I am vegetarian and I find this a great alternative for meat. The texture is great and if you hydrate this with some type of sauce or bouillon it takes on a great flavor. Super easy to cook with and turns out great.

We have now ordered these twice through Amazon, because all of our local grocery stores have stopped carrying this flavor. They are quick to mix up and the honey pieces make them naturally sweet. No other topping needed.

Have usd this product for many many years when I lived in Hawaii. Finding it through Amazon.com was a surprise - but a very pleasant one. This product is manufactured in Hawaii and the factory does not sell directly to the public and does not have wide continental US distribution. This was so easy and as usual, with amazon.com, a pleasant experience. And SO glad to find one of my favorite cooking items.

I think it is Very Expensive !!
I can get it at local Indian or Chinese store for $20 in California.

Trying Illy espresso for the first time I was really looking forward to it, primarily because of all the publicity the company gets. After following the directions in my new espresso maker I pulled the shots and came away disappointed. This is very average espresso.

I've been drinking espresso for 20 years and making it at home for the last 6. The crema was below average (very thin) as was the flavor (especially when I paid $11.95 plus shipping for an 8.8 ounce container). I have since pulled other shots with the same result: flavor was not robust and very, very average.

This marinade started out from Durkee foods and changed to Weber but the item stayed the same. This is a fantastic steak marinade but becoming harder to find. We do hope that it is not discontinued, that would be a big mistake and we would not purchase any other Weber products and tell our friends to do the same.
I must say, I am not too thrilled with the taste, but as long as I bought it, I will add a little fruit juice concentrate to it to use it up, ut I probably will not order it again.
If you miss Oreo's look no further. These are as close as you can get!
I bought this coffee for work as we were bored with our current selection and it was a massive hit. It has nice rich flavour with no nasty after taste.
I bought the peanut brittle for my son in Iraq & he says it is good, so I must agree!!

I LOVE this coffee. Unfortunately, I have a box and a half that will never get used. Why? Because I bought them and 4 out of the first 10 cups used blew open inside my Keurig brewer, blowing hot water and coffee grounds all over the inside of my brewer and all over my countertop.

I can't say what the problem was - maybe I just got a bad batch of K-Cups, but for there to be that many defective cups in one box was very odd. I've never, ever had that happen with any other K-Cups by Green Mountain or any other company.

Anyone wanna buy a box and a half of these? :-)
My grandson made a lot of new little friends with these! He loved them, and his friends loved them as well!! I was tickled to see him so happy to get these addressed to him at his home!

I just started making iced drinks with my Keurig - based on another review, I tried Tazo Awake for iced tea, and it's great - I use 16ozs. of water per K-Cup - you can make it stronger if you like, of course

review_title: Great tasting green tea and such a great deal.
review_text:
A very delicate green tea with a smooth light taste and an subtle floral tones and no bitter aftertaste.

In the 20+ years we have been drinking Ahmad teas not even once have we been disappointed. The superb quality of Ahmad teas has remained consistent throughout the years.

I am so happy to find Ahmad teas available here (thanks Amazon!) because only a few stores carry it in my area and they are quite a few miles away. The current deal for the pack of 6 is superb (much cheaper than I can find locally).

This is a great tasting tea, which I am happy to recommend.

I have eaten other Pamela products and have been really impressed with the quality. I was really disappointed with the luscious chocolate cake mix. It was decent at best when fresh and a few hours later when the cake had cooled down the texture changed and it became really dense and chewy, very unpalatable. I spit out the bite I had taken and threw the rest of the cake away.
great taste, very juicy. few, if any stems. i usually get the pavich jumbos, but my last batch was a little too dried out.
Reasonably decent flavor but POWDERED herbs do not hold too well in cooking and that very leafy quality does add to the dish.

I saw the Orgain promo at my local vitamin store and asked the sales person about it. After reading the ingredients I decided to try the chocolate. I really liked the taste, the consistency and realized later on that I had no negative side effects afterwards. Sometimes I have difficulty with digestion, burping, bloated feeling. Not with Orgain. I decided to buy a few 12 packs and have my husband try it too. He's 63 years old, I'm 56. If he's in a rush in the morning, he now drinks his Orgain in the car on the way to the city. He's a big guy, 6'3" and 275 lbs. He agrees that Orgain is a great way to start the day, replace a snack with Orgain, and feel great. I love the 16 grams of protein. I can go for 4 hours and not feel hungry or like I'm running on empty. I know I'm not missing out on important nutrients that can sustain my aging body. Thanks Orgain!
After traveling to Costa Rica, our famiy fell in love with Costa Rican coffee. These are the dark beans, which are so full of flavor. So rich and delicious! You have to try it!

Wow! My friend Tina comes up to me with this herb tea, to which she's added, of all things, SUGAR and CREAM... I was maybe skeptical, but it was delicious. I need to cut the carbs so I substitute Splenda, but it's still excellent. Just try finding it in a store...

The bully sticks were otherwise fine, except the tons of MAGGOTS in the package. Seriously. Gross.

I don't know what kind of quality control failure leads to a bunch of maggots eating the bully sticks inside the package, BUT safe to say I now consider this brand to not be of the "Finest Quality" they claim to be.

Won't be purchasing this brand again. Didn't even bother with a refund.

They were certainly packed well, with dry ice and all. I don't think they would have thawed out in a month. I've only tried the chocolate peanut butter and it was OK. For the price I was expecting much more. I like it best when almost completely thawed. Then is tastes like a frosty from Wendy's.

This hard to get item is not only tastey but a great
snack to control hunger.

Bought it because it's on sale, but it turned out to be a super disappointment.

Like the others said, it's not edible. What I feel is that it's can not be called salty. It's absolute salt!

I have to wash it in hot water for 5 minutes and then eat it. What a mess!
My three dogs love Greenies. So much so that they know the exact time every evening they are supposed to get them! They act like kids in a candy store!

I had the same issue as the 1 star reviewer in the similar product:

http://www.amazon.com/Mangia-Carmelina-Italian-Tomatoes-28-Ounce/dp/B000R8YIFI/ref=pd_sim_gro_6

I feel a bit mislead for the same reason. Let's face it: anyone who thinks they are going to get real San Marzano certified tomatoes at this price is dreaming a bit, but at least change the picture to represent exactly what is being sold.

That being said, the tomatoes are Italian organic and are good for still a decent price for organic tomatoes. I bought these for making tomato sauce for Italian Pizza and for pasta and they are actually very good for this.

4 stars for the quality, 2 stars for the mis-representation

First, I'm not a huge fan of such "instant" meals. But. . . Having said that. . . Sometimes you're in the mood for something quick-and-easy. This item is "okay". To make it a little better, adding additional veggies really helps. The "broccoli florets" are like tiny dried bits of brown paper. If you have some fresh veggies, I would leave the packet of "broccoli florets" out completely.

Kraft's Jell-O Sugar free instant pudding is a really easy way to make a wonderful dessert! This classic after dinner luxury is a great source of calcium and has no fat, so you don't have to feel guilty eating it. Also its sugar free so anyone that's diabetic can also have this wonderful little bit of heaven. It has this sweet flavor that has been chosen by people everywhere and continues to do so! I recommend it for people of all ages!
I make several batches and freeze them. My 16 month old loves them as well. I make a batch for her using mini muffin pans.
Our household buys both raw and roasted macadamias and this is our preferred roasted brand, a consistent high quality and a price that I have yet to beat from any reputable vendor.

My two doxies love this dog food! They both have nice shiny coats and are at a good weight. It doesn't have any ingredients in it that my older Doxie is allergic to so I started them on it about 6 months ago. It's a little pricey but my dogs are worth it. :-)

This is a very good natural product for helping with bloating, irregularity, and constipation. It tastes great and works. I was recently told about this product by a person who loves it. I wish I knew about it before now!

Nice bags, good size, good handles for over the shoulder carrying, Velcro tab to roll them up for easy storage. Made of a parachute type material. Not sure how well they hold up to wear and tear but they DO have reinforced seams (called French seams) which should give them added strength. I'll update, IF I run into problems.
My grand-daughter turned me on to these snacks on a trip to Oregon. Loved them! So, now they are my favorites for everyday! They fill you up and yet they are so good for you.

review_title: original mississippi mud pie's dessert mixes ( 2 large mixes)
review_text: Recieved the other mississippi mud pie mix wanted to make one before xmas. Made one want to say it is the most delious chcolate mississippi mud pie I and my friends have ever tasted they loved how it was Said it was almost like lava cake. Thank you very much. looking forward on ordering some of your bread since I have your address ( the prepared pantry)It was worth waiting for the one that wasn't shipped. Thanks again.We all loved it.

I love these snacks, I've bought them loose whenever I found them for a ridiculous price because I love them. Then I found them to order on Amazon for a bit of a discount (being bulk).... and I am actually quite disappointed, both boxes I bought are pretty stale, even thought the expiration date is 2013. I can only guess the storage is not good where they are kept through Amazon.

The flavor is great still, and they are edible, but this is the last time I purchase them through here.

Had this for the first time in NYC and went back to the bagel shop to get it for the rest of the week. It is calorie free, and includes 3% juice. Great strong Grapefruit flavor. Sweetened with Splenda (wish it were Stevia, but hey, nothing's perfect). Nice alternative to a Diet Coke/Pepsi. It reminded me of limonata soda in Italy or Orangina, but with a Grapefruit flavor. I haven't tried the other varieties.

I have used this sauce for many dishes. Yes, it is good for oriental and stir fry, but I use it as a base for many sauces when cooking steak and pork chops...richly flavored and not high in sodium. Wonderful product.

This stuff is great. As good as anything I've had at Starbucks. Smooth and rich. Full of flavor. Very satisfying and warming on a cold day. It's not low-cal though, so be careful how much you drink. One cup is 4 Weight Watcher PointsPlus points.
My dogs love these little treats. As other people have said, they smell strongly, but in a good way. I like that they are easy to break apart into training sized portions.

These dates are priced very well. They are quick and safe snack - no pits. Not as meaty as medjool dates, and it is a mixed bag. That is, some of them are more moist than the others. Some are on the dry side, but not too dry to make this unpleasant. Good option for a healthy snack that is cheaper than premium dates.

I have to admit, with a son who is an EXTREMELY picky eater, I thought these were going to go flying out the window!
But DS was taken by them and I am praising God for Koala Crisp, they are so good!

I love the fact that I can make other things with this cereal too. I've made almond crisp cookies, marshmallow crispy bars, peanut butter crispy balls ... ....

Thank you EnviroKidz! Now, if only he could have corn ... but alas, I have to be thankful in everything and I'm thankful for this cereal!!

PS ... It's also very yummy with almond milk! Yuuuuummy!!!

I am disappointed in this product. I had expected something with more flavor considering the description note: "its hearty and rich full flavor."

I prepare several dishes with Guinness Stout and even a small amount will produce a flavor that is unmistakable. The amount of stout in this recipe must be a small fraction because I could not detect it at all and certainly no hint of Worcestershire Sauce, which usually makes its presence known in dishes such as this.

For me it was much too bland and had a "pasty" flavor I associate with undercooked wheat flour thickening.

I was looking for something with less sugar than Gatorade and easy on my stomach when I rediscovered coconut water. When I was little we lived in Brownsville, TX and had access to fresh coconuts. I had almost forgotten the taste of coconut water and was very pleased to find it in such convenient packaging. Plus, free shipping makes it a bargain.

review_title: Love sesame seeds; yet this leaves a lot to be desired
review_text:
This is one of three Mrs. May's 2-ounce snacks that I've had. Even though I love sesame seeds, this flavor/variety really was disappointing. It almost tastes stale - or at least lacks flavor. I'd recommend Mrs. May's pumpkin seed or blueberry; but not the white sesame crunch.

It is great to be able to buy the 2 ounce sizes for these snacks. Really wish Mrs. May's would sell a variety pack of 2 ounce packages.

This noodle bowls just spicy, but tasteless. I would recommend you all to try on DAILY TOMYUM by ACECOOK from asian Market. It is tasteful and you will drink to last drop. Again, it is DAILY brand and TOMYUM flavor. So called: DAILY TOMYUM

review_title: Best gluten free pancake mix...so far
review_text: Just want to share that the Pamela Baking & Pancake Mix is the best I've tried so far. When following the directions, the batter is a little too watery, so I add just a little bit more mix and voila, great pancakes. My husband (who is not gluten free) likes them too.

review_title: Wish I had known more about Kombucha
review_text: After reading that some people have issues with Kombucha tea, I Googled it and read some more. I recommend that others do the same before drinking it. I am sure that Yogi Tea produces their Kombucha in a careful environment, but it can cause health problems. It also does not appear to be appropriate for people with Candida despite some claims. It contains alcohol, sugars, fungi, yeast and molds, and a study showed that it did not improve symptoms of people with Candida. I've been drinking it almost daily and have been having unexplained stomach aches lately and will try eliminating this tea to see if it helps, now that I know more. Again, read up on Kombucha and make your own informed decision.
This glaze is super smooth and yummy. I have tried all kinds and it is just as good as the ones I buy in Italy !!

review_title: Bewley's (and Barry's too) are my favorites.
review_text:
This blend was my first experience drinking Bewley's tea. I found that I liked it very much. Loved it,that is. There was one cup of it that I felt was probably the best tea I'd EVER tasted, but I've never been able to duplicate the flavor of that one cup! --Have found that my tea is best if I make it in a pot instead of a cup and carefully follow basic classic good tea-making instructions, i.e., pour boiling water in the pot to prepare it beforehand, then when tea-water is boiling, pour out the hot water from the teapot, put in tea-bags (1 to each 8 ounces of water), pour in the boiling tea-water, place the lid, let steep 4 or 5 minutes. It seems that Tea made in a pot comes out mellower, less harsh-tasting than in a cup. Still, I keep getting different flavor results with each different pot of tea I make. Very mysterious problem. Maybe I need to time the process precisely with each cup or batch and note down the results of each different timing, then see if the timing produces a consistent result every time.

(I used to flavor my tea with honey, but now I prefer Xylitol sugar substitute which is mild but satisfying, has less calories, and possibly is healthier.)

I also love Barry's Tea. Both Bewley's and Barry's are my super-favorites. (Not available in my grocery so am happy that I can order both brands through Amazon and can get super-saver no-shipping-fee.)

I had this tea when visiting Ireland and loved it. It is very much like PG. Tips - one of my favorite teas, but more malty. This makes a great breakfast cuppa and is nice in the afternoon as well. Enjoy.

If you love instant decaf you will love this K-cup coffee. The taste and aroma are pure Sanka.

I use a Keurig machine because I don't LIKE instant.

So far, one of the worst k-cups I've sampled.

These are quite possibly the best snack that was ever invented. The perfect size for a quick bite, or to get you through the day. You'll need a nice glass of milk to wash them down.

The cake itself tastes just like an Oreo cookie, but better somehow. The creme center is the real deal. I buy these whenever they're on sale, and take every box the store has. Now that amazon has them, I'm going to get them here!

This is a good shampoo with a pleasant, apple-y scent. I don't think a shampoo that claims to be for "color repair" should contain sodium laureth sulfate -- I'd rather have less lather and have my color last longer. That said, the lather is generous, and the shampoo left my hair feeling clean and not stripped of moisture.

I ordered three shampoo/conditioner products from Amazon, and all three leaked during shipping. They were wrapped in plastic, so it wasn't a huge mess, but it would be nice if Amazon could find a way to ship shampoo without it leaking.

I don't think this shampoo is anything special, and probably not worth paying extra for.

I am very pleased with the cotton candy extract I purchased. It is good quality and has a nice flavor, not too strong and not too weak. I have used it to make cookies and cupcakes and have received many compliments!
This coffee is excellent! Not to strong, not to weak when made as an 8 oz.cup. It is by far one of the best values for Kcup coffee lovers. Enjoy Life, Drink it up!

review_title: Bugles are everything Fritos wishes they were.
review_text: Bugles are a much more pleasant corn snack than even Fritos. A little taste of sweetness, thinner-walled chips, less salt, and completely deliciously crispy and scrumptious.

review_title: Not as great as I'd hoped
review_text:
They were discontinuing these at my local grocery store and after purchasing this... I can kind of see why. I know that this isn't going to be an eating experience akin to fresh or even frozen tuna steaks, but I expected a little more than this.

Taste-wise, there's not a lot of mesquite flavor. This was more salty than anything else. It's not a horrible flavor, but when you compare it to some of the flavors that abound in the bagged/canned/ready-to-eat tuna market this just falls short. I've tasted far better.

Texture-wise, my tuna was rather dry in texture. I wasn't expecting it to be like the tuna you cook up yourself, so this wasn't really a deal breaker for me. Bagged tuna is sort of a 50/50 type of thing as far as texture and dryness goes, although I did note that there was a substantial amount of water in the bag.

Overall this is just one of those items that is just a disappointment. It's not bad enough to where you'd feed the remaining bags of tuna to the stray cats or chuck the rest of your meal in the trash, but it isn't good enough to where you can see yourself going out of your way to buy more of it in the future. I've got a couple more of these tuna steaks in different flavors, so I'll see if those are any different. I wouldn't say that you should avoid this, but it wasn't really something I'd recommend rushing out and buying either.

I work at CVS and whenever someone buys this water bottle, it's because of how appealing the bottle actually looks rather than the water. These come in two different types of bottle, one being glass and other being plastic. This one is a plastic bottle. Don't get me wrong, the plastic bottle still looks beautiful and is very sturdy, it's not your average poland spring bottle. Aside from the looks, the taste of the water is no different than Poland Spring or Nestle bottled water. These usually sell for $1.60 at CVS so, this price online is kind of high. If you really want to try this water, or just want the bottle for the look and future use, just go to a local drugstore or grocery and pick one up. It will be much cheaper and you will get to see and feel the bottle in person to see if it's for you. Overall, it's a good buy and offers a very appealing bottle but the taste is pretty much the same as any other bottled water. I personally bought a bottle because it is so sturdy and I can refill it for everyday use, plus the appealing presentation of the bottle doesn't hurt either.
This is a great product..I am not allergic to any foods but am looking at eating healthier and found that this product is delicious..Also love the Pamela's Bread Mix and recieve that monthly.

Well worth the price. Good quality, great taste tea. Comes loose packed in a foil bag as displayed, so make sure you have a decent airtight container to store it in. This makes a really good cup of tea, hot or iced.

I received excellent service and the product arrived in a very timely fashion. These items were a Christmas present for our
son so it was important to receive them on time. Everything went very well and he really enjoyed receiving the tea.

After tasting the grapefruit marmalade, I went to great lengths to track down where it could be ordered.It is without doubt, the most fabulous marmalade ever! Not too sweet, and not too tart. I have given jars of it to friends who have asked where they could obtain it. Kudos to you for providing it!

Few peanuts are bad peanuts, and very few peanuts are as good as these peanuts. There may be better peanuts, but how much better, really. Your average person is not going to be finding out any time soon. So, let's pea nutty and throw back a few of these spicy roasters with a few frosty toasters. A peanut is a peanut, but hey, whatcha gonna do . . . eat more of these peanuts, of course.
They came well packaged. Watermelon is one of my favorite flavors and these one satisfy my taste. I recommend this product.

Out of the gold foil package, this matcha smells amazingly fruity. Super fruit amazing! The smell reminds me of really tarty berry yogurt. Brazilian Acai matcha appears slightly darker in powder form.

I prepared this as a traditional hot cup. This matcha didn't froth up very well nor maintain froth, but I didn't really care. Interestingly, this matcha prepared has a different colour than normal, a dark forest green, probably from the acai berry powder. By the way, I used 1/4tsp plus 175f water.

The taste of Brazilian Acai matcha is quite fruity. A mix between a tropical flavor and blackberries or blueberries. There is also a natural sweetness to this matcha - adding any additional sweetener would be overkill I think. With the fruityness and natural sweetness, this hot cup is very refreshing! There is no tart or bitterness to this matcha! I honestly was expected this to taste tart or puckery.

In the end, this was surprisingly good! Is this matcha totally cheating with getting a double whammy of healthy energizing matcha and acai? Don't pass up this matcha - I'm thinking this is up there on being one of my fav flavors from Red Leaf Teas!

cook in the boil water for 3 mins,rinse it thoroughly. It will not have any stinky smell.Perfect product for my diet.

review_title: great - but way too salty -increase volume by 50% to make it edible
review_text: Have eaten both Minestrone & Vegetable Beef - liked both very much but have just finished making & eating Vegetable beef. This would be a wonderful soup but it is way, way too salty. I had to increase the volume by 50%: 4 more cups water, 1/2 bag frozen mixed vegetables, 1/4 bag frozen peas - xtra salt free vegie broth mix (homemade), xtra Mrs Dash. When I finally got done, the soup was great. Don't know if I would buy again unless the salt is cut in half (at least). I am sure the Ministrone must have suffered from the same problem - I don't remember - I do remember my son & I liked it very much - I have been unable to find it at my grocery again, though. My grocery is WinnCo. Please lower the salt content - People can always add salt but I have to really change the soup to make it paletable for me.

review_title: China not ready for prime time
review_text:
Someday, it will be safe to buy food products from China. Today is not that day. Everyone knows China is very poorly regulated and the Chinese people suffer from all kinds of side effects as a result. Why should that problem and uncertainty be exported? The only way to manufacture in China is for the outsourcing company to STRICTLY and CONTINUOUSLY audit the manufacturing process -- because we cannot rely on their native legal and regulatory controls to ensure a safe product. Trio is not Apple Computer. They can't afford to scan the manufacturing all the time.

I had a Trio bar yesterday - first time - and so I wanted to buy more. But I'll have to think twice about this product until they bring manufacturing back to the USA or at least to a country with a recognized high manufacturing standard. Health food + uncertain origin DO NOT equal a viable product. Sorry Trio!

These are by far my favorite pretzels I've ever had. The taste is mild, which is what I like in a pretzel (that's not chocolate covered), and they're just the right ratio of thickness and size, not like some that are so thick and dry that they stick in your throat, or so thin that you're chewing super-salty air. Plus they're actually a bit healthier than most others. Unfortunately they're also more expensive, but I'm happy to say I always get what I pay for with these.

I was a little disappointed with this particular offering from the usually wonderful Starbucks line. When I first opened the package, the smell was wonderful, but not the vanilla I was expecting - more like some other spice.

Then when I brewed this coffee, same thing...not the vanilla I was expceting. I know Starbucks is known for its French Roast coffee, but I think the darkness of that roast overpowers the vanilla. It would have been better in a medium roast blend.

Not a bad coffee, just not what I was hoping for.

My pomeranian is 11 and will eat any treats. Heck, she eats more produce than most people do. So we bought the duck jerky and peanut butter bits. She won't touch either one. Now, she has never been given duck food so I'm not shocked that she wouldn't put it in her mouth, but she eats peanut butter like crazy. So, I looked at the ingredients and both packages contain tumeric, rosemary, and sage. I think that the seasoning may be what turn many dogs off. My dog won't eat seasoned meat. I'm not positive that this is the problem, or everyone's problem, but I don't see any other ingredients that would turn off dogs. Our pet supply store gave us a full refund and we chose another treat that she goes crazy over.

review_title: Good chipotle flavor for your tacos
review_text:
Taco Bell's Bold & Creamy Chipotle Sauce is the third of the Taco Bell's Bold & Creamy line we have tried at home, and it is our favorite. Both my wife and I liked this one the best out of the three. The other two were the Jalapeno sauce and the spicy ranchero sauce. We liked all three, but we liked this one a bit more.

We actually make tacos at home quite often, and that is when we used this sauce. However, it could be good on other things too. It has a good flavor, and if you are like me and order chipotle items off of menus when you are out, you most likely would like this sauce at home as I do.

There are 110 calories per 2 Tbsp. 100 of those calories come from the 11g of fat. (Soybean oil is the main ingredient So this is not a diet condiment, but how many are? No different than most mayo you get. If you want less fat, you should use salsa type toppings on your tacos.

If you like chipotle flavor, this is a good sauce for your tacos and other foods. I like it and will get it again.

review_title: BEST fo all the FLAVORS! **** 46c per packet****
review_text:
Like most of us I bought these for a snack. Great for emergency snack to at 160 calories for 2 bars(2bars in one envelope).

These bars taste great as most of the reviews agree.

The APPLE CRISP is my favorite flavor!

GREAT VALUE: 6 Boxes for $16.5 with subscribe and save. Each box has 6 packets with 2 bars. So, 6 boxes of 6 packets per box = 36 packets = 46 cents per pack of 2 bars. Delivered to your door!

Basic Nutrition (1 packet of 2 bars): Total fat 6 of 42 grams. Zero cholesterol. 11 of 42grams are sugar. 140mg sodium. Protein 3 of 42grams. Dietary fiber 2 of 42grams. Protein 4 of 42grams. These won't hurt you and they do not replace a good well balanced meal.

Crunchy: These can be messy to eat. When you take a bar out of the packets, crumbs fall out. I solved this by pulling both bars out of the packet over a sink or trash can. Shake loose all the crumbs then put them back in the packet. Problem solved.

For those that think these are too crunchy. Try: break off a piece and leave it in your mouth less than one minute then chew. I do this and it works great!

I also have the chocolate and they are very good, but this is my favorite

Enjoy, I do.

review_title: Very good if you love wasabi flavor/bite and seaweed snacks in general
review_text: Only get these if you like the taste of wasabi. Although it's a bit more subtle then the wasabi that's dabbed on your sushi, you still definitely get the wasabi bite (they seem to be along the same line of the extra spicy wasabi covered peas, maybe a little bit saltier). With subscribe and save, these packs come out to $1/pack which is quite a bit cheaper than what you spend outside Amazon (Whole Foods, Trader Joe's or supermarket) for this type of snack. You do get quite a few sheets in each pack (20) and each one is seasoned with the perfect amount of spicy/hot wasabi and salt. They are also nice and crunchy since they are packaged separately, and they do not seem as greasy as some other brands I've tried from the Asian markets. Although they are not greasy, one package (which contains two 10-sheet servings) has a total of 4 grams of fat (which is a little above my 3 gram target for low fat foods and unfortunately I cannot eat only half a pack. They seem to lose their crunchiness once they're opened...that's my excuse for eating the entire pack and I'm sticking to it).

Planters Peanut Butter Cookie Crisps 100 Calorie Packs, 6-Count Boxes (Pack of 6). These cookies are so tasty! I love the peanut taste and the crispness. A one-hundred calorie pack is just enough to get started having fun eating them, but then the limitation of the size of the package helps me put the brakes on. Probably the most important part of weight control is portion control.

The cookies have 3 grams fat, 0 mg Cholesterol, and 2 Weight Watchers points.

review_title: A decent cup of green tea, even better iced
review_text:
While I wouldn't describe myself as a tea connoisseur, I am a regular tea drinker. I like both green and black teas. I buy the Kirkland Signature Green Tea Matcha blend especially for brewing iced tea: 5-7 tea bags in cold water left overnight in the refrigerator, remove tea bags, and serve with or without ice. I find it needs no sweetener or lemon. Tea bags left longer than 24 hours will make the tea bitter and the matcha sediment can also become bitter.

There are other teas I like better, such as a Gen Mai Cha or a high quality Jasmine tea. For the price, the Kirkland brand is just fine and reliably available at Costco.

Very disappointing. I can't believe he has his name on this. Got the cherry tomato version, and was heavy on garlic, but mostly water. Would not coat pasta.
Very disappointing!!!

I have such a love/hate relationship with licorice, and I had to try this one, I just had to. Licorice in tea is one of my favorite things of all time, and the Licorice Mint tea from Red Leaf Tea is a must for me when I'm sick. And while I love allsorts, any other licorice candy is not for me. I just think it's vile. Every Easter my mother would gleefully sit by as I sorted out all of the black jelly beans from my basket. She was the only one in the family who liked them. Is there anything more gross than popping what you think is a purple jellybean into your mouth, only to discover it was a horrible, horrible, licorice one? Blegh! But allsorts....mmm. They've become a bit of tradition in our family. They're impossible to find in Tennessee, so when I'm sending a package to my folks I always include Coffee Crisp for my mom and allsorts for my dad. That is, if I can keep myself from eating them all before the package goes out! Trust me, it happens more often than I care to admit.

I kept eyeing this tea, adding it to my shopping cart and then removing it in favor of another flavor of matcha, adding it back to my wishlist. I finally bit the bullet and I'm here to find out if this is an allsort or a black jellybean.

It's not a black jellybean at all! It is a lovely sweet licorice that soothes the throat and makes me unbelievably happy. I was very scared of this blend, but those fears were unfounded. It is delicious and definitely one I need to keep around for when I'm sick but I have to deal with work anyway. Completely lovely. It's not really an allsort either, it's the licorice I've come to know and love in teas. I should have expected that, but I was scared by the picture on the website.

Licorice tends to polarize people. You either love it or you hate it. I'm one of those who loves it, but my wife is one who hates it. If it's in a tea, she can taste it, and it bothers her. But for me...oh, I love it and this matcha is beautiful in the balance of flavor to matcha. I want to make a large mug of this, but I have other matchas to taste and review!

I'm usually not a fan of...um..."adulterated spirits" where the producer has mucked with the natural goodness of the distilled essence by adding spices and other flavorings. But Captain Morgan Black Spiced Rum is an exception in my bar. I wish it was a bit lower proof as the alcohol fumes are a bit distracting (fixed by adding a few cubes of ice), but the vanilla and cassia flavors are well balanced. And, I really like the subtle charred oak undertones similar to a low-end but high-quality bourbon. I'm drinking it straight on ice with a bit of lime, but I expect it will stand up well to mixing with Trader Joe's apple cider as the weather cools a bit more this fall.

review_title: Tastes like a mixture of Taco Bell's taco sauce and bottled ranch dressing
review_text:
There isn't anything too objectionable in this, according to the ingredients list at Taco Bell's official site. It's got eggs, wheat, and soy in it (so, if you're allergic, avoid this), along with vinegar, sugar, molasses, and both saturated and unsaturated fat, propylene glycol, and some preservatives. One of them, Calcium Disodium EDTA, has caused some environmental concern based on what it breaks down into, but it's fairly common in food products, and if you're eating Taco Bell, I am guessing you're not all that worried about it.

If you are a fan of creamy salad dressings, you might like this on something like a chicken taco or a southwestern salad. I'm not a fan, and I did not like the taste of this.
This is sort of like a salty black licorice. It is not for everyone. In fact, most people who try it, don't seem to like it (at least those in the U.S.). They are great by themselves or with a beer.

BEST Belgian waffles I've ever had! So easy, health and delicious. Love the ingredients list. Contains TLC....I compared this mix to another I'd made a few days earlier. Couldn't believe the difference. The other mix by a company I won't name was so tough you had to tear a piece off by force. Heartland Foods was delectable!! Just perfect. Just add water!
I have been sprouting Alfalfa seeds for years. I thought I would try this mix and was pleasantly surprised. The radish gives it just enough of a bite to be interesting, but not overwhelming.

I am buying the same for much less than I paid to Amazon, usually I got such a good deals and I fall here. I thought I am paying much less BUT did not pay attention that I am paying more than 6 time that I needed to pay. AND I cannot return it. review_title: Interesting addition to the basic product
review_text: For those of us who need added fiber in our diets this is a painless and expedient way to get it.

When I saw the picture I was excited. I was going to give this as a 30th birthday present but I decided to try if for myself. It was a huge let down. I don't remember wink soda and it tasted like a generic mountain dew. The candy could have been from any decade. BTW where were the gummy savers. It was mostly generic stuff. Not worth the price at all. Shipping was too much. If they would take out that nasty generic soda the shipping could be cut in half.

I tend to have good luck when it comes to vending machines, and always come across one that has something stuck in a rung, free for the taking once I shake it a few times. Recently I spotted a pack of these almonds in one, shook it out, and gave it a shot. I hadn't tried them before since they were in the 'healthy' vending machine where the cheapest thing is a fiber cookie for nearly one Abe Lincoln. But hey, when almonds are free, I won't turn them down. Almonds are already great on their own, and these suckers add other great ingredients to the mix, making for an excellent snack.

You get the usual crunchy almonds, apples, flax seeds(!), balsamic vinegar, sea salt and red peppers. Flax seeds make everything better, in more ways than one, and I'm really glad that snack foods are starting to feature these more. The apple taste is faint but you definitely get it as soon as the almonds hit your tongue, and the red peppers...something that I've actually never had in my life, aren't strong at all. That was my only real concern with this snack when I was opening it. Despite my heritage, I've never had any kind of spicy foods, but I bit the bullet and I absolutely love these.

As usual with great snacks, the only bad thing is the price, but Amazon has a pretty good deal on them. While 'soledad' might mean 'loneliness' in Spanish, you'll be able to overcome that feeling as long as you've got a pack of these babies around. Buy some immediately...or shake 'em out of a vending machine.

My little cap is not coming off for poring the honey through the little spout so I have to unscrew the entire cap but no worries I am enjoying the product. Maybe the next bottle will not give me this problem.

I should have known when the box said "cheesy skillets" and had Velveeta as an ingredient that this wasn't probably for me. It did take the 20 minutes to prepare the Chicken and Broccoli Kraft Velveeta Chicken and Broccoli Skillets Dinner Kit, 14.64-Ounce (Pack of 6) meal. I had precooked the chicken, so I am not including the chicken cooking time as part of the 20 minutes.

The instructions are clear enough - cook the chicken, cook the pasta, add the broccoli and the cheese. I may have misunderstood the directions because I didn't put the broccoli in until after the pasta was cooked. The water cooked off very quickly, in about 5 minutes for me. I did have the skillet on simmer, so I don't know if this is my problem or the instructions problems. I had to add about 3/4 of a cup more of water to finish cooking the pasta and the broccoli. The broccoli florets were about the size of parsley flakes, so if you are not fond of broccoli, this is a plus for you. I happen to like broccoli. And than you pour the small packet of Velveeta on top of the food. For me, it was too much Velveeta - it could have been decreased by 1/3 and I think it would make a better tasting meal - especially with the addition of more broccoli.

For me, I just wasn't that impressed with it. I would have like more broccoli and less cheese. However, if my picky sons were still at home, I think they would have loved it. Not enough broccoli for them to whine about eating and of course, the Velveeta cheese they love.
I would recommend this to somebody who really, really liked Velveeta. It might a good kid's dinner for some folks. But for me, thanks but no thanks.

It is an overall nice soup, I wouldn't say an amazing soup, but if you like noodle soups definitely give this one a shot.

review_title: Flavor is good, but Eww, texture!
review_text:
This review is for the texture snobs in the world. As others have mentioned in their reviews, the flavor is great and the ingredients/nutrition are better. Hold on, though: there's a "but" coming.

Remember the flavored instant oatmeal we ate before we knew what "organic" or "gluten" meant? Brightly colored "banana" bits held onto their fake yellow color and their cubic shape long after the hot water mushified the oats for us. We ate fake crap, and we liked it.

We're older and wiser now, and want real bananas: real bananas that we re-hydrate ourselves with our steaming hot filtered water for optimum health and enjoyment.

Except that rehydrated banana bits don't stay in bit form. Nor do they rehydrate to sliced banana consistency. Nay! Rehydrated banana bits taste like delicious, wholesome bananas, and have the consistency of runny snot.

That's right: runny snot.

I love everything else about this oatmeal, but when I want to feel snot run down my throat, I'll go out and contract the common cold, thank you very much.

Based on other reviews, this doesn't throw everyone. Based on other reviews, you might love this oatmeal and shrug off the banana snot texture without a second thought. But if you're a huge texture snob, I thought you might want to know what you're getting into.

I will shudder and finish the rest of my banana oatmeal one snot-filled bowl at a time (unless I can't take it anymore, at which point the food bank gets a wholesome and delicious donation), but I won't purchase this again. I think I'll slice bananas into gluten-free oatmeal the next time I feel a bananas-and-cream craving.

Best of luck with your oatmeal experience, and thank you for pausing to read a different perspective.

Why would anyone bother paying before they have to by getting a lager size before the smaller size run out, when you're paying the same price anyway? Just get it when you need it, or you might find something even cheaper by the time the 16oz runs out, then you can really save money.

My wife has been on a GF diet for a few years now, and recently our son was diagnosed as well. She found and ordered this product to try out and we all loved the taste right away. But then they opened up a Trader Joe's right down the street from us this past year and we discovered a near-identical cereal product for half the price!!! Same flavor, same ingredients, same GF assurance...but only $2.99 a bag.

They even started carrying this product in our local Kroger grocery stores recently, but it's still $6.99 per bag!!! So no S&H, but you're still paying crazy $$$ for cereal.

No contest for us...TJ all the way.

this was the first time i've used this flour,and it's the best bread flour i have used.I will buy this item again,

review_title: Great Buy and the Price is Right!
review_text:
we've been devout Folgers coffee drinkers for over 35 years and never thought we'd switch brands. after my husband had a bout of heart problems, he was told to go to decaf coffee or stop drinking it, which wasn't an option. we started buying the Folgers half caff and he cut back to 2 cups a day. when i bought the keurig coffee maker Keurig B70 Platinum Brewing System i switched to the Green Mountain Breakfast Blend Green Mountain Coffee Breakfast Blend, 24-Count K-Cup for Keurig Brewers (love it) and bought the ekobrew refillable k-cup Ekobrew Cup,Refillable K-Cup For Keurig K-Cup Brewers 2-Pack - BROWN $ GREEN for him and his Folgers half-caff.

on a whim, i decided to buy a small box of the Green Mountain half-caff k cups for him to try. i was very nervous about it because he's such a die-hard Folgers kind of guy. since i don't care for decaff coffee i didn't want to have to drink it if he didn't like it. he took a couple big drinks of the coffee and i finally asked him how he liked it. he said he liked it a lot, and thought it was much better than the Folgers half-caff, it seemed to have a lot more flavor. phew!

in fairness to Folgers half-caff ground coffee, the reason he may think the Green Mountain half-caff has more flavor could be because of how much of the Folgers he used to make a cup. with these refillable k-cups it doesn't seem to be an exact science, so one cup could be a bit stronger or weaker than the next. i think this is what he was saying. because the Green Mountain k-cups are accurately pre-measured, you get the same taste in every cup.

the bottom line is, he loves the Green Mountain half-caff coffee, and we will definitely be buying more of it. i asked him if he wanted to try the Folgers half-caff keurig coffee and he said, "ummm, i don't know". for a hard-core coffee drinker to be ok with that kind of change says a lot about the GM half-caff coffee. as least it speaks to me of how great this coffee is.

As far as choc. goes this really good. But someone who reviewed should have explained it is so close to baking choc. you can't hardly tell the difference. Since I bought this I have experminted, and between 60 and 70 % is good for a dark chocalate lover.

review_title: A salon quality heat protectant at a drug store price!
review_text:
I absolutely love this stuff! I've always wanted to try a heat protectant spray over a cream, which has always weighed my hair down and left it feeling greasy. On the recommendation of countless reviews online, I thought I'd give this one a shot (especially since there was a 3 for $10 Tresemme promotion going on at my local drug store). This product performed better than I could have ever possibly imagined!

I spray this onto my towel-dried hair before I blow dry it. I only apply it from the middle of the hair downwards and a little bit extra on the ends for added protection. My favorite part is that when the hair is dried, you'd never know that any product was used at all. It's so lightweight and soft! It doesn't leave behind any crunchiness or sticky residue. And my hair absolutely looks so much healthier than it used to.

This sprays really evenly and covers well. The bottle lasts a long time since a little product goes a long way. And for less than $4 a bottle, this spray really is a steal. I've been continually shocked by the high quality of Tresemme products at the reasonable prices they're sold for.

There's no point in spending more for an equivalent product from a salon brand when this gem works just as well for less than a quarter the price!

With 5 dogs of various breeds, ages, and sizes, it's difficult to find a quality food that meets everydoggy's tastes. With NRG Maxim Buffalo, we have found it! Plates of this food are quickly licked clean!

We've tried other dehydrated/raws. HK smells very herby, and no one seemed to like it. Lucy's is apparently tasty, but its appearance will tell you it's mostly dried mashed potato. The ingredients in NRG are recognizably buffalo and veggies. There's a pleasant, fresh aroma upon adding water.

For maximum convenience, I immediately portion out the box of food into smaller plastic containers. I suppose they could stay out, but I stack most of the containers in my freezer. I add COLD water to a container and put it in the fridge overnight. Next morning, it's reconstituted and ready to serve after warming up.

Pricy? Maybe. But keep in mind that 10 pounds of dehydrated turns into much more after adding water. And the quality of this food is worth the price. I strongly believe that eating should be one of the pleasures of a dog's life. What made us think that it's okay to relegate a dog to a lifetime of eating only dry kibble? Give your canine buddy a plate of this...you'll see how much he enjoys it, and you won't want to go back to kibble!
These bars are the best, you feel like you have eaten a meal not a snack. no chalky or powder taste. AGAIN THE BEST!!!!

review_title: Is it a cookie or a bar? It tastes great!
review_text:
