review_title: Looked good to me, but not to my cats, apparently
review_text:
I bought the Whiskas Choice Cuts Seafood Variety Pack for my cats because they were starting to eat wet food (I had had some success with some canned foods), and it looked like good-quality, succulent food that I hoped my cats would enjoy. However, my cats had other ideas. They liked licking the juice (one nice thing about this product: the food is nice and juicy), but they left the meat to sit, ignored. I tried a couple different flavors, same result. I also found it difficult to get the food out of the pouches; cans are much easier to work with.

So, the unused pouches went back to the store for a full refund, which was nice. But too bad; I had hoped this would be a good food for my kitties. But I can't force-feed 'em, so...

review_title: taste great - satisfy the "chips" urge for low carb
review_text: I snack on these when I'm craving chips. These are the only things that really quiet down that craving. Maybe it is all the sodium/salt - I don't know. But I've bought so many of the atkins snacks, and although many are good, this (a non atkins product) is the only low-carb food I have that I reach for when I really feel like cheating.

I got these markers to occasionally decorate something in my boys' lunches. So far I have only done hard boiled eggs. They were thrilled when they opened their lunches! The tip is fairly firm, but writes very easily.

Here is day one of use, if the benefits are proven I will definately continue to purchase! It doesnt have much of a taste, isnt bitter like other brands of premixed acai drink. like others have said previously, yes it does not mix very well...BUT think of vitamin shots, they tend to not mix well either but millions make that purchase every day. I mixed with water and a tad of low calorie sweetener, to myself I found it tastes just like an instant tea. So that is how I will drink it to improve water intake as well. The price of this product is very sufficient. Affordable, and better tasting overall when mixed with plain water or as a shot (like a wheatgrass shot).

I use this spice by itself since this is not a hot spice but it is a mixture of some ten different spices popular in India. It is great with anything with brown rice but I do add some kind of pepper to make it hot. Bravo.

First bite was kind of a "hmmm" but then they really grew on me after a couple more. They have a nice chocolate flavor, although it is not overpowering. The sunflower seed flavor gives it a nice, nutty kick. Very crunchy, which I personally like.

At 10 calories a "nugget", they are a good little crunch without a lot of calories. If you can keep from eating an entire bag. Or two. :)

Picky teenage daughter says, "they're not bad. Pretty good". Husband says they are more like a cracker, but he likes them. I'm hoping they grow on daughter more as they'd be a great healthy addition to her lunches.

I don't like BBQ sauce that much, but my husband loves it. Our diet doesn't allow us to have the caloric stuff anymore so I thought I'd try this one out. It does have a strong hickory smoked flavor and smell to me, but my husband said it was quite good and wants me to order it by the case. If he had written the review it would have gotten 5 stars.
good deal on hard to find rye flour. product was well packaged to ship.would buy this item again. highly recommend.

Received a Keurig coffee maker for Christmas. I found Starbucks Caffe Verona K-Cup for Keurig Brewed (54 Count) available from Amazon. I recommend this brew to anyone who likes a darker more intense flavor. Ordering from Amazon makes my life so easy especially being a Prime member. Now one click of the mouse and I have 54 cups of delicious coffee on the way to my door.
searching for replacement to sad-eyed sally's bbq &blues. on my third bottle and so far so good,

This flavor Godiva coffee is my absolute favorite over all other blends and brands. It is a light vanilla flavor and great for after dinner or breakfast. I will never buy Starbucks or any other brand as Godiva vanilla has won me over. It also makes a lovely gift for coffee drinkers; the packaging is pretty. This incidentally makes a week's -12 days worth of coffee or 25 pots of 12 cups each....it makes allot. 5 stars.

Iwas delighted when I received my carousel for 35 K-cups. I can now display all my favorite K-cups. It looks neat on the counter. I choose chrome. I didn't know the carousel would turn showing all my k-cups. Before getting the carousel I kept my K-cups in a plastic container or a box. I recommend the carousel and give it a five star rate. I purchased the carousel from Amazon. I buy all my K-cups through Amazon.

Carolyn

review_title: LOVE THIS GREAT BIG BOX OF TEA!
review_text: We have been dedicated to this brand and type of tea for some years. We found several online sources. This is the best. If, for some reason, you have not tried "Yorkshire Gold," do yourself a favor and do so.

Wasn't sure what to expect, but the flavor is amazing! A little goes a long way, well worth the cost. I love the flavor of salt & the truffle aroma is intoxicating. Sprinkle a pinch over eggs, roast, veggies... it's liberating. This salt brings out the flavor of foods. My favorite is a few pinches over fried eggs, toast with grape jelly (jelly from concord grapes in my back yard) & a slice of cheese. Comfort food from my childhood. My big fear is that I drop the little jar when it's open!
this is the best tea. brew it in a porcelain teapot and use a small glass for drinking. enjoy it...

I am a bit addicted now. I really enjoy the overall flavour.
It is not too overpowering in artificial flavour, and it is not too overly fruity. There is not too much crunch, but it isn't like a "normal" Newton fig bar.
The blend of the flavours is great and the blend makes it a nice, satisfying taste that isn't too sweet, but gives it enough flavour to be enjoyable satisfying.

I thought this toy looked a bit odd and boring. I wasn't sure my dog would like it. But, she does. Toys do not last around here. My dog chews up most toys I try to give her right away, but I gave her this about a month ago and she still hasn't completely chewed it up, but she has bit off a few very tiny pieces. When I purchased this I also got two other toys and they are lasting much better. I recommend the Nylabone Double Action Combo Bone Chew, Wolf, and the Nylabone big chew knot for big dogs.

review_title: Tasty and Delicious Just Wished Did Not Have Extra "Sugar" Hidden As Carbs
review_text: I am a big fan of granola, but unfortunately not a big fan of fat, which is traditionally what one gets with granola. For those concerned about taste, I would say this is a great product and if rating on taste alone I would give this "Back to Nature" product 5 Stars. However, once again we have a product that "looks" seemingly healthy and fun, but is loaded with added carbohydrates, aka "sugar" and that is what brings the score on this product down. You think companies would work with today;s busy professionals and re-establish trust, by offering products that are truly truly healthy and not just slipping them in under the FDA or surgeon general guide lines of what is deemed good health or nutrition. I am very health conscious so always reading labels for ingredients. Furthermore I have to keep my sugar intake in check during this "look better naked" summer months. Great treat, but for those looking to shed the extra pounds of fat or get leaner during the summer save this for the winter months or during your bulking phase.

review_title: Tiger's blood... heavy on the blood!
review_text: This is about the best tasting Tiger's Blood I have ever had. I thought it might be weak, but I was soooo wrong! I have learned to put a lot less on the ice. This gallon should last years!

I am a little confused as to how to describe these things. I had high hopes since I have heard good things about Mochi. Well I must say they really have little to no flavor. I expected a good strawberry flavor but you can barely detect it. They look good in the picture but they have hardly any "strawberry" filling and you are left chewing on this tasteless sphere. Now texture wise , so weird. Kind of like a marshmallow but more rubbery??? I took a chance and tried something new. Will not be purchasing any more Mochi in the future. Tho I may not really eat the rest of them, they are kinda fun to squish in your hands when bored.

If you're ordering these, you already know how delicious they are. I just can't give them 5 stars knowing each little chocolate has 6g of fat and 4g of sat fats ...

I would only buy this 50cnt bag if you have incredible will power, otherwise you may find yourself in an emergency room.

Nonetheless, exactly as advertised. Bravo!

review_title: Gluten Free Macro Bars the perfect quick GF snack
review_text: These delicious Gluten-free snack bars taste like slightly sweetened nut butter and are chewy and satisfying. Can even replace a meal in a pinch (especially breakfast). Taste like a treat but are sound nutrition and free of common allergens.

Yes, this stuff is expensive -- but I indulge in the splurge when the taste is so heavenly. I love almond butter in general, but I have never tasted anything quite like this. Maybe it's the maple? I don't know...all I know is when I bring it to work and bust out the jar and crackers I'm the most popular girl around. Thanks, Justin!
Love these! Found them at costco awhile back. These pouches used to be larger, but there's nothing else like these that I can find. Tasty and not too unhealthy.

I've always made homemade pizza which took me hours from start to finish. Enter GalloLea Organic Pizza Kit. Now, my family can enjoy delicious, wholesome pizza in no time at all. The crust is easy to make, healthy and tastes great whether made thick or thin. An added bonus is the parchment paper included in the kit. Keeps the crust from sticking to the pan without having to use oil or shortening. The sauce has just the right blend of spices and I top it with a variety of cheeses and vegetables. A favorite in our house is mozzarella and parmesan cheeses with green peppers and onions. I also like ricotta cheese with a little pesto, sundried tomatoes and pine nuts. The combinations are endless.

review_title: Glucomannan  (tree fiber)  is NOT good for you
review_text:
0 stars and even that is too many.
If you are bound and determined to eat these things (any flavor) then please take the time to read and study Glucomannan, where it comes from and the possible side effects of consuming it.
Frankly, I cannot believe that it is being sold as a food product to be consumed in a fairly large quantity.

Should you eat these noodles do plan on staying close to home as you may find that the Glucomannan has quite an effect on your digestive system.

Personally, I'd rather eat a tree.

Plus they smell just awful - when you open the package you get a waft of dead fish.
Great company, when item came in stock they let me know, I ordered it and it came quickly. Definately a company to buy from!

review_title: Tasty and Nutritious, now that's a a big win!
review_text: I love that it's organic and all natural. These are so tasty, we ran through the 18 pack in a day or two. Great for anyone that is on a busy shcedule, but still wants to eat healthy snacks.
This 200 pack Welch's Jelly Assortment was ordered for a church food pantry. The product arrived quickly and in good condition. This is a great value and the church was happy.
For a fast and easy side dish, this can work but my broccoli loving son didn't like this at all. He said the texture was "weird". I'll stick with regular mac & cheese

This soup mix is the easiest meal I make. It qualifies as a hearty meal in my book, and it can be on the table and into a tummy that is headed out for soccer or football. I discovered this soup mix from Bob's Red Mill last year and I have ordered it in bulk for my deep freeze. It is like a bean mix except that it cooks much faster because all the pieces are tiny, but it still tastes like a real homemade soup. There are even some weensy little whole wheat alphabet pasta pieces in there. So cute! You can dress it up any way you like. There are several variations on the package, but it's also great just with broth. Add some whole wheat bread and dinner is served.

Here's a link to exactly how I cook a bag of it: [...]

review_title: not as good as the original Boomi Bar
review_text: This reformulated bar is less tasty and harder to eat than the original Boomi Bar. It's chewier and more oily, which I find to be a worse texture. I hope they bring back the original bar because it was an integral between-meal snack for my gluten-free diet. The change is bad enough that I may drop this bar entirely and eat more Larabars and KIND bars.

The Switch Black Cherry is one of the most refreshing drinks that I have ever tried. It is 100% pure cherry juice and the difference from the regular sodas is quite obvious. Right from the first sip it feels richer and tastier than any soda product out there. After drinking it, you may never go back to regular sodas again. It works well by itself or with a meal. This may be an ideal substitute for sodas for kids, as it is a much more nutritious drink. The only problem that I have is the size of the can. At 8.3 ounces it may be enough for a kid, but you would need more than one of these to quench an adult's thirst.

I have to agree with another reviewer that this is not the best of the Kona coffees that I have relished in the past. That includes those that I have had the pleasure of drinking in Hawaii several times. Frankly, I found it to have a bitter aftertaste rather than the smooth finish that is typical of this type of superior coffee. I can't knock the seller for his promptness, though I was surprised the coffee arrived in a bloated looking USPS priority envelope.

Now granted I haven't been to Hawaii for a number of years so I am not sure if the criteria for Kona coffee quality has slipped to a large degree. If this is any indicator then I suspect that I will not be in the market for Kona in the future as it is not worth to me the premium price. I've been an avid coffee drinker for over 40 years, and tasted coffee from virtually every global region. Unfortunately, this coffee was a disappointment, and I will not be ordering it again.
This Taco Bell Chipotle sauce is pretty good, and makes a nice addition for a taco. It wasn't very spicy, so I would have liked it better if it had a little heat, but the flavor was nice.

I ordered this as a gift for my husbands birthday. While i saw the picture of this item i didnt actually expect to receive what i did. The package itself weighs over 15 pounds and the array of pasta was faboulis. My husband it a big fan of pasta but honestly we almost never actually buy it so this was nice gift and much bigger than i thought it would actually be. Honestly it was worth the money for him.

review_title: You can't tell it is GF
review_text:
The Gluten Free Penne from Schar is by far the best gluten free pasta out there. You can't even tell the difference from normal pasta, it tastes so delicious.

I am excited about this product, because now I can eat my favorite dishes again and do not have to feel left out. Once you tried them you will know why this brand has become the #1 gluten-free brand in Europe. Their quality is really great and I look forward to trying out more of their products.

Also, they aren't as pricey as other gluten free products that I have seen around. Especially, not here on Amazon - good product and good price!

review_title: Pass on This for Davidson's organic chamomile
review_text: I love tea and herbal tea. I have ordered Davidson's 1-pound organic chamomile tea from Amazon numerous times now. This is the first time I have order from Tao of Tea and only did so because Davidson's did not have it available on here at this time. I will not order Tao of Tea again. I received my product and it seemed stale and was nothing but shake. When you get Chamomile tea it should be flowers not shake. Every time I have ordered Davidson's chamomile it has been wonderful. I would suggest you pass on this brand and get Davidson's if it is available. I believe it is even a little bit cheaper too from Davidson's which is an added bonus. I am not impressed with Tao of Tea.

The taste for these is okay to me, but they are really, really dry and crumbly. They are so dry, parts of them always get stuck in the back of my mouth when I eat them. If you are like me and prefer brownies a little mushy and moist, you will NOT like these. Sure, they have natural ingredients but they are not much healthier than other BETTER brownies on the market. If you are gonna eat a brownie, eat a brownie for God's sake! They just aren't meant to be anywhere close to health food.
My Dog loves them but the only thing that bothers me is they only last about 10 to 15 min.And they are a little pricey.
Only order during the winter months. they arive melted together when you order them during the summer. When they are in good condition they are very good.

Pringles Light Fat Free Potato Crisps, SourCream & Onion, 5.82-Ounce Tubes (Pack of 14)

The wife brought these home for some reason. I don't think this is a good use for recycled paper. I have my doubts about the cardboard can tasting much different. I bet ya I can eat just one! I wish I could give it no stars.

I purchased the small churpi chews (2 in a pack) for my chihuahua puppy and my mom's adult standard dachshund. The small churpi lasted my chihuahua for 3 months of regular chewing, and my mom reported her dachshund finished his in 3 days. My chihuahua was between 2 and 3 pounds over the course of the first chew and the dachshund was around 20 lbs. After the success of the first chew, I purchased the medium sized chew for my chihuahua (who is now full sized at 4 lbs) because it is longer and she can hold onto it easier with her paws. It's been a week now, and she's just barely scratched the surface of the chew. She loves it and carries it around with her all the time!
I think that these are long lasting if you have a toy breed or a dog that isn't a heavy chewer.

I am very pleased with this mixer....even though the jar of pnut butter I had was a different size from the lid of the mixer....was still able to use it and do the job quickly and without the usual mess.
It's great to get a product that does what it says it will!
excellent product, fast delivery. i use these to make vegan chocolate and this is the best price for the product.

A good, strong French Roast with a smooth mouthfeel and no bitter finish. Definitely planning to try San Francisco Bay's other products. I love how much less plastic is wasted. (My Keurig is my best friend, but there's so much waste in most disposable K-Cups!)
All fruit, nothing added. It's sad that's so hard to find. They have a great taste so it's a win all around.

review_title: Beware of the good reviews this is a very salty rub
review_text:
I bought this product due to all the good reviews. I bbq using a weber smokey mountain often. I usually make my own rubs. I thought this might be a good product to keep on hand in case I didn't feel like making a rub.

Most good bbq rubs have a base of salt and brown sugar. This rub does not contain any sugar. It is however very heavy on the salt. I am used to using a large amount of rub when bbq to get a nice bark. I made some ribs using this rub and they were so salty they were almost inedible. I tasted the rub before hand and cut it with some brown sugar but clearly not enough.

I have used this rub on other foods and had better results. A little is very delicious on french fries or corn on the cob where I use this in place of salt.

If you intend on using this rub heavily for low and slow cooking plan on cutting it heavily with brown sugar. I prefer a salty spicy rub over an overly sweet rub but this is too much on its own. I just wish they used more of the other ingrediantes and less salt as the spice is very tasty.

If you plan on using this to spice up other foods in place of salt you may be very happy with this.

Marinade a pork tenderlion, grill, then use the meat to make Cuban Sandwiches. YUM.
You can search the internet for the basic Cuban Sandwich recipe, but definitely use Mojo Criollo for the pork marinade. I've tried other homemade recipes for the marinade - this tastes better, is easier and economical.
One of our favorites.

You can't go wrong with Bigelows Tea Chai Green Tea. It's amazing! Whether you brew a small or large cup of it (I prefer unsweetened), the chai spices gives plain green tea (not my favorite tea) an almost sweet, full-bodied, spicy flavor. Love it, especially in the evening.

review_title: sweet, creamy, buttery flavor. Excellent product.
review_text:
This is the second brand of ghee that I have tried and I like this one far and away above the other one (Nanak Pure Desi Ghee). This one doesn't separate and has a smooth creamy consistency, whileas the other one is more gritty and the flavor is not nice just straight out of the jar. Purity Farm has me eating it straight out of the jar and it smells just heavenly.

I am very pleased with this product and would definitely buy it again. I especially like that it is casein-free as I have a dairy intolerance so even the small amounts of milk protein in regular butter might be an issue, but with this ghee I don't have to worry about it.

*update* It is now a few months since I started ordering this and I have started ordering two jars at a time to make sure that I'm never without. I have confirmed that regular butter DOES cause me issues but ghee is perfectly fine due to being casein-free. I read that ghee is also an ayurvedic remedy for many things and is supposed to be healing for leaky-gut syndrome.

I also have to say that it makes a FANTASTIC moisturizer for the face! I have ultra ultra sensitive skin that just cannot handle the slightest amount of chemicals on it. I thought to myself one day to try ghee as a facial moisturizer as I desperately needed it. I put it on, thinking that I might get red from it or break out as I have from every other moisturizer attempt and... no reaction! Best of all, my dry, scaly patch on my face is now soft and smooth after only two applications! I've had that scaly patch for months! I am so excited about this find since most of the time I cannot do any topicals at all, but my skin just loved the ghee and drank it up. A little goes a long way, so if you do this, a pea-sized amount is plenty for a large area. Rub in gently but if your skin is dry it will soak up quickly leaving you feeling soft but not oily if you put on just a little bit at a time. You can always put more as needed, so don't overdo at the beginning.
sweet leaf products..I love this all natural good for you product..great product..good doing business with seller..will buy again in the future..thanks
I use this on a regular basis, for example, thickening and flavoring stews, adding oomph to sauteed chicken breast, thickening vegetable soup.

Plain chocolate digestives are the exemplar of the British chocolate biscuit; McVitie were the inventors (in 1839 if you care about such historical facts) of this lovely Scottish (not English, please note!) confection, so these offered here can be regarded as definitive of their class. Whilst available in a milk chocolate variety, the plain (dark) chocolate version is by far to be preferred. It is a solid, satisfying mix of wholewheat cookie and bitter chocolate coating of the finest quality. Purists will, naturally, wish to stick to the plain wholemeal digestive, both those biscuit- or chocolate-lovers wishing to spoil themselves (and their waistline) will opt for these every time.

American buyers may wish to remember that Digestive biscuits are officially classed in biscuit grading circles as the second most boring biscuit in the world (after Rich Tea and ahead of Hobnobs) so may take a little bit of getting used to, if you're not familiar with British biscuits. But I guarantee that after your second, you'll be addicted. And then you'll wonder where the rest of the pack went.

review_title: A Tasty Treat With Fiber and a Good Shelf Life.
review_text:
We usually have a case of Orville Redenbacher Microwave Popcorn in the pantry for snacks. We prefer such things as popcorn and nuts much better than candy, and I think they are much better for you. It, after all, is just corn with a few flavors and we try to keep the flavoring to a minimus. Plus, it has a lot of fiber. The popping is perfect every time. We just set our microwave on "popcorn" and it comes out just fine with no further adjustment. We bought this 40-pack box of Orville Redenbacher's Smart Pop at our local Costco warehouse store. We expect it will provide good snacks, have a good shelf life and last a long time.

review_title: A healthy cereal that tastes good? I didn't know that was possible!
review_text:
Rarely am I an advocate for eating healthy foods. You'll often find me devouring a whole pizza by myself or inhaling several pounds of meat at the local Famous Daves. And when it comes cereal, the whole "healthy" section just seems like wasted space to me. I'd sooner eat 2 or 3 boxes of Lucky Charms in a week over a box of Total. All this was true until I was introduced to a fine creation known as Honey Bunches of Oats w/ Strawberries.

Now don't get me wrong. Honey Bunches of Oats w/ Strawberries hasn't changed my opinons on healthy cereals. In fact, original Honey Bunches of Oats is nothing more than some corn flakes and oat clusters that taste completely unoriginal. And unoriginallity really doesn't taste so great. Enter strawberries. Those old corn flakes and oat clusters remain here but added in are strawberries. Now, you're probably thinking the same as I did when I first saw the box. "Uhh, probably just some crappy strawberry flavoring smeared on the flakes..." This is not the case. Included in this cereal are REAL dried strawberry slices. Whoever thought of putting them inside the cereal should be given medal. They take the cereal from space-wasting-unoriginal-tasting trash to something that actually tastes GOOD... and it's healthy! The only downside to this cereal is that it's not as resilient when soaked in milk as other cereals. It will get soggy if you don't eat fast enough, but that's never really a problem for me.

If you are a diehard fan of tooth rotting, chest pain inducing cereals, such as myself, you could definitely try this out. I did and I'm not ashamed to say that Honey Bunches of Oats w/ Strawberries has become part of my regular cereal lineup.

review_title: What are the ingredients in Taco Bell Bold & Creamy Spicy Ranchero Sauce?
review_text:
Ranchero sauce is traditionally tomato-based, similar to the salsa brought to the table with tortilla chips in "Mexican" restaurants.

I compared the ingredients of this sauce to a jar of commercial mayonnaise. The primary difference is the substitution of whey for some of the egg plus the addition of flavorings, colorings, preservatives, and chemicals to adjust the texture and thickness.

The resulting mildly spicy sauce has the appearance and consistency of Thousand Island dressing with tiny specks in it but with a very unpleasant chemical smell and taste.

You can easily make a much better-tasting and less expensive substitute for this sauce by adding a tablespoon of salsa or ketchup, a teaspoon of chili powder, and a half-teaspoon each of garlic powder and sugar to a cup of mayonnaise, with vinegar-based hot sauce to taste.

Ingredients:

Soybean Oil, Water, Whey (from Milk), Vinegar, Buttermilk*, Salt, Egg Yolks, Sugar.

Contains less than 2% of Spice, Tomato*, Modified Food Starch, Garlic*, Garlic Juice, Onions*, Xanthan Gum, Citric Acid, Yeast Extract, Phosphoric Acid, Gum Arabic, Corn Syrup, Disodium Inosinate and Disodium Guanylate, Natural Flavor, Artificial Color, with Sorbic Acid and Calcium Disodium EDTA as preservatives.

*Dried. Contains: Milk, Egg.

Nutrition:

Serving size: 2 Tablespoons 29g
Servings - 8

Amount per serving:
Calories - 110
Total fat - 11g
Saturated fat - 1.5g
Trans fat - 0g
Cholesterol - 10mg
Sodium - 310mg
Total Carbohydrates - 3g
Fiber - 0g
Sugars - 1g
Protein - <1g
Vitamin A - 2% DV
Vitamin C - 0% DV
Calcium - 0% DV
Iron 0% - DV

*** Note that although the bottle looks exactly like a regular Kraft salad dressing bottle, it is only 8 ounces instead of the usual 16 ounces.

review_title: As water goes, it's good. As a cure, it's hard to tell...
review_text:
It's hard to review the efficacy of a product like Essentia's 9.5 pH Drinking Water after consuming just 1.5L (the sample size provided to Vine reviewers), so let's just say that as a water product goes, there's nothing either extraordinary or offensive about this H2O. The bottler went to a lot of work to fine tune the chemistry and flavor of this product (micro-filtration, UV lighting, reverse osmosis, mineral "enhancement" and something called ionic separation), but you're going to be disappointed if you pour a glass and expect it to taste like, umm, robust good health. Essentia's 9.5 pH Drinking Water tastes like...water, albeit with that trademark flat metallic taste that comes from altering the ingredient list a little.

Is it worth the price? For somebody enduring the daily torment of GERD, I'd say it's definitely worth a try. Does it work? You'll have to rely on other reviewers to make that call.

review_title: Limited usefulness and strong fishy odor
review_text:
I bought the huge quantity (4 bags) required and it is more that a lifetime supply. The reason I bought this is I had read in a book that it is a perfect salt substitute and has lots of potassium and magnesium, which are helpful as a natural remedy for high blood pressure. What I learned is that a "serving" of dulse (what you need to have to get the minerals promised) is something like two teaspoons. Believe me, when you see and smell this stuff you won't be eating two teaspoons in ten sittings, let alone as one serving. So far, I have put it in clam chowder because it's already fishy tasting anyway.

If you are interested in getting more potassium and magnesium, I suggest Naked Juice or Bolthouse Farms Banana-strawberry juice. It has a ton of potassium and tastes pretty good. Also, you can just eat lots of fresh fruits and veggies and accomplish the same thing.

I like "Vernor's", and drink it all the time. It is old fashioned drink from Michigan, since 1866, and the labeling was known for its lovely green and gold colors and the gnome character. If you look at the can it is actually called ginger soda instead of ale and is different than typical ginger ale in taste. Vernor's has a strong vanilla presence, is smoother and more moist, and sweeter than a ginger ale. It is closer to a cream soda than ginger ale, very good. A ginger ale is more dry, higher in carbonation, and has a spicier bite to it. Vernor's has 39 grams sugar in the full size can, versus 33 grams per can for Schweppes Ginger Ale. Neither has caffeine.

The recipe has changed a bit over the years mostly in the choice of sweeteners. They use the same high fructose corn syrup that the cost-cutting mega soda companies use, rather than the cane sugar they used to use. The down side to that is that the corn syrup syrup drowns out some of the flavors. Many microbrews are using cane sugar again, although it costs more, to get a better taste. Vernor's is still aged in oak barrels for 3 years, no doubt accounting for the smoothness. All in all a great soda for anytime. The soda is also known, like other ginger drinks, for its use as a home remedy for an upset stomach.
Vernor's is a regional soda, and there are many areas of the country you can't find it in. That is why it sells here. They may be selling it in glass bottles again soon, at least there is a page that says so the day I am writing this comment. Search at Amazon for ASIN: B001JK2SEG to see what I mean. Vernor's has quite a history, and there are two books at Amazon about the soda. The first is Vernor's Ginger Ale (Images of America: Michigan) ISBN-10: 0738551856. The second book is "The Vernor's Story: From Gnomes to Now" ISBN-10: 0472066978 a book about the company's marketing green and gold packaging with the famous gnome, and Vernor's fans.

review_title: T O O......C H U N K Y......A N D.....C R U N C H Y
review_text:
I had not realized until just now that these FERRERO ROCHER HAZELNUT CHOCOLATES 48 COUNT GIFT BOX, were made by the SAME Ferrero Company that makes "Nutella" Hazelnut-Chocolate spread, (which I totally love to eat!) I had some of these individual chocolates once, and did NOT like them! Those who share my taste in chocolates will not like them either! For these Ferraro Rochet Hazelenut
Choclolates, have bits of hazel nuts, (finely chopped...but NOT finely chopped enough for me), embedded in a chocolate coating, the whole surrounding a whole hazelnut. I'm one of those people who likes FILETS....boneless fish filets, boneless chicken filets, etc. I, (and I know I'm NOT alone here), like my chocolate mixtures to be complete MIXTURES....like Nutella spread, (or "figaro" hazelnut chocolate pieces...also called "chocolate truffles"). Biting through
hard, embedded "pieces" of things -- even such tiny pieces of hazelnut as the chocolate-hazelnut coating has here -- is NOT for me, or others like me. In this, I have discovered, I, (or make that, "we"), have true "preppy" tastes, (even though I did attend plain old Queens College in NYC -- part of the very public
City University of New York where, it seems, (to me, at least), egalitarian has gone from good to exremes -- and even fraternities and sororities are officially outlawed! To some, this may seem only "fair" and "equitable". But to status-seeking me, it seems SO unglamourous! I guess I'm not the typical Queens College of the City University of New York student, though I do sincerely laud them for making eduction more available for everyone. I only wish they had more of what private universities have. To me, "equality" is equal luxuries for all, not equal utilitarianism for all. Same goal, different methods, I guess....) In "The Official Preppy Handbook", it states that preppies like filets...no bones to slice through, or fat to trim away. (Why waste the time, why spend the effort?) The same has to go, (at least for me --and others with "preppy" tastes), for these otherwise "gourmet" quality confections. Those little bits of chopped hazelnuts in the coating -- not to mention the BIG hazelnut in the centre -- make eating DIFFICULT, and spoil the pleasure of eating. Rather than have a (fancy) chocolate that I must chew through, I would prefer a plain old Hershey choclate bar. It, like the more expensive Nutella, IS a chocolate "filet". I suppose this liking of the "ordinary" Hershey bar, (and a plain one please -- not one with almonds), proves I am NOT a snob -- I, (and others like me), simply have TASTE!

I thought I was alone in this. But since reading "The Official Preppy Handbook", I know that others -- far richer than myself -- also have these tastes. This, of course, vindicates me, and I feel glad of it.
Enjoy "Ferrero Rocher Hazelnut Chocolates 48 Count Gift Box" if you wish...and give it to friends, if that is your desire. But this sort of chocolate, because of the way it is formed, is NOT my choice for either myself or my friends. And, no matter how excellent the chocolate itself is, it will NEVER be of "gourmet" quality, to me!

review_text:
By improvising I mean that I have no coffeemaker other than my humble gold mesh basket that fits into a cup. So I put the pod into the bottom of the basket, added a little water to pre-prime it -- and then added hot water. With a soup spoon I imitated the process that a coffeemaker that uses pods would do: I pressed the spoon into the pod several times, and VOILA!! A WONDERFUL brew emerged in the cup. I enjoyed a cuppa with NO residual stomach acid and no bitterness -- I didn't even need sugar. Later, I added some soy milk and the taste was just as enjoyable.

I liked reading the dedication on the box. This coffee was grown with care, and lovingly dedicated to an incredible individual, Bob Marley.

Even though I will continue to have to improvise when brewing this coffee, I look forward to enjoying every single pod.

I have a big yard with sandy-clay soil,pretty soft ground in sc.the moles used to be a big problem.spent aproximely a month buying traps,seeing how this moles destroyed my yard with no results.I will save you time:trapline and cinch traps are good products but don't work.the moles feel the trap inside the tunnel,it's a no go.loop traps work somehow,but after many trips I realized that they are way too wide for the mole.I ended up buying this one.I own and used 2 trapline,2 cinchtrap,2 choke mole nash trap and only one victor out of sight.so far I caught 2 moles and guess what,both with the victor out of sight.the last one I caught I set the 2 trapline first in the beginning of the run,the cinch after that,the choke,and then the victor and then the chocke.went to check 2 hours later.found the tunnel deviating both the traplines and the cinch,tripped the chocke one...I knew that after the fence the victor had to do the trick...and I was right.my second one with the same trap!.I own all kinds of traps but only this one had work twice and fast.the secret?I believe is the fact that is not as wide as other traps,the trigger is very sensitive,the blades are curvy and push the animal inwards when closed...very fast and strongly...any trap that goes inside the tunnel is doomed to fail in my yard.the moles feel it and avoid it alltogether.the choke is fine idea,just too wide and won't catch the thing that probably stays in between the choke while it closes...this is my story.don't spend 80 dollars like me.buy one of this,find the run that goes straight around your fence or house,set it on top of the tunnel,don't dig nothing,just spade a slot where the blades will sit,press the tongue-trigger in the middle and enjoy the sweet revenge.I just wanted to share my experience.I have 7 mole traps that don't work.save yourself some money.

Worse, the ingredients can only be seen if you peel off the back label. And what do we find as the first ingredient? Petrolatum, a product otherwise known as petroleum jelly. This malicious product has been found in breast cancer tumors. Applying directly to the skin inhibits the skin's ability to breathe. (Do an internet search of Petrolatum and you'll quickly find the same research results.) Oof, bad. Once it's in your hair, plan on it staying there through 6 or 8 shampoos (unless, as another reviewer has suggested, you use a detergent on your hair that can unhinge the petroleum molecules). This product is not recommended for humans or animals.

review_title: Good dog food, but a bit pricey
review_text:
If they're picky eaters dogs may care about the size, shape, texture, smell, and/or taste of a food; or, if they're like my dog, they care that it stays still long enough that they can gobble it down. Owners should care about calorie content, that the food is intended for the proper age group (puppy, adult, or senior), and that the food is certified to meet the nutritional requirements set by the Association of American Feed Control Officials (AAFCO). And, unless you are independently wealthy, price.

Because my dog is not a picky eater, I can't comment on the taste of Chef Michael's. However, it is much higher in calories than a basic dog food such as Pedigree Adult Complete Nutrition Small Crunchy Bites Original Beef Dry Dog Food, 30-Pound, which means that a much smaller amount should be fed, something that she definitely objects to.

There is one notable point about Chef Michael's-
All pet foods produced in the United States must meet certain minimal nutritional requirements, which are set by AAFCO. Somewhere on its bag or can each food will have a short statement saying that is has either been *formulated* to meet AAFCO standards, or has undergone *feeding trials* and been shown to meet AAFCO standards. AAFCO has set standards for 2 life stages; growth (i.e. puppies) and reproduction, and adult. (Senior is not recognized as a separate stage.) Because feeding trials are so expensive, such foods are usually formulated to meet the needs of *all life stages*. Growth and gestation/lactation have much higher nutritional requirements than a normal adult dog has. Therefore, such a food may not be ideal, particularly if you pet has a weight problem. Foods that are *formulated* (i.e. do not undergo feeding tests), are more often designed to meet the needs of a specific life stage. Chef Michael's is the only pet food (dog or cat), that I have ever found that has undergone feeding trials AND is designed for a specific life stage.

The importance of feeding trials, formulations, and life stages is debatable, and in the end is an individual decision.

Finally, despite what some people claim, it is not possible to assess the quality of a food from the ingredient list. Ingredient descriptions, for example any listing of a meat product or by-product, are vague and there can be huge variation in the quality of a product with the same name from one food to another. In the same vein, claims that certain ingredients shouldn't be in dog food should be taken with a grain (or large jar) of salt. When chosing a food for your pet, talk to your vet, don't believe a stranger on the internet with unknown qualifications.

As for me, although my dog enjoyed Chef Michael's, both the calorie content and price are too high to make it her normal dog food.

review_title: THIS IS TRULY A GOURMET HOT SAUCE!
review_text:
Peri-Peri means "pepper pepper" in Swahili, and the pepper referred to is the African Bird's Eye Pepper which was carried back by Portuguese traders in the 14th century to Angola and Mozambique, and has finally ended up on worldwide tables, lucky for us!

The hot sauce is indeed hot. In that aspect I would compare it to our own Tobasco Sauce. It is not intolerably hot. I find it to my taste. It is particularly a delicious additive to shrimp and chicken.

Alas to say, I do not have the courage to sample "Hot Hot Peri-Peri Sauce."

It is sun-ripend, and the exotic mix of herbs and spices is what makes it pass with flying colors. Really a treat.

Be sure to keep it refrigerated after opening. One warning: check the shipping fees. They are steep. But since the seller's website has a $20.00 minimum on orders, unless you want to buy more than one item, Amazon seems the best way.

review_title: Easy to use, seems to work for a while
review_text: The particular critters we want to get rid of are chipmunks - they're destroying our garden, tunneling into the lawn and even invading the house. The Havahart Critter Ridder is easy to use - just pop the cap and shake onto the ground. I tried it around a peach tree the chipmunks had been fond of, and it did seem to reduce the number of attacks. I didn't expect miracles, given the number of chipmunks we have around, but it did seem to help for a while. As with all products of this nature, it needs to be periodically reapplied.

I didn't have high expectations for this but it worked so I'll share what I did:

I stamped down ALL of the tunnels in the yard the evening before. The next morning I looked for new tunnel, especially long straight ones because apparently those are the ones most travelled. I then placed the trap on said tunnel and I had a dead mole within two hours. Like a previous reviewer, I did not follow the manufacturers instructions and instead followed his which I reccomend doing because it's not only easier but also works.
Our dog, a young boxer mix, loves these treats! She will sit like an angel before getting one of these treats.

In my case, the six hour energy did make me feel peppy but it also made my heart race so I decided not to try it again.

Don't let this put you off, it does seem to work though in my case it also had some side effects.

He has been on diet food for 6 years and I could not get him to lose weight (he also has pica and anxiety related to food, so food limiting worked minimally). This is the first time I can remember buying Dry Non-Fat Milk Powder but I thought I'd give it a try considering that a gallon of milk at the grocery store is nearing 4.00. So after reading the reviews on this one I figured I'd try it. It mixed well in the blender, I added 1.5 cups of water to 0.5 cup of milk powder, blended it for a little, and then added 2 more cups of water to blend. It came out fine, but at the bottom was lumps of foam. Not really a con but I guess it all foams up down there where you add the powder. I will try mixing it different ways to see if I can get rid of the foam or something, but it's not a huge deal. It smells like that fresh milk you get from the farm, it doesn't smell like the over processed store milk you buy. I really don't mind it because I am sure it will just taste fine with my cereal and other things. For the price of this milk, you get a good deal. I made a quart and it doesn't even look like I put a dent in the one 26-ounce package. This stuff can last about 2 years on the shelf. If this continues to work out, I will never buy expensive milk at the store, this stuff is probably better for me anyways. So all in all, I gave it a 4 out of 5, because of the foam and because I thought it would taste exactly like the store milk that other people said, but it is a little different. I am pretty picky when it comes to taste and everything. I think non-fat and skim milk are two totally different kinds of milk. I used to think they are the same but I could be wrong. Skim milk is my favorite.

EDIT: The foam has gone done once refrigerated as I expected so that is good. Finally took a taste of the milk and it's not at all what I expected. I guess I just don't like the taste of Fresh Milk but if YOU do then this is the stuff to get. I guess I can use it for baking or something else or maybe even as cereal milk. We will see.

EDIT(2): I had the quart of milk in the fridge for a little over 4 hours now. I added ice cubes to it, about a tablespoon of vanilla extract, and just a couple pours of regular skim milk. The taste is sooooo much better and tastes like a Vanilla milkshake. I am so happy with this taste and I suggest you do the same thing if you want it to taste good. I really think just adding the ice and let it get really cold helped the taste the most though, and the Vanilla was just icing on the cake. I don't think I needed to add the couple pours of regular milk but I thought I'd give it a try because of what some websites said. But overall I am now happy I bought this stuff because it's gonna save me so much money than buying a Gallon of milk for 3.75 a gallon and have 1/4 of that be spoiled, etc. Now I can just make milk whenever I need it and not worry about it. I suggest you do the steps I have as well. Don't judge it by how it tastes right after you mix it because if you did, you'd give it a 1 star rating like I was going to.

review_title: white rice IS brown rice...just naked...
review_text:
To the two-star review that has decided to eschew this product because the ingredients are "white rice with rice bran" instead of brown rice, that's a difference without distinction. Brown rice is unpolished white rice. For manufacturing/cooking or some other reason, the manufacturer decided to use polished brown rice, i.e. white rice and then add back the bran. I see no downside.

To go further, they're crackers, not your main source for anything. If you eat rice as a staple or because you can't have wheat, then by all means serve brown rice, but like I said, they are a snack. Ok, beat that to death... now on to the review.

I didn't realize that Amazon sold these, so I've been buying them in the supermarket. Just ordered a 12-pack. I like that they have no after-taste, have lots of crunch, will stand up to dip (or salsa, although they are a little small for that) and that they are relatively low in calories compared to other crackers like Ritz, Triscuits, etc.

There is only one negative. I have a very difficult time eating just a couple. I have to put the package back in the fridge, less they all disappear while I am doing the bills, watching tv, etc.

...and my dogs love them, too...

Our son picks a theme and the experts at Greenfire come up with awesome looking fortunes to give to his friends. I have also used the cookies as gifts for colleagues as well as clients and they think they not only look cool but they are yummy too. I love the fact that we get to be creative and different at a reasonable price. Perhaps even better than the actual cookies -- the staff at Greenfire. Always accommodating and easy to work with. I have been buying the Flavor Snacks variety since she was only a few months old. The size is perfect for her and they fit wonderfully into her toys. When she was a puppy I broke them into small pieces, but now that she's an adult I either stuff a whole one in her toys or give one to her as a treat. She has a Kong Goodie Bone and I break these Milk-Bones in half and stuff them in the holes (her bone is the large size I think - I wasn't sure how big she would get when I bought it). This 8 pound box is very convenient as I don't have to worry about running out for months. I put about half in a large, air-tight container and left the rest in the box and taped it shut since they wouldn't all fit in my container. I will certainly be buying the big box again when I eventually run out.

Gary Peterson

review_title: Warning: will cause bad breathe (but oh, so delicious)
review_text:
These chips are very very good. The flavoring on them is the perfect bbq flavor. Unfortunately they leave you with terrible breath (maybe don't eat them before going in for a kiss or talking in someone's face!).

They have an odd crisp that's unsettling at first, but if you give it a try, you get used to it, and will most likely enjoy it. You eat them expecting that crunch of a potato chip, but are greeted by something more airy. It's a pretty unique texture that's not much like what I've eaten before.

They are not baked or fried, but popped. Which means less calories and fat. They also seem to have the ability to fill me up even though the bag is small (smaller than most I think). So that is a double win.

I'd highly recommend trying these in store before investing in a big box of these unless you are adventurous (or generous enough to give away if they displease you)...but DO try them!

Our dogs look forward to these treats EVERY evening. If we forget (or try to forget) to give them one - they let us know it! They aren't messy, don't smell, don't stain, don't get soggy and we don't have to worry about them getting struck in their throats like rawhides. Highly recommend.

review_title: Great Way To Carry Splenda - Highly Recommended
review_text:
Splenda Minis are a very convenient way to carry Splenda with you when traveling.

I mostly use Splenda since the other non-sugar sweeteners have never tasted "right" to me, but not every restaurant or person I'm visiting serves it. I had started to carry the little packets (Splenda No Calorie Sweetener, Granular, Individual Packets, 700-Count Box) with me, but they get grungy if left in the pocket too long and do take up a good bit of space.

The 200 Splenda Minis in the hard plastic dispenser are equal to 100 packets, so these will last a good long time. Note: Be sure to give the tablets time to dissolve and stir well.

Highly Recommended!

CFH

Blanxart has been my favorite ever since I first bought it, pretty much on a whim, at a cheese shop in Vermont a couple of years ago. I was really excited to find I could buy it on Amazon, but, living in Texas, I'm limited on when I can buy it, as it just can't survive the heat. But, about the chocolate, the flavor and texture are great! I like that old-world, kind of gritty texture, so different from the smooth, overly sugary, mass-produced "chocolates" people buy every day. You must try Blanxart!
I love French truffles, and these are very good. The chocolate is so smooth and rich!
We really enjoyed this olive oil and will be buying again. As a matter of fact, we are just running out. We gave some away as gifts for the holidays and kept some for ourselves.

My only complaint about this product is that sometimes it is difficult to squeeze just a very small amount from the bottle into the mixture to be colored. I don't do massive amounts of baking, and I find that sometimes it is really hard to get a very light color when adding this product to very small quantities of mixture. However, the colors are very bright and I was very pleased to find that the color is evenly distributed throughout the mixture. I thought this product was a little pricy, but after trying it out, I do believe that it was worth the extra money. The product seems of high quality and it shows in my cooking whenever I want to add some color to things.

We drink this all the time, finially we dont have to lug it in from the store
Amazon delivers it to our door step.
At about a 1.00 a bottle.. this is not a bad price for the best mineral water in the world !

review_title: The One, The Only, Original Raisin Bran
review_text: Skinner's Raisin Bran is the only brand we like, because it does not get too soggy in the bowl. We like the crunch and the roasted flavor. Sometimes, my husband will have a bowl for dessert, as it is sweet, but not cloying. It is hard (impossible?) to find in stores these days, so we were pleased to find that we can get a regular (no pun intended!) supply on the "Subscribe and Save" program at Amazon, for a reasonable price.
This item came from the shipper quick and as described. my dog LOVES greenies- and they really do seem to keep his teeth and breath clean.
For some unknown reasons this product is not available on most local pet store, I am so happy that Amazon has this item available. Thank you.

review_title: Great deal on best GF pretzels I've found!
review_text: I fell in love with Glutino stick pretzels when I started a gluten-free diet six months ago. I actually think they taste better than the regular pretzels I used to eat! The only downside was the price -- the local store charged $8.99 per 14 ounce bag. When I checked on Amazon, I was pleased to find they carry Glutino pretzels. Interestingly, the 8 ounce bags turn out to be a better price per ounce than the 14 ounce ones, so that's what I ordered. With the subscribe and save deal, the 8-ounce bags came out at just over $3 apiece. If you or your gluten-free child loves pretzels for a snack, and you have the space to store a dozen bags, this is an awesome deal! I have a handful of friends who also are gluten-free, so I've shared some of my order with them. These are amazingly good pretzels and Amazon's subscribe and save price is the best you're going to find anywhere!!

review_title: Good durable product stors milk well
review_text: This product does what is supposed to do and fits inside the pump so you can pump and store easily. Sometimes the lids can be hard to open but besides that it is a good product. My son (1 year old) also likes to play with them so it can be a toy once you are done with them.
My cat will meow at me (more like a howl), demanding that I give him a pinch of his favorite snack food. I give him just a pinch at a time. One ounce will last me about a month.

Making peach chutney on the spur of the moment, I was out of my usual "plain old" cheapie vinegar. I substituted this Marukan rice wine vinegar and was delighted with the results. My chutney was deliciously mellow, while still tart enough to compliment the curries it accompanied. Henceforth I'll keep a bottle of this in the pantry!

I love sea salt and I love almonds. This snack is so good, I had to keep reminding myself that this is a healthy snack. Unbelievable.

Almonds have a lot of health benefits. One benefit, in addition to all the others, is heart-health.

This snack is perfect for anytime you want a snack, but don't need anything unhealthy.

review_title: Good drink for your exercise routine
review_text: I read about H2O Overdrive in a Runners World magazine ad and amazon was one of the only place I could find it. This is a fantastic energy drink that would rival the other brands out there. The only "dislike" I have is that it is a little more expensive for an energy drink, but it has tons of vitamins and less than 1 gram of sugar which is a plus if you are trying to cut down on sugar intake.

Maybe you won't find this in the kitchen's of Giadia and Emeril... but that's only because they haven't tried it yet. Truly one of my favorite seasonings. It's great on popcorn, baked potatoes, cheesy oven potatoes, toasted bread, fish, chicken, the list goes on... My friends tease me about it. My grocery store used to carry it, but now I buy it on Amazon. Buy some and see what you think.

The product was shipped in a manilla envelope with a bit of bubble wrap. The lid was broken off the screw top and the lip of the safe was dented. I glued the top back with super-glue and straightened it back up. (I didn't feel like waiting for a replacement.) Additionally, the can was not as pictured. It was an Arizona health drink which sort of sticks out in my pantry with canned goods. I kept it anyway. It looks like and feels like a real can so overall, I think this is a great consealed safe.
My only suggestion is for the company to use a small box to ship it. If you order one, you might request it. I would buy this product again.

review_title: Get rid of the crystal light - this is so much better!
review_text: This is a unique powdered product. It is unsweetened and turns water a brilliant green. The taste is of green tea, and not bitter. If you like matcha flavored things, you will enjoy this. It's very nice in the summer months when you don't want a hot drink. It's also a good value since you get 10 packets in the box. 0 calories and so much healthier and tastier than crystal light!

review_title: Great for kids with food allergies!
review_text: I have been using this product since my son was diagnosed with a peanut allergy. This size is so convinient ! He is a very picky eater and this is what he asks for every morning on toast. I love it too! Would recommend it for sure!

review_title: Love to use it as an air freshener
review_text: I discovered this tea at a Pilot Truck Stop when I was on a trip recently. I fell in love with it. I even used the package that the diffuser came in to freshen my car!

In addition to the sweetness from the honey, there is a detectable molasses flavor which I really like. I believe I would have a hard time making a better sauce from scratch.

Quick & easy -delivery-eased the shipping & handling charges a BIT!Wanted to get Longevity for my adult-senior cats BUT was unable.My cats including the kitten-3& a half months really like their Blue Buffalo.Some might say it*s overpriced though

These are wonderful. We have been very happy with this product even my teenage daughter likes them. I am very unhappy that they are no longer available.
Some of the best Espresso Pods on the market. Makes fixing a cup of espresso a snap Rich full body taste. Great product.

So I had bought a 2 pack of these same treats from pet co while I bought their cage back when they were a month or two. I totally forgot I stashed the other bag of these in my cabinet.. Soo cute. I forgot how much they had loved these back then! They beg and start doing tricks for me when I ask if they want a treat. or if I open the door to the cabinet. lol

review_title: why is pork so hard to find?
review_text: Pork ramen is a family favorite but for some reason it is very hard to find in my location. So happy to find out on Amazon at not a bad price! Arrived in good condition...will buy again.

I discovered this hot sauce while traveling around in Belize, it's on most tables in a lot of restaurants. It has a lovely tang from the grapefruit, and is not tongue burning off hot, but a pleasing gentle heat. I am never without!

Moreover, the beans remained fresh & retained their flavor after I opened the bag. This particular dark bean makes a very fine and elegant cup of coffee.

This is a great product. I buy my Potato Starch from Amazon each time I need it (subscribe and save, in fact) and it's always fresh. So many wonderful things come out of my kitchen, and I have Amazon to thank for many of the ingredients.

review_title: Tried it anyway, despite blood pressure warning
review_text: Although I am on a blood pressure med, I went ahead and tried this anyway. I doubt I was able to choke down enough to affect my med, because the taste was terrible. And I'm a fan of pomegranates, so that's not the issue. EBOOST tasted bitter and medicinal - there are much better, more palatable, ways to boost your immune system.

This stuff is potent! Seriously, I could dot my tongue on the surface and taste a mouthful. This caramel dip is so sweet, it's like saccharine. It's so saccharine, a diabetic would pass on it. And it really doesn't taste too much like caramel. I mean, vaguely, it does, but that's if you haven't had caramel in A REALLY long time. As with most WF products, don't look to this as a replacement; look at it as an alternative. I have a feeling this jar will be in my fridge a looong time, which could be good or bad.

If your glueten intolerant - here is your answer to great bread and pie crust. After almost 4 years of my husband getting diagnosed, pies became a thing of the past for us until now! Also, the bread is fantastic - so easy to make and takes awesome. You will not be disappointed with this product. Pamela's Products Amazing Wheat Free & Gluten-free Bread Mix, 4-Pound Bags (Pack of 3)

review_title: Good Taste but Be Careful if You're Counting Calories
review_text:
My husband is a type II diabetic so we almost always eat whole grain pasta but sometimes you just gotta splurge a little and I'm always on the lookout for something I can have on hand that cooks fast.

I fixed this as per the package directions except I used some diced, baked chicken that I already had in the freezer. This is easy to make. Just brown the chicken and then add the seasoning and pasta along with 2.5 cups of water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover and simmer for 7 minutes then add the broccoli, cover and cook an additional 2-4 minutes.

If you already have the cooked chicken available you could have dinner on the table in about 15 minutes! Even if you are cooking the chicken from raw, this is still a very quick meal.

The first bite was almost too cheesy to me. I had some frozen chopped broccoli so I steamed about half a package of it and added it to the skillet. The taste improved quite a bit for me with the added benefit of more veggies.

The downside to this meal is that about a cup of it is around 400 calories, 8 grams of fat. I plugged this into the weight watchers points calculator and it's 7 points per cup if you fix it as per the directions.

This isn't something I could eat often but with the addition of extra broccoli and taking into consideration how good tasting/convenient it is, I would buy a couple of boxes to keep on hand for times when I don't have a lot of time to cook.
Love the flavor, don't like the way it stains my counters if it spills, or my teeth if I drink too much!

review_title: Can't miss a week without it!
review_text:
This is my second 4-box purchase of this product. My thoughts after my first bowl of this was, "Really? Does protein cereal HAVE to taste so dull?" But, I thought, this is an excellent source of protein, so I was hoping I could "flavor it up."

If you add some berries and two packs of Truvia or Splenda, you really can create a high-protein, low-carb cereal that tastes great. Be creative! I eat it with different types of berries (blackberries, strawberries, raspberries), usually 2-3 times a week (the rest of the week it's eggs and/or greek yogurt). It's really helped me with my cereal cravings and high-protein, low-carb diet, and now I couldn't make it a week without it.

This has been my favorite hot chocolate since I first tried it years ago. I cannot be without it and send away for it if I can't find it locally. It is better by far than any other I've ever tried and even better than much more expensive brands. I don't think you can find better hot cocoa on the planet. This is the very best!
Kerri Bennett Williamson, Author

I absolutely love these shortbread cookies. Unfortunately my store is out of them and doesn't know if they'll get them back in stock. They are plain shortbread...not flavored. I would order the butterscotch if I knew the nutrition count was the same. Could someone give me the nutrition count on the flavored ones?

The plain ones are: 76 cal, 2.5 fat, 5 carbs, less than 1 protein, 0 fiber. Serving size 3 to 4 cookies.

On weight watchers these are only 1 point a serving.

Thank You, Eileen

I love this tea. I found it by accident at a grocery store near me while i was searching for "Wild sweet orange". The minute I openned the packet I smell a rose scent similar to that found with Rose essential oil. (not the scent of rosewater, not the cloying scent of rose perfume nor even the perfumey sweetness of fresh roses, but a scent that is the true essence of roses.) The fragrance flooded me with pleasant memories. I see one reviewer didn't like it. I guess it is not for everyone. I love it. It is truly unique. I have never had a tea like this. After finishing half a box in 3 days, I have been in search of somewhere else to buy it.

The tea itself is more subtle than the aromatics that are released when the packet is openned. That is much harder to discribe . slight smell of rose with a hint of woodiness. It is more subtle than a lot of their teas. Too bad everyone can't try a sample before committing to an entire box.

Edit 12/12/10 OK maybe it is not so much the roses that give the strong scent. maybe it is the geranium oil. Whatever it is, it reminds me of a spa not a chinese herbalist.

Love this coffee! Tastes fresh and can taste the hazelnut flavor (but it's not overwhelming). Also, I'm pretty sensitive to caffeine and this is one of those decaf coffees that is truly low caffeine (some decafs have higher levels). But best of all, it tastes great.

So many crackers on the market claim to be whole grain but are in reality filled with white flour. These flatbread crackers are *genuinely* whole grain. They are really delicious and very good for you. And...they go great with hummus! Yum!

review_title: Great tasting base for soups, dips, and many other recipes
review_text:
This organic miso tastes better than any of the other kinds I've tried and it's also less expensive than what the majority of local stores have. The package is resealable and comes with more servings than you could shake a chopstick at. Despite being made from only five natural ingredients (one of them being water) this miso has a very rich flavor. It is also low in carbs and high in Vitamin B12, Acidophilus, Zinc, Folate (Vitamin B9), Phosphorus, Magnesium, Iron, Copper, Calcium, Vitamin B3, and Vitamin B2. This soup goes great with rice, sushi, noodles, and adzuki beans (or any other type) but also is great for dips, sauces, etc. Numerous studies show the health benefits, such as improved immune response*, of eating fermented foods such as this.

* [...]

Nice combination of ingredients so that it is VERY hot, yet still has great flavor. It has a little added sugar, which adds a nice bit of sweetness to the mix. For the hotter salsas, you really can't go wrong with this. This is one of my top four favorites, which include Mrs. Renfros hot salsa, Screaming Sphincter and Pain is Good #37.
This high fiber cereal pleases kids and adults alike. It does not get soggy in milk and is equally good crumbled over yogurt and fruit.

review_title: "Best Gummi Bears I Have Ever Had
review_text:

If the taste were good I could overlook the gumminess bit add them together and it's beyond unpleasant. Yuck. So glad I only bought a small pack to try first!

Am buying again, taste like eating out and you can make it quickly at home. The Pad Thai was the best but this was also excellent and the price is good because in the market they are 1/3 the weight and expensive as opposed to Amazon

I was a long term subscriber on the Amazon plan for Fancy Feast Fish and Shrimp
I just cancelled my subscription today.
Let me start out by saying Amazon is top notch with their program of subscribe and save
Which is why I placed it at two stars.
However I would like to know what the Friskies corporation DID with this product....
I think that there has been a change to the formula.
It now stinks so badly my cat who is not overly fussy and ate this flavor happily and eagerly for 3 years and now will not even touch this stuff!!
She is a healthy 3 year old cat, who loved her Fancy Feast treat.
And I noticed the change when serving it to her with the horrid almost rancid over-fishy smell and texture within the last month or so.
And yes, while I realize, that it is a fish and shrimp mix and will smell somewhat the smell seems now completely atrocious.
My guess is that the company started using a lower grade of product.

I put this review out here to see if anyone also has encountered this with this product recently also??
So I was resigned to cancelling my subscription- Again through no fault of Amazon
And I now ordered from Amazon, "Halo Spot's Stew" Crab, shrimp, and chicken flavor mix canned cat food
And I will see how that goes...
Just wanted to leave my feedback on my disappointment on what this product has seemed to become.

My dogs LOVE Greenies and get very excited at just their mention. I only use them once or twice a week, so the big pack lasts a while. Also, Amazon has the best price I've seen for these anywhere - at least the large and jumbo sizes. I was as excited to see them on Amazon as my dogs are to get them!

Amazon featured Catit refill grass kit. I have about 6 of these kits and love to keep fresh grass for my cats. I always have one or two growing in stages. My cats love it and come running when they see me walk in with the grass. It is neat and easy to grow. And fun!

I bought 4. Two are growing. The other two did not root and died. I planted all four the same way and all received the same amount of sun and water. The two that survived are growing well.

review_title: If you are not from Buffalo...
review_text: Give this great mustard a chance. Weber's is a regional favorite in Western New York for a good reason. It has a strong, hearty mustard flavor with a zing of horseradish. Its great on ham or salami or any hearty cold cuts and it shines with grilled hot dogs or Polish sausage. It is also a terrific secret ingredient in deviled eggs and potato salad. Its bright atomic yellow color may make you think of French's, but that is where the similarity ends...this condiment packs a punch!

review_title: Delicious, but you're paying for packaging
review_text: I was doing a space unit during camp this year, and thought this would be a fun addition. The kids loved it! However, we had snack afterwards and one of the students had freeze dried apples that were nearly identical only packaged as a regular old snack... his were easier to obtain and cheaper as well.
Our little guy eats one of these a day after dinner. he sits and waits until they are given. A super price for the box and fast shipping. Will definately order again.

I have two cats that both eat this food. I adopted each of them and switched them to grain-free food as soon as possible. My little one had very soft stools until he was put on a 100% grain-free diet. Both of their coats are incredibly soft, they aren't overweight and they are full of energy. It is more expensive than other brands out there, but my cats' health is worth it to me, especially if it will help with possible health issues in the future. The vet is very happy with their health and my boys are very happy with their human.

review_title: I don't have a taste for it but maybe you will

BaconSalt is exactly what you think it is. It literally makes everything taste better. Try it on anything, it even goes on popcorn! I got it as a gift and I'm going to give it as a gift. Everyone needs to try it, and since there is no fat, no calories and it is kosher, it is for everyone!

This stuff is GREAT! A little hard to open at first (dont throw away the top!) but is very tasty and very refreshing. If you want to try something new, go for this. not a bad drink - but why call it lychee juice when the ingredients are pear juice and lychee puree (in that order)?

review_title: Mr. Yoshida is a yummy man.
review_text: Here in Gainesville Florida we have a place that serves grilled meats and vegetables basted in a sweet teriyaki sauce over rice. I'm addicted to the sauce, if I could I would go out and get a giant plastic container, fill it up with it, and pour it over everything there is. I would even consider teriyaki baths. I always want to take a million "to go" containers of the sauce when I visit but that would be really rude. Since I cannot do that, I have found a substitute in Mr. Yoshida. It's not as thick, and its not exactly the same flavor, it is a delicious marinade/sauce in its own right. If you love teriyaki flavor it's definitely worth trying. I put mine in stir fry, marinate meats, and use it as a dipping sauce for dumplings. I'm sure there is a million other different uses. Mr. Yoshida always does me right!

Been buying mixed selection at Pet Smart for $1.80/10oz package.
Came to Amazon looking for a MORE REASONABLE price to buy by the case
Now I'll stay happily with Pet Smart.
Dogs love the stuff though.
The Amaretto is my favorite of all the great tasting Caffe D'Vita products. And I really do like them better than anything Starbucks has to offer, not to mention they're all 99% caffine free.

I have recently taken a liking to Chai tea thanks to a store in the mall called Teavana; Couldn't afford their prices, so the next best thing originally was some loose Chai tea from Marshalls, which was great -- but they only had one can of the stuff. That said, it's not awful, just don't expect anything phenomenal. I'll likely be switching to a different brand after I'm done w/this bag.

review_title: Excellent Choice For Organic Coffee - Fresh and Good

review_title: Natural Soda with a Great Taste
review_text:
This was one of two Switch sodas I tried. Surprisingly, this one was my favorite. I thought I would prefer the Cherry version, but the Watermelon Strawberry had a much better taste.

I like that Switch is an all-natural product with lots of vitamin C and no extra sugar added. The pleasant taste makes this drink a winner for me. If normal sodas are too bubbly for you, then you'll enjoy Switch's lower carbonation.

I will ding the drink for its price. It's a little expensive for what you get. However, if all-natural products are important to you then it's worth the price.

In my opinion, the lamb and rice strips are more digestable for the dogs. I have two smaller dogs, both have some trouble with different foods. These lamb & rice are "processed" like a hamburger jerky that breaks into pieces easily and then, in my opinion, digest more easily for the dogs. The other products, JERKY, is actual strips of duck or chicken jerky. It is very hard to tear apart and seems to have strong tendons or strips in the treats that would seem to be stringy.

All in all, I would rather give my dogs the treat that breaks easily for me and they seem to enjoy without getting sick and/or not eating the next day. Our Malti-Poo actually has noises coming from her stomach when she eats the thicker, tougher jerky treats. The lamb & rice is my choice and, if you look close at that treats when purchasing, you'll see what this looks like compared to the tougher jerky treats.

review_title: Good for my kids allergies & GRANOLA BAR RECIPE
review_text:
My kids have many food allergies sensitivities and this mix is fine for them to eat. It is a nice mix and sweet enough to feel like a treat. However, my kids (6, 5 & 3 years old) tend to pick out the fruit pieces they like and leave the rest...even though they like those other ingredients when served separately.

This trail mix now serves a new purpose for us. Recently we stopped ordering the Enjoy Life granola bars due to the brown rice syrup and the reports that some brown rice syrup contains arsenic. I have figured out how to make my own granola bars using this mix and a few other ingredients. Here is the recipe in case anyone else is interested:

3 bags Enjoy Life No Nuts Seed and Fruit mix (I bet any of the blends would work)
1/4 Cup Coconut Flour
1/4 Cup Ground Flax Seeds
1/4 Cup Cacao Nibs (these are unsweetened, and optional. I'm sure the Enjoy Life mini chocolate chips would be great in here too, or raisins...)

In case you are unfamiliar with any of the ingredients here are samples of each:
Enjoy Life not nuts! Beach Bash Nut Free Trail Mix, Gluten, Dairy & Nut Free, 6-Ounce Bags (Pack of 6)
Bob's Red Mill Organic Coconut Flour -- 16 oz
Bob's Red Mill Organic Flaxseed Meal, 16-Ounce Packages (Pack of 4)
Navitas Naturals Cacao Nibs, 16-Ounce Pouches (Pack of 2)

You could add a little honey or other sweetener, but we don't. The fruit in it makes it just sweet enough. However, due to my kids' allergies they are used to this type of food. If you are just converting off a typical American diet, you might want it sweeter. Also, this recipe seems pretty forgiving if you want to experiment. I might try some with sunflower butter. I bet it would good, but this way is tasty and pretty simple, and I'm not sure that I want to add any more $ to these since we already go through 2-3 batches a week.

Before you start, preheat the oven to 300-350. (This is important if you want to do it the busy mom way.) Put it all in the food processor and let it become very fine granules, it will take a few minutes. Add 1/2 cup extremely hot water and keep it whizzing in the food processor for just a minute or so to mix it together. Pour into a pan. I use an 11 inch by 7 inch Pyrex pan. It helps if you grease the bottom. The mixture is very sticky, so spreading it out evenly is a little tricky. I use a large metal spatula-type thing that is actually for cutting dough. The parts that stick to the spatula I take off and put in the corners of the pan. I'm able to get it nice and smooth, just expect it to be thick and sticky. Next bake it for 10-15 minutes, or put it in and turn off the oven so you don't have to remember it. That's what I do and it works great. I just let it stay in there until I'm ready to cut it into bars or squares. It is much easier to cut if you wait for it to cool completely.

So, now that I am using this to make granola bars, we are going through a lot of this. But it's healthy and free of all the allergens my kids have. I hope this helps someone.

In case anyone is wondering about ingredients. The bags I just got (with a new designed tan, orange and brown bag) list this:
Raw Sunflower Kernels, Pineapples*, Roasted Hulled Pumkin Seeds (Sunflower Oil (Sunflower Oil, Citric Acid), salt), Cranberries*, Apricots*.
*Sulfite-free
Also, they expire in September of this year...so, about 6 months from when I got them.
