review_title: good litter but now too expensive so back to Just the Crystals
review_text: I bought a few bags of this when Amazon was out of Just the Crystals cat litter. It worked really well - no urine pooling under the crystals because it is so fine. It's much less scratchy on feet and the floor too. Unfortunately I will no longer be buying this litter as the price went from below $9 to $15.49 for a 5lb bag. In comparison Just the Crystals is $17.89 for 8.8 lbs and is pretty decent for the price. If anyone knows of a store that has good prices in Northern California please leave a message in the comments. Thanks.

review_title: Great powder, not strong berry flavor
review_text:
The powder is great. I use this in conjunction with a multivitamin to get all my nutrients for the day. However, I've tried the regular Green SuperFood powder, the berry flavor is not very strong. I could almost interchange the two flavors and the taste wouldn't be very discernible. The berry flavor that you can taste is very artificial tasting.

Minus the berry taste I recommend this or any Green SuperFood to a lot of people. I prefer to use these in smoothies. However when I don't have time to put together a smoothie the powder is great with just almond milk (extra protein). I know they say you can just add it to water and you can, but the taste is much to be desired. I wouldn't recommend blending it with soy milk. I attempt this hoping to cut some fat from almond milk, but the powder with soy milk seemed to emulsify creating a very un-tasty like mousse. I'm not a culinary genius so I'm not sure why this happened. But it was gross.

review_title: Squid Ink Fettuccine with cream sauce
review_text: After seaching long & hard for Squid Ink Noodles, I found them Amazon. I was not dissappointed by this product, it has lived up to my expectations. I make them according to the package directions. I reduce heavy cream in a pan & add lemon zest & fresh chopped Rosemary. This is truely a great tasting pasta dish!
Horrible, melted slimy and tasted bad. Gave to a child and she did not even want to eat it. Do not buy. get a bag of gummies instead.

My vet recommended these capsules for my cat who has chronic urinary tract issues (probably stones). These work really well. My cat has had very few UT issues since beginning Chondro-Flex. I sprinkle it in his wet food and add a bit of water to get him to take in more fluids. He likes it, and there are no "pilling" problems. He has to take it every day for it to work.

My husband doesn't drink coffee or any other energy drinks so when he was deployed I had a hard time finding an affordable way to ship cases of red bull to him. This was the perfect solution and arrived in the same amount of time as if I'd sent it from home. It just saved me a lot of work.

review_title: The BEST Christmas candy stocking stuffer...EVER
review_text:
Ever since we discovered these several years ago, they have been a staple item on our Christmas stocking stuffer list. All of the kids (and I) absolutely LOVE these. Normally I'm more of a chocolate flavor purist, perhaps allowing peanuts (M&Ms) or almonds (Hershey bars) to be part of the mix, but until I tasted these chocolate eggs, the thought of mixing chocolate and fruit flavors grossed me out (e.g., cherry chocolates, etc.).

Anyway, the combination of chocolate and orange flavors is heavenly. I like the size, the shape, and the way they are put together. AND, of course, the flavor.

Just in case you are not familair with these already, the chocolate orange comes wrapped in a foil covering. One end is covered with a sticker. What you need to do before you open the wrapping is hold the egg in your hand and whack it down onto a solid surface on the end where the sticker is. This action allows the sections of the chocolate orange to be easily pulled apart after you remove the wrapper.

All in all, this is a fantastic treat, and I try to be a good boy all year long in the hopes that there will be another one of these in my stocking come Christmas morning!

Merry Christmas, and a 5-star treat!

I feel in this with this candy when I went on a trip to Savannah, every year when I went to visit, I would buy it by the pounds. All of a sudden,I went to purchase it and they no longer had it, needless to say I was SO disappointed. I was constantly searching the internet to find this fruit candy. When I finally found a site that did not have ridiculous shipping fees, I jumped on it. I am very happy now...Thank You

review_title: Great tray! No problems with seller.
review_text:
I got this to put under my Keurig coffee maker, and it has been wonderful in clearing up the clutter in the area. The coffee pods fit well in the tray, and it looks exactly as it does in the photo. The only problem is that the Keurig occasionally vibrates on the tray when it starts up. This is easily remedied by securing the silver part of the Keurig overflow tray (the base part you put the coffee cups on) with a little tape or just removing the overflow tray completely.

The seller was great - I got the item fast and in perfect shape.

review_title: very sweet, not enough nut flavor
review_text: i ordinarily eat plain cheerios or oatmeal for breakfast, so trying this cereal was like having a candy bar for breakfast. that being said, i think this crunch nut cereal resembles honey nut cheerios in appearance, but the flavor is more like that honey smacks cereal. i thought it was very sweet (but that might be because of what my usual breakfast is), and did not notice any nut flavor at all.

I drink green tea almost every day and I always stick with the traditional ones. I don't like other flavors in my tea. Personally, this one tastes horrible. I just can't stand the combination of lemon balm and verbena and green tea. Well, if you want to try something new, you may want to consider this product. If you're a traditional green tea consumer like me, you should avoid it.

My family prefers the blue agave to the lighter colored and sweeter agaves. We use it to make raw truffles (sooo good) and on top of pancakes and waffles, cereal, rice pudding, etc. We had no problems with shipments leaking so far.
These are just a wonderful tasty snack! Can't say much more than that. They may be an expensive snack, but I am hooked. I will continue to keep these in the house!

This is an interesting product. My advice, if you're looking for an alternative to "candy" licorice or twists, you'll want to avoid this. If you're looking for a better type of licorice, than this product might be good for you.

I'd describe this product as "old-fashioned". It seems to be made in a way in which many mass-produced licorice products aren't made anymore. Most interesting are the ingredients: organic wheat flour, organic sugar, elderberry extract, and pomegranate juice concentrate. While "organic" isn't necessarily any more healthy for you than non-organic products, the "wheat flour" is very interesting, and probably helps add the 2g of fiber to each serving.

If I had to describe the taste -- I'd say "sour". The first piece you eat will be "curious", but if you eat an entire 5-piece serving (3.5 serving in the package), the flavor actually improves. I do think the use of wheat flour makes them a bit "pasty" or "chalky". And pomegranate isn't necessarily sweet -- so expect a tart experience.

Bottom line -- the kids won't like this, but health-conscious adults may.
Organic ginger root-groud. Re-usable glass jar. Comes in set of three. Very good fresh taste.

I never thought I would like seaweed, but it rehydrates into green leaves like spinach, has a subtle ocean smell, and tastes surprisingly good. The best news is how good it is for the body. I steam it along with brown rice and either use it as a side or put it in soup. Great product.

review_title: Just what I was looking for
review_text: Too bad I can't find this in my local store, but at least I found it. This is very finely ground, but like fine cornmeal. I am diabetic, and quinoa is great for me, but grinding the quinoa myself never resulted in flour. My blood sugar is always lower the morning after I have quinoa with dinner, and this flour also makes good muffins and pancakes. It doesn't have gluten though, so they have better texture if use use at least some (unbleached, not enriched) flour. I also use it as a coating for sautéed meat, fish, or vegetables.
Love this It is so easy ...Sometimes I throw some cubed chicken in this and make a meal out of it great for a side dish.
Amazon listing under Peerless is outdated, as this is a Brazilian import, similar to Peerless. Peerless candy manufacturer has apparently gone out of business.

review_title: Each 7 ounce package makes a nice meal or two with additional ingredients
review_text: Each individual 7-ounce package of Peacock Brown Rice Vermicelli comes with preparation instructions and two simple recipes, and contains enough vermicelli to make meals for up to two people. I usually combine vermicelli with any two or three of the following: diced pork, shrimp, tofu, chicken, shiitake mushroom, and / or green vegetables. Add soy sauce and other condiments when you pan cook them, or if you prefer to make soup, just add chicken broth and bring them to boil. Either way, you only have to soak the vermicelli in water at room temperature for about a minute before you start cooking.
THIS WAS BEYOND MY EXPECTATIONS. THE VARIETY WAS A GREAT BLEND OF DIFFERENT SOUPS AND SO EASY TO FIX. EVEN MY HUSBAND LIKED THEM. I QUICKLY ORDERED ANOTHER SET, BECAUSE IT WAS SO GOOD. TASTY AND EASY

I needed 24 candy bars for a Valentine's project for my son's class. In my local stores, these candy bars run $1.19 or so. These bars are a great price and arrived way before the expected arrival date. I would use this seller again. Nicely packages as well to prevent breakage.

I love these treats for my Jack Russell for three reasons;

*Great Ingredients, no corn, wheat, or dairy. Just rice, meat, a little molasses, a little canola oil, and preserved with vitamin E. And not just alpha tocopherol...these treats have Mixed Tocopherols! If you know anything about vitamin E, you know it's a vitamin complex. The more parts of the complex that are included, the more healthy it is. The tocopherols are the things that help to preserve the product in a good natural way.

*The size of these are really small, just a little over an inch. Shaped just like milkbones, they are a really cute mini version. And they break really easy, so I don't feel bad giving my little guy several pieces each day.

*My dog loves these treats! I have to admit he's not picky, but it's still great that he loves such a healthy treat!

And finally, my dog does not have food sensitivities or allergies, but if he did, these would still be a great choice!
When these are gone I will definitely buy more!

It was costing an arm and a leg to go to Starbucks every morning, added up to over $1200 a year. I've had Mexican Spiced Cocoa in San Francisco and though it was the best ever. This cocoa, I add to my favorite coffee and add whipped cream in my travel cup and it's the best. Still love Starbucks! Keeps me warm, even at 30 below.

Quite frankly, you'll be doing just as well with a five cents-per-bag cup of Salada, Tetley, or Red Rose. I am not sure of the difference between coconut flavor and coconut extract, but the Frontier product added coconut flavor to my recipe. I haven't compared it with other coconut flavors so I don't know if it could have been stronger. I had to use quite a bit to get the coconut punch I wanted but I appreciate that it is a natural alcohol free alternative to the imitation coconut flavor available at my neighborhood grocery store.

The instructions tell you to add the milk and the pudding mix, stir for 2 minutes and then say the magic words and watch the pudding change color. In reality, as soon as you add the milk, the green powder turns into green pudding. Super lame. The kids were bummed.

The packaging lends itself very well for gift giving. The selection is great, without duplicates. My one complaint is the expiration date of the product, since the whole idea behind this Keurig coffee craze is convenience and freshness. Two months until the coffee expires is not very fresh in my book. I would not purchase coffee off the shelf with such a date. Another box of coffee I purchased at the same time had six months until expiration, which I consider to be reasonable. Twenty dollars is a pretty steep price for 20 cups of coffee that aren't very fresh.

I eat this on Peanut Butter Toast for breakfast everyday and love it. I think it's a great product. As good as regular marshmallow dip? Probably not. But, if you want a hint of marshmallow with no calories in one serving. It's good stuff.

I have been reading about the healthy properties of cherries and since fresh cherries are only in season for a short time, there are a great alternative. They taste great and you don't need a lot to enjoy the benefits, plus they come in a sturdy resealable pack so they are great to take with you for a snack at any time.

I was really excited to try this flour after so many great reviews. I've been baking gluten-free for over 2 years now and am at the point where I can make most things taste exactly like gluten items, but find that many recipes take quite a bit of adjustment and almost all get dry quickly. I've gotten a bit tired of mixing flours and wanted to try a quick solution. Anyway, as soon as my sample came in I made vegan cinnamon rolls from the recipe on the Better Batter website. I immediately noticed a funky smell to the flour, which I think is from all the tapioca. Now, the recipe came together beautifully, the consistency of the dough was perfect and I've never before gotten quite such perfect texture and rise in gluten-free breads. That said, the tapioca taste is overwhelming! Even my husband, who is not picky at all, had to force the first few bites. After that the cinnamon does cover up the funky flour taste, but I could never feel confident serving something I made with this product to guests. It has what I consider to be the distinctive "gluten-free taste" that you find in most processed gluten free foods. This taste has always bothered me way more than texture issues. I guess it's back to mixing my own mixes using Authentic Foods superfine flours.

Like many of you I struggle with my weight.
And like many of you I've been looking for a shortcut.

Ephedra.
Caffeine.
Hoodia.
Acai Berry.
African Mango.

Ephedra worked but was too dangerous and was outlawed.
Caffeine only has a mild thermogenic effect and keeps me up at night.
Hoodia doesn't work.
Acai Berry may have great antioxidant properties but is for the most part useless for weight loss.
African Mango is the same as Acai.
There are many others but all are the same, all hype and no results.
Then I was reading a new diet book from France called "The Dukan Diet".
The diet itself is ok but I have no intention of following it.
I've already lost 20 lbs since January 1st of 2011 and I have about 20 more to go before I reach my goal weight just through some common sense approaches to everyday eating.
Meaning, cutting out the junk food and soda.
In "The Dukan Diet" the author suggest you eat one tablespoon of raw oat bran before every meal.
This made me curious so I decided to try it.
Now I realize that Quaker oats are rolled oats not oat bran.
The only difference between the two are that bran has a slightly better nutritional profile.
That's it.
So for my experiment I just got the darn rolled oats.
And.........take a tablespoon right before a meal with a huge glass of water and........baam........greatly reduced appetite.
Why bother with expensive and ineffective hoodia when every grocery store has Quaker Oats?
You want something that suppresses your appetite then this is really the Holy Grail you've been looking for.
Try it; you'll be very happy with the results.
Not only will it suppress your appetite but it has fiber and other goodies your body needs.
It also keeps your blood sugar down which aids with preventing weight gain.
Guess what?
Mom was right, oatmeal IS good for you.
So far I'm down another five pounds.
Yayyyyyyyy.
This is one "supplement" I can definitely stick with for the long haul.
I know a lot of folks don't like the taste but I just let it dissolve in my mouth and it tastes like granola.
Not gross, quite tasty actually.
I can't believe I'm giving something I wouldn't go near as a child my highest recommendation but I am.
Maybe we do get smarter as we get older.
Too bad we get fat, bald, and wrinkly too.
Oh well, I suppose everything has to have a trade off.
Quaker Oats?
Tasty.
Nutritious.
Good for weight loss.
Recommended.
I've eaten Mapo tofu in China and the US. This sauce is nothing like I've ever tasted, in the worst way possible.

review_title: Zoe Diva Select Olive Oil is great
review_text: I really like the Zoe Diva olive oils. It has a mild flavor, and we use it in just about everything. The price was good and delivered to my door, how can you beat that.

You gotta love this gadget. Keeps my k-cups in order - - not in my cabinet, not on the counter, not in the pantry . . . r i g h t above and to the left of my keurig coffee maker. The ONLY reason I did not give it 5 stars was because I wanted it all to be hidden . . however the space under the upper cabinet is slightly shallow so you can see 1/2 of the k-cups in their slots. No fault of the gadget, just be aware it will only hide the cups if the depth of the aforementioned cabinet is deeper than the standard. New kitchen cabinets are in my future . . . and I know that this space needs to be about 1/2" more than standard if it continues to bother me.

Exceptional product, but as of this writing suggest that the company work on the cost of shipping and the packaging. We did two orders and the bars were placed in a very large box for their size. On one order of only 4 bars, the shipping was $16.
Maybe one needs to order more, but in this case only 4 were needed.
Again, a really exceptional product, especially the mint and orange truffle bars!

Excellence is what we've come to expect every time we open a bag of a Bob's Red Mill product, and their Soy Flour is no exception. Soft as cornstarch, it adds just the right amount of low-gluten volume to allow for more successful results when working with vegetarian/vegan alternatives or ultra-soft muffin or pastry doughs. Just made some gluten free bread using this flour and the Authentic foods sorghum flour. These flours make wonderful bread. Its a keeper.

I Received this product as part of the Amazon Vine Program.

This has a good taste not too sweet and not strong.

This is not comparable to a coffee shop Cappucino. It would not replace it.

It is more on the go and no-time-to-stop type of deal.

I was curious about trying this brand of natural sweetner. I previously bought some stevia that had a weird aftertaste. However, this brand is great. It's the actual sugar crystals and no aftertaste. One packet can sweeten 16 oz perfectly. Will definitely be buying again!
Works well making wheat bread. Price and volume fits the need for making 3-5 units per week. Store it up to nine months after opening in freezer.
This was a good selection of flavored K-Cup coffees & I recieved a free box of K-Cup hot cocoa with my purchase

We love these sardines.They are firm and whole, they don't fall to pieces when you pick them up. We tried some from Walmart and they were mushy so my kids would not eat them. We think they taste great and they are a convenient way to get some fish into our diet.

I ordered these from Amazon, but they are not listing it as a verified purchase which is odd. I only mention this as I tend to believe the verified purchases eliminate the "shill factor." The nuts were delivered in perfect condition on a hot day. These candies are delicious and if you can resist not eating at least half of one package as a single serving, you have more self control than I! Organic dark chocolate covered Brazil nuts are difficult to find at a reasonable price. Now I wish I had purchased more while they were on sale. The nuts are fresh tasting medium sized covered in a glossy dark chocolate. This chocolate does not readily melt on your fingers, such as non-glossy chocolate typically does. I am enjoying these heavenly treats (5 pieces for 219 calories).

review_title: Dentist-approved, although the flavor isn't everlasting.
review_text: My dentist recommended this gum (apparently the Recaldent stuff isn't just advertising). The cool mint is a good mint flavor, comparable to any other mint (not spearmint or peppermint) gum. Only problem is that the flavor doesn't last particularly long.

review_title: Great tasting and very high in fiber
review_text:
I love all of Eden's products I have bought and I have bought many. They have quality in every package.

This item is something I bought thinking it was gluten free, but after reading more info on Kamut via the internet, I don't think it is. Thankfully I am not gluten intolerant, simply wheat sensitive. This product does not seem to have the same affect on my body as regular wheat.

It is high in fiber (5g) per serving for pasta and 9g of protein (Unheard of in pasta), plus high in phosphorus and magnesium (both 20% of the recommended daily requirements).

My boyfriend who is a huge bread and pasta lover said this tasted just like the real thing served with spagetti sauce - we did not try it alone with butter.

I have an issue with the ingredients. The very first ingredient is "Organic Reduced Minerals Whey". Um, what is that? According to Google, it is a by-product of cheese manufacturing. I can't find anything about the nutritional soundness of this stuff, just other moms going "what is this?". Most other baby formulas have milk as their first ingredient, or whey protein. I'm guessing reduced minerals whey is similar to whey protein but I just don't know enough about it for it to be the #1 product in my daughter's diet. To me, it sounds like a cost cutting measure.

I would much prefer to give my daughter organic formula but all the organic formulas I've found have these reduced minerals as the first ingredient, except for Baby's Only, which is brown rice syrup, aka sugar. So until I find an organic formula with the primary ingredient being milk, we are going with non-organic. I just don't think it is worth compromising the quality of the ingredients for organic.
Great taste. Started eating it on a vacation to NZ. Hard to find in the states.

I've tried so many breads and I love Pamela's and use it for bagels, hot rolls, hamburger buns and bread. It is great tasting, easy to make and so near like the wheat kinds of bread. I won't use any other brand. My family even likes it.

Friends call me an addict! Yes, I eat these everyday for lunch. They are the best. I have tried other brands and got tired of mushy fish and blah flavors. These are worth every penny. No surprise - I have a subscription to these. They are not like eating a light fish. Try them with a spray of extra virgin oil and fresh lemon. You already know how healthy fish is for you; now see the results while really enjoying the fish. Oh yes, I have been eating them for five years and am still loving them everyday.

For a 'gooey' cookie, I was expecting something, well, a bit more gooier and a stronger fudge taste. These cookies had a decent chocolate taste and didn't lack for chocolate chips, but while they were soft they didn't really leave that much of a gooey impression (not to go overboard on the word, here, even if it's what's blazoned on the package). If anything, they leave a bit of a dry mouth and call out for a glass of milk while munching on them, along with a bit of a sugary aftertaste (I had to double-check to see if there was any artificial sweeteners in the ingredients and didn't see any jump out from the list).

So all in all, not a bad cookie if you're looking for something soft and chocolately to satisfy a chocolate craving, but a bit disappointing if you're hoping for something more along the lines of fudgey and moist.

review_title: These are not as good as they look!
review_text: My dog loves pumpkin-based canned food on his staple kibble (with warm water on top all mixed in to make it a meal...like hot soup), but he doesnt care for these. i spent a lot of money on these too. they are very light and airy, so 3 ounces is like a decent size canister. the shipper was good though. write me here on amazon me if you want to buy these from me. they are fresh and purchased April 2011 and will be stored in a cool, dry, dark place now. oh, i have a golden (if that matters). review_text: I was once in this dance contest and I won one of these delicious boxes of Duncan Hines Cake mixes. My Duck Walk cleared the floor as the crowd shouted for more. Do not waste your money on this product. It has an awful rancid flavor (this might be due to improper storage procedures and oxidation). Cannot return it either - Amazon return policy. Go for a superior product like the one from KAF.

A great discovery.

review_title: The only formula my son will drink
review_text: I exclusively breast fed my son for the first four months and then had to start supplementing with formula due to weight gain issues. We originally started with Enfamil but he wouldn't drink it. Then we moved on to the ready made and he loved it, but it made him sick. Long story short, he had a milk protein allergy. We then moved on to the Enfamil brand soy powder. He took it for a week and then refused it all together. Finally, we found this ready to eat Similac w soy. It has saved us! These 8 oz bottles are perfect. Just open the formula and make a bottle. The measurement is always right and the formula is always room temp so there is no need to warm it. We originally tried the soy powder but he wouldn't take that either. The 8 oz size is great for older babies. The 2 oz size is great for newborns. Unfortunately, they do not have a measurement for in between. If your child drinks 4 oz you either need to use 2, 2 oz bottles or half of an 8 oz bottle. I am giving it 1 star because they boast just adding a nipple to the bottle and feeding, but it doesn't come with said nipples. You have to buy them separately. Overall, we cannot live without this formula and highly recommend it!
Tazo tea is my favorite, although Passion is not my favorite variety. I ordered several different boxes and flavors, and all were great. The price was right, and shipping was fast.

review_title: Great for toddlers! They are yummy, but don't completely mask the veggie flavor
review_text:
My son is 16 months and, while he is a balanced and good eater, I still feel he needs more vegetables during the day. I try to send portions of canned veggies to day care (which, sadly, are his favorite -- I think it's because they're soft and he only has 5 teeth -- I buy the "no salt added ones"), but I don't think they really worry about whether he eats the veggies as long as he eats SOMETHING. WELL, with these pouches I don't have to worry as much that he isn't getting the nutrients and exposure to the flavor of the veggies. He will drain these pouches in a second and then keep handing it back to me to so I can squeeze it to get the last drops to come out. Sadly for him, he can already get the last drop out on his own so I have to tell him it's all gone.

And, just in case you're worried they are mostly fruit, you're probably right. Pear is listed as the first ingredient with peas and green beans listed after. But, I did taste this one and it tastes exactly like pear sauce and pea puree mixed togehter. It was pretty sweet, but so are actual peas. I was impressed that the pear flavor didn't completely mask the pea flavor, so he is still developing a taste for vegetables, not just fruit. Bottom line -- he loves these and it is better than no veggies at all!

I give it a 4 only because they are (too) pricey. I get that they are organic, so maybe they have to be that expensive, and it's expensive packaing, but $1.50 per pouch is a bit much. You can buy pouches like this from Motts and other companies (in boxes of 4) that are just apple sauce or apple/strawberry sauce for much, much cheaper. Likewise, Beechnut makes some with sweet potato and pumpkin that are $.99. The thing is, none of those products include any GREEN veggies, which is why I shell out the $ for these. I suggest buying in bulk because it will save you a little $ and you're going to want to have one for every day.

I've tried several brands of stevia -- I found them funny tasting. I persisted in trying different types because of the lure of an all natural, no-calorie sweetener. The artificial sweeteners don't agree with me. And I never liked the idea of putting them in my body. Nu Naturals Liquid Stevia is MUCH better than any stevia I tried before. I use it in cold and hot foods/beverages, as the lone sweetener or sometimes with a natural sweetener like maple syrup or honey (to reduce calories). Because I like it so much, I haven't bothered to try Nu Naturals Stevia Powder, so I can't offer a comparison. I highly recommend this stevia product!
If you plan on eating these directly, they are not good. Buying these will make you want to make your own, knowing your own first try will taste better.

review_title: Delicious, nutritious, but online it's expensive-itious
review_text:
For some reason, this listing doesn't indicate what flavor of vitamin water zero is being sold on this page. Big oversight. I found this page because I was searching for acai-blueberry-pomegranate - the image here could be that particular flavor but is slightly lighter and may instead be fruit punch or dragonfruit.

I was also wondering if there were any bulk pricing deals on Amazon that would save me the hassle of constantly lugging dozens of bottles from my local store, but that doesn't appear to be the case.

One way or another, I am completely and totally hooked on vitamin water zero and recommend it highly. It is very tasty, and nutritionally has the following:
100% of daily requirements for vitamin c
100% for vitamin B5 B6, B12
25% for vitamin vitamin A
100% for vitamin niacin
100% for vitamin pantothenic acid
manganese, vitamin B3, magnesium and antioxidant minerals
There are few if any preservatives.

It's also ZERO calories and flavored with stevia, a natural sweetener, supposedly less questionable healthwise than sucralose/splenda or other artificial sweeteners.

I've tried about six flavors of vitamin water zero (note that it looks almost the same as glaceau vitamin water and glaceau vitamin water XXX so pay attention to the label) and acai-blueberry-pomegranate is by far my favorite. I don't care too much for the lemonade one - it's too sweet.

In regard to the price, yes, it is over-priced here, especially when you add shipping. In my own local environment (near Boston), about six supermarkets and grocery stores near me carry it, at prices ranging from 99 cents per bottle to 3 bottles for $4 to (at pricey convenience stores) $1.99 a bottle. Given that I drink three bottles a day, the latter is too expensive. So basically I buy it in bulk at the lowest price, and dilute it to make it last longer. It's still tasty when you add a considerable amount of water to it.

Amazon does have a listing or two priced at 99 cents a bottle, and with free shipping, but the only ones I've seen so far are is the lemonade flavor, which is actually quite popular. But if you're a person who prefers lemon in water without sugar, then you too would probably find the lemonade flavor too sweet.

Since there are many electrolyte-type waters out there that are not very tasty, have limited nutritional value, a lot of preservatives and sometimes a hefty amount of calories - do yourself a favor at try glaceau vitamin water zero. However if buying at Amazon, do a search for all of them listed to find the best price and best shipping deal.

review_title: $19.99 for a case at Home Depot
review_text:
You can purchase a case of this for $19.99 plus sales tax at Home Depot. They also have Fanta Orange for the same price. I have also seen individual bottles at Publix Supermarket for $1.39. Publix also has Mexican Sprite.

The difference is sugar instead of corn syrup. Sugar is better. The US and Japan are the only countries in the world that use corn syrup instead of sugar.

Many grocery items on Amazon aren't worth it for one simple reason. They are not directly sold by Amazon. They are sold by a third-party who also charges shipping. People are looking to make a buck off of people who don't pay attention or don't research. Many people don't know you can buy Mexican Coke at Home Depot or other local retailers. Publix will order you as much Mexican Coke as you want and you may get a volume discount.

I gave this two stars only because the price is absurd and because there are no other two star items. The product is fine for sugar water.
