The Salt content!
you can taste the salt before the beef jerky

review_title: Good but not as memorable as I'd anticipated.
review_text:
I am not a coffee snob. I am, however, a former barista who appreciates coffee that has been properly roasted ground and brewed. When I have company I often am complimented on what a good cup of coffee I've served and delight in explaining to my guests that my secret is fresh, coarse ground beans and a french press. That said I do like Starbucks' Breakfast Blend quite a bit though I prefer medium roasts and have recently discovered an excellent fair trade store brand Columbian that is divine.

Unless I am in the mood for a coffee treat (think Caramel Frappucinos) or a home brewed cappucino I only drink my coffee black. I've loved the smell and taste of coffee since I was far too young to be drinking it so when I drink it I want to taste coffee, not sugar and dairy. A badly made cup of coffee is downright depressing. I won't drink percolated coffee unless there is nothing else available because it tends to destroy the oils in coffee beans that give them a smooth flavor. Same with overly roasted beans. But I'm getting off subject here...

What did I think of Melitta Cafe Collection's Blanc et Noir?

The first thing I noticed when I opened the bag was that I wasn't instantly hit with its scent. Half of what I love about making coffee is what I like to think of as the foreplay... that enticing odor teasing me when I open up the bag! Okay, I guess I can live without it. As this is a very finely ground blend I chose to prepare it as espresso. I would not recommend this coffee to those who use a french press (course ground is best when using that method) as you will end up drinking a LOT of the powdery grind that passes through the mesh of your press which for some can cause digestive upsets and for others is just gross. I did not try percolating it but I'm certain others who will review this product will use that method so check out what they thought if you'd like to know.

I tried Blanc et Noir both as my preferred cuppa, black, and with steamed milk, as a latte. I was expecting a much stronger flavor for such a beautiful dark brew. As a stand-alone cup of black coffee it wasn't overly memorable. Smooth without the bitterness darker roasts often have or the acidic bite of lighter roasts. It sounds good in theory but by combining the two they somehow loose what makes each special. Which is why I tried it with some steamed milk and a little foam as well. I must say that if you like milk in your coffee this is a lovely coffee to pair with it. Unlike traditional espresso which is a dark roast, the added light roast just gave it something I can't seem to find the right word for.

While I don't think Blanc et Noir is one I'd choose for brewing at home given other options I would definitely request it if it were being offered at a coffee house I was patronizing.

Ingredient list is nutritionally solid, but you can't put strawberry seeds into baby food and expect it to be well-tolerated. I actually give all kinds of these pouch baby food purees to my 3-year-old boy because he loves them and they're good for you no matter what your age. Of the dozens of different varieties of pouch baby foods I've given him, this is the only one he has rejected, saying "I don't like seeds." He is typically pretty tolerant for his age but this grossed him out.

Also obviously the price here is ridiculous.

review_title: Rye flour at Amazon really made my day
review_text:
I live in a rural area of South Carolina where rye flour must be a dirty word. I have looked in all the stores available within 50 miles, Walmart included, with no success. Thank you Amazon for offering this product.

I am very pleased with Arrowhead am rye organic flour. I can't stop baking rye bread with caraway seeds. I think I'm addicted to the stuff.

My order was received in perfect condition and professionally packaged. No loose flour or tears, etc. The flour is wonderful to work with and I use it in my 10 yr. old machine with great success. Thanks for offering quality products at a reasonable price.

review_title: Chip's surface has Shiney, PLastic-y Pre-fab appearance
review_text: Not overly flavorful, and it tasted very prepackaged for a hippie-type snack. I was expecting a snack more like the yummy hummus-chips as opposed to a communion wafer

review_title: a wonderful treat for the pups
review_text: these arrived very quickly & i was excited to see how the pups would like them. i like that there are so many in the package. i've always held in high regard anything rawhide & sweet potato so this was a no brainer....our pups love both! these sticks are the best! they incorporate chewing, gnawing, flavor & fun. i definitely would buy these again.

review_title: Cats still skepticle, though I am firm.
review_text: I have been giving this to my cats for nearly two years on an off and on basis. I feel this is healthier than some of the other foods, I can afford. I do have one pan of this, and one pan of the other good brand I have been feeding. The cats don't seem to eat as much when I feed only Diamond brand. Two are in let's say early stages of obesity, and the other two are trim. They all hunt, and three are very active, the fourth is also considering his age. He can certainly move when he needs to, he escaped the close teeth of a pit bull a couple weeks ago. The pan of the other food will soon be replaced with Diamond brand as soon as that last bag is gone. I think their coats, and health is a good testament for the Diamond brand food. It even looks good to me, no I am not about to try it, but it looks richer.

review_title: Looks good on paper but my dog never stopped being sick.
review_text: We used Nature's Variety (non Instinct formula) for a while, but when we moved to our current location, the closest we could find was Natural Balance, so we had to switch. When NB put out their grain free Alpha Dog, I tried that out and was amazed at how my normally very smelly Boston Terrier suddenly didn't seem to have any gas at all. This got me searching for reviews, where I discovered the NV's Instinct line was rated much higher than NB's Alpha dog. When I discovered the Amazon subscriptions, and how inexpensive that makes it(relative to what I pay at the pet store for organic pet food), I decided to give NV another try. For the first week, I mixed the last of the old food with the new to get him used to it. He had diarrhea and the worst gas EVER for days. Now it's been a week on just the NV food and the sickness has not stopped. I though maybe he was suffering from seasonal allergies and that's why he seemed so blahhh lately. Last night I put it all together and realized it's the food. I really wish I could get a refund, but at least I stopped the subscription in time not to get another.

review_title: Not a better canned tomato product out there.
review_text:
Okay - I should probably qualify this by saying there is no way I have tasted EVERY canned tomato product out there - but I have tasted a lot of them, including many of the DOP certified.

Using San Marzano tomatoes makes a significant difference in any dish I use them in, but especially in the classic tomato sauces. Rich, sweet, gorgeous color. Incredible price for ORGANIC San Marzanos - my pantry will never be without a case of these ready to go - with these on hand I can whip up something pretty impressive on the fly. And I never have to reach for a can of tomato paste to add body or extra tomato umph to sauces - this provides plenty.

So yeah - these do not have the official DOP designation - but the flavor is what counts and these have it in abundance.

I have not yet had a problem with receiving dented or otherwise damaged cans - this will be my 4th case from Amazon. Also - gotta admit I love the pull-tab lids - not a reason for buying a specific brand of tomatoes certainly, but it is a nice bonus for this lazy girl.

As I will continue to order these as long as they are offered and I am still cooking, I will be sure to update if I receive a damaged case. Sad to see it has been an issue with some.

I was pleasantly surprised to see this in the organic section of my local grocer. Bought 2 cans, got home, and was ready to tear my new discovery up!

After 1 bite I was ready to throw my bowl in the sink, it was wretched. I took a 2nd bite to make sure I wasn't hallucinating on how the potato taste took over the entire soup. By the 3rd bite, I was done. It was as if the potato sucked up all the spices and left the entire soup bland, its all I could taste besides a hint of the coconut. For a second I thought I was dining at a Luby's restaurant it was so bland.

This is not a good substitute to what you can get in a Thai restaurant. I would suggest the following products, of which I would give 4 or 5 stars:
Thai Kitchen Coconut Ginger Soup, 14 Ounces (Pack of 12)
A Taste of Thai Coconut Ginger Soup Base, 2.1-Ounce Packets (Pack of 12)
They are more on par with the authentic taste.

I would stay clear of this soup. If you still want to try it, I would suggest going to your local grocer and pick up 1 can only and go from there.

While it's more authentic, and probably has better taste to mix and grind the spices as you use them, this isn't always practical, especially if your meals range over a lot of different cuisines.

This garam is very nice, and works well for everything I've made, very tasty.

Without luck, I tried to find an excellent decaf K-cup for my Keurig brewer. Now I heat my water in the Keurig and put decaf Italian brew Starbucks into the water in my cup. Excellent. Extra cost is well worth the flavor. Packaging keeps coffee fresh.

I bought these almonds after reading the previous 1 star review. After tasting them, I realized that they are 100% over-roasted. Only positive thing I can say about these almonds is the packaging. But apart from that...they really taste burnt. I hope that the manufacturers of these almonds can stop over roasting them and add a little bit extra salt. I have down-graded the rating from 3 to 2 stars as I think these are well below average tasting almonds.

I am a great coffee lover and am constantly searching for the perfect cup that I can brew at home. Tully's French Roast is at the top of the list with my K-cup brewer. The coffee is delicious, great dark roast taste, and I do look forward to each morning great cup of coffee....Each night before I go to bed I am excited to get up the next morning to my great cup of coffee.....

The price was great as we saved quite a bit of money compared to retail outlets. The delivery was prompt and we were lucky to get it under a free shipping promotion. The product itself is one of the best scone mixes that we have tried.

I buy the smaller version of these logs too and I love the size of these! They're hefty and they last a while. Easy to open and hang up with the built in hook eye. They hold up very well in warm weather too. A nice alternative to greasy suet.

review_title: Bear was Awesome, shipping was tight
review_text: Got everything on time and in pretty good condition. the packaging was just a little too small and squished the bear.

These were given to me as a gift this year. I have a well-developed sweet tooth, and these truffles are so rich and smooth that I could only eat one or two at a time. They are the perfect little indulgence.

review_title: The crackers are great as usual.
review_text:
The product was offered for review because of the packaging. They are stacks of square crackers, one half of the large ones that we are used to. They come in two serving packs. I liked having the crackers already broken in half, because that's the size I give to a child. I think I'd prefer to have re-sealable packaging, but this wasn't bad. We never did have to eat soggy or stale crackers.
Just be aware, this is a package that is a right size for having several toddlers hanging on to your skirts, not a size pre-packaged to put in lunch boxes.

Tasty and spicy. This is a major upgrade from the el'cheapo ramen you might be used to during the college days. If you are big eater like me, this will not be a full meal, but it can come close to it if you add a couple eggs while cooking (or even after if you are ok with that).

review_title: Not as good as the other MTR products
review_text:
I am an Indian guy and a big fan of MTR products. MTR products which I like include: Chaana Masala, Bhindi Masala and Rasam Rice. However, I was not pleased with the "Pau Bhaji"

We opened this package last night for dinner and there was a pungent horrible smell from the packet. We had to throw it in the trash and make paav bhaaji from scratch.

Also, the contents of the package did not look as depicted on the picture on the box.

I am going to try Kitchens of India brands.

review_title: I love them, Great Low Carb Snack
review_text: When I ordered was somewhat nervous, because of other ratings that were negative. So relieved when they got here and I tried them. They taste like oven baked cheddar bites-which I really like. If you ordered them thinking they were going to be exactly like potato chips, you would be disappointed. I have been on a low carb diet since Feb now and look for snack type items, with crunchy being a big plus-these are very tasty and I will definitely be getting them again. (as well as trying some of the other flavors!) They are salty, which I like. I have to disclose however that my DH is not as happy with them as I am -we do have a tendancy to like opposite flavors. So for me that just means more for my lunch pail. But if you are like me and scrape the cheese off the cookie sheet when your baking has some overflow-you will probably really like these. Only rated them 4 star because of DH -who still ate them by the by and said he would rate them as ok, so since I would say 5 star. Compromise, right?

review_title: A bit too salty, but other flavors are nice
review_text:
I love Indian food and often buy various curry pastes in a local Indian store. I decided to try Kitchen of India Curries, and this Fish Paste was the first one I have tried.

I am attaching 5 photographs I took along my preparation.

The paste comes in a silver blister pack that can be stored without refrigeration for a long time. So these packages are good to keep on hand when you need a quick meal. Photo shows blister pack just taken out of the package.

The second photo shows the paste removed from the package, so you can see the amount you get in package.
The recipe on the box tells us to add 2/3 of the cup of plain water and dissolve the paste. Dissolved paste is shown on the next photo. The paste dissolved easily, it only took a few minutes.
The fourth photo shows salmon covered with the fish sauce about to go into a microwave.
The fifth photo shows the cooked dish ready to be consumed with some rice.

Overall, I liked the flavors, however I found the sauce too salty for my taste. I had scrape it off the fish to enjoy it more, however what penetrated the fish and a little of the sauce that was left mixed with rice tasted very good.

I think next time, I will try dissolving the paste in 1 cup coconut milk or 1 cup of water (upping the amount recommended on the box) to dilute the salty flavor more.

The box said that there is enough paste for 4 servings, and in fact this was exactly right. The amount you see in the photos will be 4 servings of fish. Each serving of sauce is 45 calories. I suspect it was less for me since I did not eat all of the source due to saltiness. The amount of Sodium per serving is 580 mg. I bought a variety pack, so I compared the amount of Sodium in Fish curry to other flavors from Kitchens of India, and Fish curry has the second lowest amount among the 6 flavors. So this tells me to dilute the next one I try more than I plan to dilute this one.

So bottom line: I found the flavor to be pleasant, and the I believe the saltiness can be improved by using more water/coconut milk to dissolve the paste than recommended on the package.

We were recommended by a friend that the malt beverage can enhance the milk for breast feeding. My wife started drinking this after our kid was born, and it worked very well. She drinks 1 bottle a day or every 2 days. The hop is the ingredient stimulating the milk, and the malt can boost her energy level. Perfect drink.

review_title: Excellent taste, fresh, no need of cooking. Company has said it has no Arsenic.
review_text:
I was worried about Arsenic, which quite often pollutes Rice Bran, especially for brans coming from China. So I called Bobs Red Mill customer service to ask if there was any control on the level of Arsenic in the bran. They told me it comes from California and there is no risk of it being contaminated with Arsenic. I hope thats the truth...
Its too bad that they dont offer an organic one. I searched everywhere and it seems an organic rice bran is nowhere to be found.
Anyway this is really fresh, tastes fantastic, great smooth texture, you can use it in sooo many ways, and it's ready to eat, no need of cooking it.

I was hesitant to purchase this product, based on other reviews addressing issues with a manufacturer defect; however, I placed the order anyway and was very disappointed to find that several of the k-cups caused a very nasty, gritty substance to come out of the k-cup when making coffee. I had to use four k-cups before I was able to finally get an actual cup of coffee. Once I finally was able to make a true cup of coffee, the taste was pretty good (why I am giving two stars versus one star). I must've gotten an older batch of the k-cups because they apparently explode in the keurig machine if the top of the k-cup is flat instead of bubbled. I had to throw away over half of my package and needless to say, I will not be wasting my hard-earned money on these k-cups in the future. It's a shame, because for the price and the taste, it seemed like a good deal. It was very inconvenient to have to clean my keurig machine after the k-cups exploded. I have ordered other name brand k-cups (I.e. donut shop, green mountain, Gloria jeans, timothy's, etc) and I've never had an issue with their k-cups exploding in my keurig machine. I thought their manufacturing defect was resolved, but appartently it has not been resolved.
The Flavor does lasts awhile....too bad the gum itself doesn't. It does taste great and you can feel the minty freshness it just doesnt lasts as long as I had thought it would.

review_title: Not the best soba on the block
review_text: If these are "No. 1" in Japan as the label says, maybe it's because children there like the blander taste. I think noodles need a little salt; other soba brands vary quite a bit in sodium content but zero is not enough for best flavor. I haven't tried the Annie Chun - from the picture they seem darker in color and that might indicate a bit more oomph.

I love this stuff!! I drink it for my breakfast drink. All of The grocery stores are not carrying this and therefore, good ole Amazon will be getting my business once again! Amazon is the best.....keep up the good work Amazon!!
If you like instant this is a good deal. I priced it at Cub, a local grocery store, and the unit price was about two bucks less. This tastes fine. It tastes like instant coffee.

OMG....this has got to be THE most foul tasting slop I have ever tried. It tastes nothing like cheese and queso dip but more like yellow/orange food coloring and thick motor oil. I can not possibly even conceive it that anyone at the factory tasting lab spooned this slop in their mouth and exclaimed its goodness and said "Let's go with it".
It's difficult to explain how bad this tastes, the only thing that comes to mind is a motor oil and gasoline mix.
Please please PLEASE! For your own good.....leave this crap on the shelf!
I'm doing this for you people!

This is a hot smoked wild caught Alaskan pink salmon, prepared in the traditional Native American Style, not European Style cold smoked Lox. This product is best served at room temperature, rather than cold, which is what the previous reviewer probably did, thinking they had purchased lox. The 'goo' that came out of their pouch was probably the result of the refrigeration. I've been eating this product for several years (they opened back about 25 years ago) and I've never had 'goo' come out of a package.

A note about the smell, cold smoked lox doesn't have nearly the strong smoked salmon smell/taste as hot smoked salmon, so it can be a big surprise for those who aren't familiar with the product.

Personally, I prefer Sockeye over Pink Salmon, however both products are very tasty. It's great to serve with cheese and crackers, or to break up and add to an alfredo pasta dish. Just remember, it isn't lox!

Just got a coffee drawer for my new kitchen -- what a pleasant surprise ... It looks good and functions as expected. Great design! It is just the right size -- fits right under my keurig machine, no extra space taken from my limited counter space. Fill the drawer with an assortment of coffees and teas and your choice is at your finger tip.

Maxwell House International Coffee Peppermint Mocha Latte has a very nice flavor and a lovely foamy top when mixed. It's simple and quick to make and a nice cup of coffee. I'm more of a "little coffee with my cream and sugar" kind of person so this is the perfect type of product for me as I really didn't get much of a flavor of actual coffee.
I give this item four stars as it has quite a nice flavor and makes a nice, hot, relaxing drink.

Had come across a number of comments mentioning that the taste isn't the same as it used to be.

I don't know how it tasted previously, but the contents of this packaging are perfectly fine in taste. I found that cooling the contents a bit tasted much better than consuming them at room temperature or chilling them.

Also, the contents contain more sodium than water but, then again, you derive maximum benefit from coconut water if you use it as protection against dehydration or for replenishment, and not as a substitute for regular water. Also, drinks such as Gatorade or Powerade seemed to make me feel more bloated - so far, I have experienced nothing of that sort with Zico. Admittedly, carrying a Powerade bottle to the gym looks much more normal than carrying a Zico coconut water bottle :-)

For what it's worth, my mom-in-law suffers from hyperacidity and has found the Natural variant of ZICO to be a blessing.

I didn't give this 5 stars because I haven't been consuming it for long to know what side effects there might be, and nothing beats real coconut water, right out of a tropical coconut.
Great product, no calories, no sugar easy to prepare and easy to store. Small plastic tubd are great.

i tried golean crunch as part of kashi's free sample gimmick--i'll assume that the quality is the same as the store-bought!... anyway, i rate it highly on taste, as it reminds me a little bit of the honey smacks i used to eat, only not so overly sweet. and its health factor is pretty fantastic too (for me, some things taste better when you know they're good for you, although usually it's the opposite!) but the texture, well, they don't call it crunch for nothing--it was a little too crunchy for me. in some bites, the crystallized honey or whatever was like rock candy, and rock solid. still, it's good stuff, but i'll take one of kashi's crunch bars over this any day.

The best honey ever!!! I have hated honey all my life...used to gag on it as a child when my mother would try to give it to me for a cough or sore throat. This stuff is different, totally. First of all it is not a syrup. It is a creamy pale yellowish mass of ecstasy....finely crystalized goop almost custardlike that I have found myself eating spoonful by spoonful right out of the jar. It is that good. And it is the purest of the pure and most nutritious. So it pleases the senses as well as the mind. Delicious stuff. Try it. Buy it before all the bees die due to Monsanto's Genetically Modified crops which are killing them off world wide. Hurry buy cases of it. You won't regret it. Can even be used on wounds.

I have some that I let sit in a storage bin where it got too hot and must have cooked it. It is now melted and darker and somewhat separated between a lite syrup and crispy crystals. It is still the best though with a little more of that traditional honey flavor. I use a teaspoon of it in my hot oatmeal. Ummm yum. A little goes a long way.

I have loved Kashi cereal for years and recently found these bars, which are like some of the other cereal bars (NutriGrain?) I have had in the past, but from everything I can tell, seem to be of better quality. I think they work great as a snack or a quick breakfast. Better than a candy bar, but not as good a the raw fruit! :)

I used to eat this Runts Candy since i was 8 and i'm now 30, so i ordered the bulk case 30lb, although the packing was not strong enough to carry all the weight but i received it and it really tastes VERY VERY VERY DELICIOUS.
I GOT A TONS OF CANDY ;-)

review_title: Seemed worth a try at first, but, with use, it's really hateful.
review_text:
Just got my first bottle. Delivery unproblematic: fulfilled by "Amazon LLC."; seems of good quality; came very fast. Like Barlean's flax oil, it comes in a non-trasparent-black, squeezable, very soft plastic bottle with a valve in the spout. The bottle is sealed under the cap: you gotta unscrew the cap and peel off the seal before you can use it. Press date is in September, which is a bit old, but otoh, hemp oil isn't as perishable as flax oil, so maybe it's OK.

Since I've just learned of the purported health benefits of this oil, I can only comment on the taste of it: on the bottle, and here, and elsewhere online, hempseed oil is said to have a really nice, "nutty" flavour. Well, not true: actually, it's more medicinal/grassy, reminiscent of rosemary. Not unpleasant, but nothing special -- actually, flax is marginally better (though I don't particularly like it either). I suspect that were it not for their purported healthfulness, no one would use either of these oils. But supposedly they're good, so we'll see. Unlike flax oil (which has some solids floating in it and needs to be shaken up before use), hemp oil has a homogeneous consistency -- like any other cooking oil, except for the colour: a sort of of luminous green, believe it or not; quite a bit like antifreeze. So these are my initial impressions; I'll update this review if I discover anything noteworthy as I use it.

12/28/10: I have to bring the stars down: the taste is horrible; in a matter of days I've grown to hate it -- it's like a greasy medicine of some sort, an emetic. There's _nothing_ it's good with, it SPOILS the taste of anything you add it to. Why do health foods taste so bad? Are they really healthful or it's just hype 'cause the maker wants to sell it? I really can't recommend this product (though it was delivered fine, and in good packaging, so the manufacturing part is fine; just the oil itself is horrible). Flax oil is far less disgusting.

01/08/2011: Just poured the remaining half-bottle down the toilet. A solemn moment of personal liberation. Healthful my as.

review_title: Tofu noodles not  too good and shipment took too long.
review_text: This product just was not that good. It took a really long time to receive the product.

I am a big fan of Terra sweet potato chips for their nutrition and flavor, so I figured I would try the spiced chips. They aren't terrible, but something about the smell reminds me of body odor. I would imagine it is the cumin, so for some it might be an acquired taste.

As for the packaging; They were delivered and left in the sun for a couple hours (we are currently having a 100+ heat wave) and 7/12 of the bags exploded in the box. I think Terra may put too much air in the bags.

What more can I say about a Hershey chocolate product? It's absolutely my favorite combination of flavor and melt-in-the-mouth ecstacy. Packaged easy to freeze,
retains its good qualities after being moved into a special home in the fridge.

review_title: What's the deal with the price
review_text:
This review is based on price not on coffee quality or taste.

Do the math.............

2 boxes of 25 K-cups = 50 K-cups @ 41.95 = $0.839/cup

On Green mountain web site 24 K-Cups @ $13.95 = $0.581/cup

You can get that down to $11.95 for $0.498/cup with the "cafe express".

Even if you buy from Keurig they are $13.95 for 24 K-cups.

I love Amazon but that seems like a BIG price for the joy of buying from Amazon.

review_title: Tastes good, but kicks off my reflux
review_text:
The taste is decent. Not good, but not really horrible. If it didn't kick off my reflux, I could live with it. But, unfortunately, it does, so I can't, so the taste is irrelevant. I appreciate them trying! Maybe a dark french roast isn't the best flavor for a low-acid coffee drink though?

I'm rating this three stars because I think someone with less severe reflux might be okay with it.

This syrup is so~ fantastic! This makes every drink so yummy. Be careful! This is kinda addictive. You'll use this syrup every thing you drink. Also, the price is so good. At my Walmart local shop, the half sized bottle was about $5! What a good deal! This is the best thing I bought from amazon!

I like these for a small lunch, with a little tastey kick to it, not hot but a little spicey. The crackers and the very little plastic spoon to get the tuna onto crackers make this a grab and go lunch for me. While I call this lunch, my son barely calls it a snack. I do have a hard time opening the tuna because the can is small, which makes it hard for me to get a good grip. But I will be getting more becaue I like the unique taste of this tuna.
I think this is an excellent product. I actually thought the cheese was consistent with regular mac & cheese. My daughter loved it.
My sister loves this spice. She uses it on everything. I bought this for her, my Dad, & myself. I've yet to try it, but I'm looking forward to it.

review_title: Great food for a great price
review_text: This is the first non-organic or three dog bakery food that my rescued Aussiedoodle has gotten excited over. She loves the small disk-shaped pieces and the taste! I love that I can feel good about the quality of the ingredients and nutrients, but also the price. Highly recommend. Pro Pac is cheaper then the other high-grade dog foods because they only do their most of their marketing in local pet stores.

review_title: Simply put, I loved this coffee
review_text: Since I am a fan of flavored coffee, particularly coffee flavored with natural ingredients, I knew I had to try this coffee. As soon as I opened the package to brew a pot, my kitchen smelled of hints of vanilla (my four year old son even asked me if I was baking something). If anything, the aroma is most satisfying when drinking this coffee, but the taste isn't too strong. In sum, this coffee is good because of its aroma and delicious taste. I am certainly impressed with Starbucks' new vanilla addition, and I will most definitely continue purchasing this coffee. For a two pack purchase on Amazon, the price is quite reasonable. Bottom line: if you enjoy drinking a nice scented coffee, this one is a must-try!
Mix this sauce 50-50 with catsup for a super topping for meatloaf. Not a sauce to cook with but can be added the last 5 mins. amd on the side. Amazing!!!!!!

After finding the right way to brew this coffee - as strong as I like it - , I realized that there was nothing extraordinary about the flavor. It's not completely bland, it just doesn't have anything special that would make me want to buy it.

Pros:
- Organic
- Part of the money goes to a good cause
Cons:
- Nothing special in the type of roast.
- Too high price.

I'd rather stick to other brands that I like better than buy Marley coffee.
Best horseradish sauce I have tried, not too hot, just right. Makes any sandwich (esp. roast beef) taste just right.

I don't like the three K-cups that are available right now for hot chocolate above about Ok. But this packet version from Ghirardelli is some of the best Hot Chocolate I've ever had. Really a rich chocolate taste.

My four cats LOVE these treats!! They go from easy going animals to crazed fiends at the sound fo the bag opening. No one wants to share. They all want the whole bag for themselves. It is a sight to behold!

This is rather expensive in our local grocery store (Ingles) and I cannot even find it at Wal-Mart! But here on Amazon they've got the BEST deal and man what a smooth taste! You'll love it! Hey try out my tea:
Lovelace's Southern Sweet Tea
Ingredients: 3 Twinings Ceylon Orange Pekoe Tea Bags and 1 1/3 cup of sugar (or Splenda or 2/3 Sugar and 2/3 Splenda to cut calories)
Directions: Pour full coffee pot of water into Coffee maker, put 3 tea bags in drainage area. Allow Pot to fill with Hot tea. Meanwhile, put 1 1/3 cup sugar in your pitcher (or container) after tea's done brewing pour in over sugar. Mix well to ensure sugar has dissolved. Next, pour in enough cool water to fill or for iced tea put in enough ice cubes to make it. Makes 1 gallon of Sweet (southern) Tea. YUMMY!
I got these for seasonal Halloween baking and they are so cute- perfect for cupcakes! Can't wait to use these.

I purchased the Columbian flavor of these K-Cups. The coffee was OK, but it involves some work to get it brew correctly. The sides of the cup are not rigid enough for the keurig maker to puncture the bottom of the cup easily. There is a noticeable difference in the thickness of the walls of these K-Cups versus any of the other brands that I have. When I placed the first cup in the machine and pulled the handle down, the machine started brewing, but nothing came out. I realized that the sides of the cup had bent and the bottom was not punched through, so no coffee could come out. Luckily I realized this before too long so I was able to keep the water and grinds from going all over. In order to get the cup to insert properly and puncture the bottom, I have to quickly jam it into the machine. This is a bit of an inconvenience, but these were the least expensive K-Cups I could find.

I have ordered this several times. I use it in baking, cooking, and have even learned to make my own lotion using this product.

Thanks for a great product!!
Absolutely love this stuff. We use it for everything, salads, dips, meats, everything. Made lots's of Caprese salads this summer. Drizzling the tomatoes with this basalmic vinegar.... yummy!!
excellent product; remains fresh and always easy to use just the amount you need

If you've never tried this chocolate before, you'll be surprised how good the unlikely combination of chocolate with a bit of grainy sea salt can taste. It is considered dark chocolate but its 47% cocoa content is less than most darks - so it might be overly sweet if you're a fan of really dark chocolate. It has a bit of a milk toffee flavor. It's good enough that it's still one of my favorites. In comparison, Ghirardelli Intense Dark Sea Salt Soiree is similar but even better in my opinion...it uses more fully-flavored chocolate as well as almond bits.

review_title: The BEST oatmeal, like a dessert for breakfast!
review_text: This is the BEST oatmeal anywhere on the planet! I found a quick and easy way to cook it for those that like lots of fruit. I use a 'fuzzy logic" rice cooker on the porridge setting (2 hrs.) but set my kitchen timer for only 25 minutes--which is all it takes to cook as it is a sealed rice cooker that steams the contents as it cooks. I use 1/2 of a rice cup measurer of oatmeal, 3/4 of a regular liquid measuring cup of water, 3/4 of a reg. liquid measuring cup of either cranberry, apple or pomegranate juice, then slice up a whole apple, add some dried cranberries, goji berries or other dried fruit and set the rice cooker. 25 minutes later when the oatmeal is done, I add walnuts--and perhaps more fresh fruit if you like. The fruit juice is sweet enough not to need sugar or any other sweetener! It is truly like a very healthy dessert for breakfast!

review_title: Bushy yet brainy with gentle caresses
review_text:
This chamo-spearmint combo killer is both, a powerful mind enhancer and your soothing masseuse of the night. Your mind will buzz at the optimal I.Q. levels while your body will experience an erotic meltdown.

Second, great affordable price.

Third, tiny complaint> packages sealed too tight. You can't open them by hand. And scissors make mistakes so might end up loosing the tailpiece. But that's being nitty picky.

review_title: I Wish Amazon Added Them To Subscribe and Save.
review_text: These crackers are the best I have ever had. I really do wish Amazon would sell them. My only problem with them is I want more and more of them. Super delicious and being from the Glutino brand you can rest assured they are really gluten free.

We own a hair salon and have served this tea for almost 3 years now and our clients come in and request it. It has been a huge hit.
Absolutley love it.

Vitamin Squeeze Energy Drink, Fruit Punch, 12-Ounce

In my opinion, as far as taste, less is more with this product.
I drink only water or tea during the day so I was looking forward to adding a nutritional drink to my diet.
The first time I tried it, I added too much and it tasted like medicine; more like cherry than fruit punch.
As far as packaging, I like having this in a bottle instead of packets however the squeeze part in the cap just did not work for me. I was using a mug to drink from and even though I had this totally upside down it managed to go everywhere! I think just a tiny hole would make it easier and less messy to use. I didn't try this with a water bottle so I assume that would be easier to dispense. Also, with water in my mug I could add more water if I added too much of the drink.
I didn't feel energized at any time after drinking this product. The added vitamins can only enhance my diet so I like this aspect. The vitamin content is only 40% for the B (3, 5, 6, 12) and 120% for Vitamin C and 10% for Chromium and Zinc. No sugar or sodium!
Since I like the liquid form I will probably buy this in a different flavor.

I have been getting this variety of Fancy Feast Medleys on Subscribe and Save for more than two years. I have 2 cats who have been eating this product for two years. Pretty good for Picky Little Kitties, and I think they all are, unless they are truly literally starving, like my Ferals sometimes are when they first wander in.
I do feed this on rotation, so that they do not have it every day...maybe once every 10 days. But they still eat it and they still dig in right away and do not do the "Sniff, nose up in the air 'I will eat it later' routine." They just sit down and eat.
My ferals get it if there are any left overs (otherwise they get dry food and the less expensive Friskies) and, of course, they announce it is just fine.
I figure that if the two Picky Little Kitties are still eating happily after two years, that it must be a winner.
Both have just recently been to the vet and were given a clean bill of health. Neither is overweight and no one is sickly; therefore, My kitties and I recommend it.

review_title: Too sweet! Don't buy food from China if I know in advance!
review_text:
Pickled Ginger shouldn't be so overly sweetened.
I think your offerings should include the country of origin, especially on food products. I make it a rule to not purchase food from China but didn't know this was until I received it.
Laine Massey
YOU CAN GET THIS FOR $20.50 AT SAM'S. THISI IS NOT A GOOD PRICE. I BUY THIS ALL THE TIME AS I HAVE 6 CATS AND THEY LIKE THE FOOD

I love this coffee. I do wish it came already ground as I don't have a grinder and use a blender instead. But it's worth it. Many flavored coffees I've tried only seem to have a flavored odor that doesn't reach the coffee, but this one manages both.

I love this sprinkle cheese product. Put it on mashed potatoes, macaroni, just about anything you want. I sent some to family and they are thrilled with it. The merchant "Hometown Favorites" is the best...I will definitely do business with them anytime.
I was disappointed with these K-cups; they were nowhere as good at most of them I have ordered from Amazon. I would not order again.
Arrived a day early, they taste great & they are very fresh. Will buy again because my family loves them too.

review_title: Organic Chicken Bought Fresh is $3.00 a pound vs. $6.08 lb Canned
review_text:
O.K. I must first admit I haven't purchased this item - I almost did and was about to click to order then I did the math. Even at the lowest price with Subscribe and Save it works out to .38 cents an ounce x 16 ounces = $6.08 per pound. Now we all know even the best quality canned chicken is nowhere near a good tasting as a freshly roasted chicken. My local supermarket sell Full Circle Organic Whole Chicken costs $3.00 a pound. It is so easy to put a whole chicken in a baking pan and roast it for 40 minutes. Plus you can boil down the bones for soup stock and get more value out of it with bone broth - very nutrient dense and great tasting - good for your bones if you eat the yummy warm gelatin broth.

So just consider this a consumer advisory. I couldn't find anywhere in the product info what agencies certifies this as "organic". I have been eating organic food 100% of my diet since 1969 and I never heard of Valley Fresh brand.

If I have my choice of paying $6.08 a pound for chicken in a can which sat in a warehouse for how knows long and $3.00 a pound for a fresh roasting chicken to make homemade chicken, I'll choose homemade anytime.

BTW - Subscribe and Save on Amazon has terrific deals for organic food. Especially on hemp foods and Nutiva and Nativa brand which is very expensive in natural food store but low priced on Amazon Subscribe and Save.

review_title: Decent flavor but slightly musty smell
review_text: We ordered this product based on the mostly positive reviews. When we opened the bag we noticed a slightly musty odor. We did decide to try a pot and while the flavor wasn't too bad, we won't be making a second pot. The smell was too much for us.
This coffee was too weak for me. I found some Green Mountain French Roast decaf at a local Bed and Bath store and really like it - it's bolder, stronger.
I like mushroom bread and adding for a special flavor to other bread. Arrowhead makes good stuff.

review_title: Very delicious - hits that carb craving spot but not too badly
review_text:
I have been buying the grass fed beef, the cagefree happy chicken eggs, the least pasteurized whole cream and watching my carbs in attempting to eat more healthfully. Then I discovered this Chef Boyardee Rice chicken & vegetables. I may not eat perfectly all the time, but when the carb urge hits - plus me being asian - the rice in this entree is the perfect amount, and has the most delicious and satisfying taste! I had been buying Cambells chicken and rice soups but this Boyardee rice with chicken and vegies is more substantial. To me, the mix of rice, vegies, and chicken is perfect and delicious. I am so pleased that this product is available, and you can't fault its amazingly low price. Plus the container is so cute and it fits into your car trunk easily (for emergencies during the winter in Northern Virginia - when the snow is so bad and the traffic so bad that you can't go home for 13 hours while you are sitting in the car along with thousands of others stuck with you as in 2011's snow in DC) for you and to share with others - and also in your glove compartment and in your lunch tote!
I am so pleased this product is available!!!!

review_title: I love this tea - to each her own taste buds!
review_text:
I love the aroma and the taste of the fruit and vanilla combo, and the addition of ginseng for an added energy boost rounds this tea out nicely for me. It's a sweet tea, which makes it a wonderful healthy alternative to desert after dinner.

I see that others didn't like it, but it's really a matter of personal taste...different courses for different horses!
This is a cheese flavored rice... pretty much what you'd expect from the box. A good backup or side.

I routinely get five hours of sleep on weekdays, which leaves me tired all day if i don't do something to combat it. I normally use 5-hour energy, but didn't like the price, so I tried these. They work as well or better than 5-hour, and keep me energized from 8 in the morning until well into the afternoon.

My son (15 mo.) and daughter (3 y/o) both LOVE Mum Mums. We first tried these with my daughter when we started looking for teething biscuits. She had one teething biscuit, which made a nasty orange mess all over her and her clothes, AND a big piece broke off which she choked on. After that, I threw the rest of the box away. I then found Mum Mums, and they have been a fixture in our house/car/diaper bag/stroller since. We lovingly refer to these rice cakes as a "little piece of our sanity." When the kids were first on finger foods, they would slowly munch on these while we fixed meals or were waiting on our meals at restaurants. They disolve, so if a little piece breaks off, it's not a big deal. Their shape is good for little hands that are learning to get food to their mouths. As they have gotten older, they simply enjoy them as snacks. I buy these by the case, and I give a box to my friends as their kids get to that stage where they are wanting to feed themselves. 5 STARS!!!!!

This is wonderful delicious just like movie popcorn. It's salty so nothing extra needed but just the best made at home popcorn ever...I pop it in a dutch oven on my stove top..Can you imagine if it were popped in a popcorn machine? It's just the best!

I would say this is better than most but, not by much. I found it slightly bland for featured use but, good for main course cooking where the oil is used as a tool rather than a treat. All in all you can't go wrong with this product, all I say is shop around a bit you might find something that better suits your all around needs.
Partanna Extra Virgin Olive Oil, 17-Ounces

review_title: yum yum yummy~ I can not stop eating~
review_text:
First I bought YummyEarth Organic pops, not vitamin C in a loal store.
Those are really yumm~ so I hurry to ordered vitamin C things.
However, there are not some taste like, apple, mango, watermelon what are most delicious kinds! There are only berry taste thing in a container. That's why I gave the grade star 2.
But if you ordered first time to try? Yes You may like this as much as I do.

Great quality the flavor lasts long and the price is great.
I chew lots of gum and this is my brand and flavor.
I like it.

Awhile ago I set out to cut back the gluten in our house. This is one of the products I found to try. It makes the most amazing waffles ever!!! Once we had these waffles, Pamelas became a permanent part of our kitchen. All my kids love them, they have protein from the eggs and are super, super easy. I also use this if a recipe calls for flour, like a gravy, etc. It works great. No one would believe its gluten-free! I will say that its not a 100% health product. Its about half white rice and half brown from what I can tell. So its not a "whole grain" product. We make the waffles about once a week or every other week, as a treat. top with organic jelly or raw honey!

review_title: By far the BEST GF cake mix I've found!
review_text: My little girl is allergic to wheat and I love baking for her. We've tried multiple brands and haven't been satisfied. I decided to give this gf cake mix a try and it makes PERFECT cupcakes and cakes! So fluffy, the texture is identical to its wheat counterpart! My only complaint is that it's made with rice flour. I wish there were other Betty Crocker GF cake mixes that use potato or tapioca flours. My daughter also happens to be allergic to rice but she gets a cake made with rice flour for special occasions.

As far as flavor goes, this is my favorite. Since I enjoy the healing properties of green tea, I mix the two together. I drink this all year round. I've stayed with Celestial Seasonings because I happen to like it the best.

review_title: King Leo Soft Peppermint sticks gift tin
review_text:
I love to give these as gifts at Christmas time.
Everyone loves them and having them in the tin keeps them soft. I bought this for my 4 year old niece who is allegric to peanuts. Will definitely keep buying these every year.

My turkey sauage bites arrived before time and in great condition.But, because of the bad reviews, I opened all 6 packages to check them out--perfect. Now, what to do with 6-8oz packes of sauage! I put a covered dish by my computer and another by my viewing chair. I made a variety of flavors out of the rest in 4oz wide mouth ball jars---one with bay leave and heated pickle juice; whole peppercorns and hot peppers; mixed with sundried tomatoes packed in grapeseed oil and capers; slice some to go on pizzas, salads, popcorn and made a great spicy dip with garlic and red onions. Can't wait for my next shipment!!

I tried this out when a friend of mine who worked at a running shoe store recommended me this.

Comparing Sport beans with GU is like comparing Chipotle with Mikey D.

I used this for a full marathon, hikes on Grand Canyon, Mt. Whitey, and Halfdome. Watermelon is my favorite flavor!

review_title: Tastes great, not sure how much it works
review_text: I am a very active person and I got this tea because it was hyped up to be a muscle recovery tea and is loaded with antioxidants. While I haven't been drinking it consistently enough to see if it has helped my recovery periods I do know that it tastes great and has a lot of green tea in it. They are still finding benefits to drinking green tea so I figure it can't hurt. The taste is great, it's green tea, with a very subtle lemon taste. I enjoy it a lot and would reccommend to anyone considering buying it.

review_title: You Cant Make Better Pie Crust! Seriously!!
review_text:
This makes the best pie crust ever. Maybe only better results if you use Lard. Every one raves about my pies! And most serious pie eaters know its the crust that makes the pie! Each box makes FOUR crusts, I think that is a bit of a stretch for a 9 inch pie, if you want a fancy fluted crust anyway. I think it is much easier to work with if you
chill the dough 30-40 min, before rolling out, although directions do not suggest this. Anyway try it and see for yourself! no more pie crust intimidation , only beautiful and delicious pies!
This is a probiotic packet that you can mix in water and it tastes great. It has helped my digestive system tremendously and would highly recommend it.
This product is good for your digestion and is a delight. If you like licorice, you'll like this Australian licorice. I keep it in the refrigerator and eat one or two at a sitting (or standing).

I generally just drink coffee to have some caffeine and a warm drink in the morning. Fancy coffees generally don't do any more for me than Yuban. I do like hazelnut coffee though. And this was one of the better ones that I've tried. It's not bitter, and the taste doesn't try to overwhelm you. Sure, I'll buy it again.

I love this stuff. I'm on my second bottle. Not too hot that you can't pour it on your tacos (YMMV). Slightly garlicky but not overbearingly so. It's not vinegary like Tabasco and it's thick kinda like ketchup. The green color is quite bright and appealing.

I give it four stars because it has preservatives and artificial colorings.

review_title: The perfect jus for a steak
review_text: Using this vinegar with a small amount of beef stock simmer together till it reduces, then add some unsalted butter [about 1oz] a little at a time, add the pre cooked warm steak for a couple of minutes, it is just delicious.

We first tried this on a deep fried flowering onion and it was delicious, so we then tried it with deep frying small chicken pieces, onion rings and shrimp. Everything was wonderful. This is a delicious and versatile product!

I received my batch in November of 2011. This coffee turned out great and makes a nice strong brew that isn't bitter. I like a really strong brew. I use a Bialetti moka pot and a blade grinder to prepare my coffee and it turns out great. I have tried this coffee many different ways; straight, with half and half, and other times with syrups. I will continue to buy.

review_title: use it on all your steaks and burgers
review_text: My wife is from Texas and introduced me to this stuff. I now use it on basically everything I put on the grill (especially beef). Adds a nice subtle flavor and makes the meat look even more appetizing from the nice browning after you pull it off the grill. Fortunately this sauce doesn't require refrigeration after opening or you'd be in trouble because you only need a couple of drops per burger (about the same amount as if you were using Worcestershire sauce). So this bottle will probably last me YEARS. But that's okay... since I've never found it in any supermarket north of Richmond. Enjoy.
This bar is so good, but you need to warm it up in the microwave. They are so much better warm. My kids also love them! @quakersoftbar
Great tasting with the dark chocolate. Chewy but not too sweet. Just like I remembered.

My family believes in an apple a day so this is a great solution when you can't eat fresh. It's so convenient & tastes great. I have tried several other brands of pouch apple sauce & this is by far the best. Only reason I gave it 4 stars is that it's a bit pricey for apple sauce.

I've been using Folger's Coffee Singles for 3 years or so. Since I am the only coffee drinker in the house, they are so simple to use and taste as good as brewed coffee...but no leftovers! I also try to control my caffeine intake and what is more simple than a single serving of real brewed coffee made in your cup. I take them traveling, better than the hotel coffee anyday. The decaf is also great. The answer to camping, work and play.

I really just got this item as a filler, but they sure did make my dog happy. He ate them up in no time, and really enjoyed them. I liked how easy they were to give him, and that they were not messy and that they were actually chewed up and eaten, and I did not find them hidden in my closet the next day. I'd buy them again for him.

I purchased these very large cans of chili because they were priced ridiculously cheaply at the time. I planned to split the cans up into smaller portions and freeze the smaller portions to make a large number of meals. The first can I opened looked a little different from other Hormel chili I have had in the past. The beans were all mashed up and there were large chunks of cubed meat in the chili. Normally the meat resembles little pieces of ground meat. These were weird unappetizing chunks. The chili had no chili taste. It tasted like slightly burned re-fried beans. The night after eating this and the next day, there were numerous trips to the bathroom. Won't give details - but it wasn't pleasant.
A few days later, I opened a second can hoping the first was just a one time thing. The second had the same mashed up beans and meat chunks. I tossed it and now have four large cans of chili that I will never use.
Maybe this was just a bad batch. It almost seems as if they scraped the old chili residue off of their pots, threw in some leftover meat chunks, canned it and sent it to me. Needless to say, I will never purchase this product again.

review_title: Tastes just like the real thing
review_text:
Great for the sweettooth craving something different for the young and old alike.

Especially great when mixed with the other candy corn treats.

Ok, there are plenty of reviews and I might be giving the same review as someone else but whatever. I am on the HCG diet which is why I give these things a four star rating, (otherwise, um...yuk? Tofu and squash noodles are waaaaay better) However, tofu and squash are NOT allowed on the HCG diet ! People, this stuff has no calories, no sugar, no fat...just fiber. What do people expect? Something delicious??? Anyways, this is what I do: quick drain and rinse then throw them into some already boiled water for a minute or two. Meanwhile I have some sort of stir-fry going on in a separate pan (I eat this stuff for dinner) So after the noodles have boiled for about 2 minutes I toss them in there and mix it all up with......secret ingredient......BEEF BULLION!!!! These things soak the beef flavor up and without the bullion I wouldn't bother eating them on the HCG diet. They are still a bit gummy but the added flavor helps A LOT. For no added calories or fat, this stuff is great.
After years of coping with IBS symptoms, I've used the tea and peppermint capsules with AMAZING success. I will be reordering the capsules

What could be better than a mouth full of honey with a caffeinated kick?! The price for Honeystinger seems more justifiable compared to other gels as they're made using 100% made in the USA bee pee. Other gels use corn based products which means they're dependent more heavily on petroleum based industrial agriculture (the price of a pound of maltodextrin--the main calorie containing ingredient in Gu and others-- hasn't even remotely kept pace with inflation thanks to farm subsidies... but it's price has paralleled the price of oil).

I'm still not keen on the little gel packs and prefer to buy bottles and use refillable flasks instead. Also, the honey is of course really, really sweet (so delicious!) which makes it more difficult to get down smoothly.

This cereal is pretty tasty, considering the macros. No, it won't taste like frosted flakes or some other high-carb sugar-laden cereal, but it definitely doesn't taste like cardboard either. Some of the pieces get soft in milk, and other bits stay crunchy - I like the texture. My favorite flavor is Maple Pecan, but the Strawberry is fine as well.

review_title: Great plants and a great buy
review_text:
I just recently decided to purchase this set of plants after doing tons of research online.
I have not had much experience with growing plants in general, but have always been interested in carnivorus plants.
Although the flytraps that I previously bought at Home Depot and Walmart which were inside little terarium domes died very fast.

With that being said about my current "skill" level.
I found these plants were sent very fast and arrived in great condition in plastic zip lock baggies with
some moss and wet gauze wrapped around the roots . As I already expected
most of the traps on the Venus flytrap arrived closed and the sundew was missing its "dew". Even so
the plants looked healthy. After one just one day most of the traps opened and the sundew's "dew" started forming.

So far it seems very easy to take care of these carnivorous plants. I just am following the provided
directions and using the included mesh pots and moss. These plants might actually have some hope of
surviving now. If not it would likely be my fault.

Thinking of buying some more now since these are such cool plants and they seem to have awakened my inner child.
Might post some pictures later.

I could not see paying more shipping then I paid for the popcorn!!!!! :(
Will never buy anything more from this company!

I like the Italian roast but love the Colombian roast, for it's satisfying flavor.
Please stock the Hazelnut Blend ,if available. as I would like to purchase it also.
Thank you.

I had my first Chunky in Canada and fell in love. I brought some home with me and my friends LOVE them as well! Only wish I could go to the store and buy them!!!

review_title: Best flavor of any sea salt I have tried!
review_text: Great product. Yummy! Tastiest sea salt out there as far as I am concerned. Nice price for this much salt. Grains are large (and very salty) so it's easy to over salt stuff, you'll get used to using it quickly. I have bought other sea salts that were much more expensive, but I genuinely prefer this. It's grey in color (that may bother some but not me) and too moist to put in salt grinders (I'm considering trying the fine grain version). Great to use when marinating your finest bad-ass Fred Flintstone-type steaks! Try this, I highly recommend!

What can I say...I luv these Cherry Flavored Pops!The only time I could get these Pops was Valentines Day from the Dollar General Store,and I hated to have to wait a whole year to get them...But now I can order them from Amazon!Thank you Amazon.
nice flavor. I don't usually like the fruity flavored ones. I'm partial to regular iced tea, but this is really good.

This a a perfect solution to my creamer needs. I do not have to refrigerate it. It lasts at least a month for me. It meets my hospitality needs when friends come over (they love it). It's economical as I do not have to keep buying creamers every week. It is far better than the powder version! I would like to see the price go down, have it available in local stores or wholesale stores. My only negative is I have to take the top off when it nears the bottom to get the rest as the pump cannot reach it; however I love the pump idea - it works perfectly for my needs. This would also work great at businesses and church fellowships.

review_title: Yummy & Fresh Marzipan and Dark Chocolate
review_text: This is the second time I ordered this item to mail. This time I forgot the gift card so I called the company and referenced the order and they took care of it for me. I continue to bre very happy with the product and the professionalism of the company.

I order this tea for my husband who has been all but addicted to it for years. It is strong, black, slightly sweet tea with tasty and fragrant oils added. Delivery always takes a week to 10 days even if I order it directly from Market Spice. We discovered it at Pike's Place Market about 30 years ago and have been buying it ever since.

review_title: Reminds me of the Carnation Instant Breakfast of long ago
review_text:
I bought this product for my Aide, who has lived in a few different countries and they always had Milo. I had never heard of it and we couldn't find it in our local CT stores. The directions call for using a quarter cup of warm water to liquify 3 level tablespoons of the powder and then add warm or cold water or whole cream milk and sweeten to your taste. I found it faintly reminiscent of the Carnation Instand Breakfast I drank many years ago, as a teenager. It has a chocolate flavor, slightly malty but grainy. The flavor is not as malty as Ovaltine. I'll have to see if they still make the Carnation Instant Breakfast, that was good with milk.

So, overall it only rates 3 stars because it's good but not great or fantastic!

Life is hectic and it's the small things that matter. A fresh start to each day with a cup of great coffee! The personalized product of the one cup San Francisco Bay is AMAZING! The depth, aroma, flavor, color are far superior to others on the market. Great quality, great price, great cup of coffee-

ADVISORY: This Couscous is "Pre-Cooked".

The description I read did not say it was a "pre-cooked" (I dislike that term because "pre-cooked" means it's raw!). As a person who wanted to learn to make cousocous (this would be my first time working with it), I wanted the original kind, as it is used traditionally, so I was/am a little disappointed with this choice. I will still work with it, obviously, and it works out because it's summer here, but wanted to make sure people know this product is already cooked.

Bag arrived with tape on it, so I hope there are no adverse effects. An update will be posted.

review_title: 3 out of the 4 tins were delicious
review_text:
I was surprised to find the quality and taste of these teas to be fairly high.
Thees teas have a hint of dark chocolate from their leaves, unlike what you would usually find.
Recommended if you willing to put up with one less tin since I was unable to drink "Organic Green Paradise"*.

review_title: love one coconut water with guava
review_text: love this item. Keep reorder it. Use before or after workouts and as a snack. You must try it. You will like it too.

FYI: Marukai Market Place in southern California, currently sells Mochiko Sweet Rice Flour for $1.48/1 pound box. I use it for making a simple mochi which my family enjoys very much.

I don't know why it is so difficult to find this at the supermarket but as long as I can purchase it on Amazon, I'm good.

review_title: This is my 1st experience with Puerh tea & I love it!!
review_text: I love Numi's teas!! They're organic and taste great. The chocolate puerh is my 1st experience with puerh tea. I love the smell and flavor of this product. It smells like chocolate, orange with a hint of vanilla in when it comes out of the bag and has the subtle taste of all three flavors once brewed. I'm glad I tried this puerh... I'll be back for more!

love the water, but the order came from italy and the freight killed me.
buy it at sams club instead and you'll have the same great water on someone elses freight bill
It isn't anything special... plus it is so powdery and light that it doesn't stick to the popcorn... and I felt like I inhaled more than I actually ate of the stuff, not pleasant to say the least.

I started eating these out of desperation when I was put on an anti-candida diet---no gluten, no sugar, no yeast, etc. I fell in love--they are and have been my gluten free go-to cracker ever since (though I got off the diet, I remained gluten and sugar free because I feel better that way.) review_title: Pretty good, although the number one ingredient is wheat.
review_text:
I must state up front that I am not much of a fruity candy lover. That being said, the Pomegranate Licorice is pretty good. You can taste the fruit, but you also get the anise flavor. That is an important distinction as my wife, who loves similar products, didn't like these due to the anise. I personally liked them BECAUSE of the anise flavor.

I didn't care for the fact that the number one ingredient is wheat, but beyond that it appears to be a quality product.

If you are looking for a less messy version of licorice for the children, then be sure to try these! They're soft, easy to chew, and they don't get your hands all sticky and gross in the car, in the summer, at the beach, etc. We love all the flavos and sometimes mix these in with the chocolate to have a very nice snack! Great item, great price too, highly recommend!

Edit: Upgraded to 5-star. They appear to have changed the recipe, as the last couple containers I have acquired have tasted substantially better! It almost tastes like Swiss Miss cocoa (and while Swiss Miss is not really my idea of great cocoa, for a protein drink mixed with water, that is pretty good!)

Summary: This is the best non-whey, non-soy protein I have found, and I would highly recommend it to anyone with soy+whey/dairy sensitivities! I rated this 4 stars, but if you can't have whey, it is definitely a 5-star product.

I'm allergic to both whey and soy, which has made finding a good protein supplement quite a pain! I have tried pretty much everything on the market, including a variety of wheat, buckwheat, and brown rice proteins. If you are looking for the highest quality protein available, you are unfortunately limited to egg, soy, whey, or casein (based upon PDCAAS, the FDA and FAO/WHO's method of determining protein quality), so egg is really preferable to the alternatives. Fortunately, this is, in terms of consistency, digestibility, and nutritive content (0 fat 0 carbs), and overall flavor, the best of the various proteins I tried. In case you are wondering, there is no cholesterol in this protein, either. Texture and overall 'drinkability' are great... I drink it mixed with water with no issues. Flavor is decent: obviously, none of us are drinking protein powder expecting a gourmet flavor extravaganza. This protein is decently tasty if you like chocolate (not as good as most whey/casein proteins, but better than almost all non-whey proteins... I tried quite a few of those as well before discovering that I could not tolerate dairy proteins). Be warned: the vanilla version of this product tastes terrible. In my opinion, it is barely drinkable.

Four stars overall. If I had no food sensitivities, I might still use this in conjunction with whey protein, but would probably not use it exclusively.
Five stars if you can't have whey protein!

I hope that this review is helpful!

This soup is delicious made as directed on the package. The light broth with a hint of lemon juice flavor makes a great bowl of warmth. The hot pepper is in a separate pouch so you can adjust to personal taste.

The stores all carry either the Arnold Palmer Lite(most common, better tasting), or the Zero(less calories, but less flavor). This mix produces an iced tea that tastes like neither of them. Somehow the settling that occurs with this mix, makes it either come out too sweet, or too sour. For one thing, it's sugar based, not splenda like the lite in the stores. The amount the directions say to mix do not give the drink enough flavor, you'll use more than the directions say. I bought this once, but will not buy again.

So I decided to give this flavor a try as well. It's not as good as the Green Tea. Obviously, the tea is very artificial tasting, not like their bottled tea. It's powdered instant tea though so I it's to be expected. However, after a few sips, it grew on me. The fruitiness is there but not over powering. The packaging is very convenient. review_title: Really worked miracles on our cat (and our sanity..no more vomit!!)
review_text:
This food was really a miracle for our cat! My medium coat Maine Coon mix giant (19lbs, breed normal weight) was throwing up daily, sometimes a couple of times per day, for almost a year until I started him on this food. It took 1-2 weeks for his digestive system to settle down and for him to slow down while eating, which was both a bi-product of the vomiting/eating cycle and an additional cause of secondary vomiting.

Several trips to the doctor and far too much $$$ spent on test hadn't helped. One vet suspected IBS, another thought food allergies or extreme hairballs, etc. Tried several other hairball or sensitive stomach/allergy brands for 2 week trails, but none helped until this one. A friend/vet suggested trying this brand and it was a godsend! He has only thrown up once now since we started feeding him this about 4 months ago. He has since added back the weight he lost during that period (was down to 13lbs at one point!!) and his coat seems softer than before too. The food price seemed pretty high, but it hindsight, it was beyond worth it when you consider the alternative health problems and constant cleaning of vomit.

review_title: Take a sharp object to defend your self
review_text:
Unlike most people, I prefer not a weak coffee but a milder roast than the burnt stuff most coffee houses make that requires many accoutrements to mask like cream, sugar and artificial flavors. Therefore, I purposely only tried the Columbian.

I work in a place that has poorly serviced coffee "dispensing machines." They are fun in that you never know what will come out in a day-to-day basis. Some time just your coins back. A few people have illicit electric coffee pots, others use a French press, and still others sneak out for the real thing.

Now that I discovered the Columbian Via. I have to defend my cup from all those noses that circle my cup. I know there are directions on the pack; however, I now have my own ritual. Clean cup, fill 2/3rds filtered water. 90 seconds in the microwave. Shake contents to bottom of pack. Rip the whole top off the pack with teeth. Pour pack in coup and watch sizzle. Stir for a few seconds. Add a tad of ice. Hope I can make it back to my desk before the cup empties.

I was never a fan of reconstituted coffee however I think it is the addition of the micro-ground coffee that adds the edge necessary.

In any event you owe it to yourself to try it.

review_title: Prices change here alot, sometimes good deal, sometimes bad deal
review_text:
Of course, I like this cereal, it's like frosted cheerios with marshmallows, but I wanted to review the internet buying option. Most food products, especially candy, is a rip-off from most Amazon sellers. However, the cereal is usually cheap. I think the 11.5oz was about $2.60 at Wal-Mart, but I paid only $6.86 for the 1 package of 3 boxes here on Amazon, so that is about $2.29 for each box. That was a good deal, but now the page says $15 for 3 boxes, which is $5 a box. That is a rip for the 11.5oz box; you can get a 16oz box for only about $3.00 at Wal-Mart. So you have to watch the prices to see if it's cheaper or not.

I was worried about the cereal not being fresh, but it is. My Lucky Charms were the new ones with the new marshmallows in them; currently it's the different colors of shooting stars. My boxes from Amazon have the same expiration date as the Frosted Flakes I bought at Wal-Mart, so that's a good thing. They taste good too (just in case there is other problems that could be wrong with them).

So overall, check the prices to see if it is worth it, the prices change both in the store and online, alot (mostly they change online, though). I bought from here like a month ago, but wouldn't buy them this second, probably would, again, in the future, though.

MADE IN CHINA, MADE IN CHINA, MADE IN CHINA. Buyers beware if your concerned where your dog treats are made.

Reading the smokehouse home page the following statement says nothing about duck treats so I figured they are safe and made in the USA.

"Smokehouse makes the vast majority of our treats at our plant in Sun Valley, Ca. but we do import some of our chicken treats from China. All imported chicken treats are extensively tested here in the United States by an independent laboratory. No product is shipped out until all lab test are back. In the many years we have been importing and testing these products nothing has ever been found that could be harmful to our pets."

I received my package of duck treats, opened them up to feed my dog while telling my friends I ordered these from amazon because they were not made in china and looked at the back of the package and saw the three dreaded words MADE IN CHINA. Gasp. Return, return, return.

I'm giving these treats four stars because my dog would give them ten if he could, and I would give them one just for the smell alone. I have to open the window and grab the treat with a paper towel and still wash my hands after, but my dog absolutely loves these treats, above all others. review_title: Sweet & carbonated - NOT for the 'faint of heart' like me!
review_text:
Wow, I was quite unprepared for the amount of sweetness packed in this sugary drink! It advertises 'no sugar added' and '100% juice carbonated', so I was expecting somewhat sweet tasting, but not like this ~ it was overwhelmingly sweet, and packed 140 calories, 35g sugar and 36g carbs in a 'no sugar added'-'no corn syrup'- 'no preservatives'-'no artificial colors'-'gluten free' drink! I would expect that from a juice like apple or cranberry, but I would also expect it to TASTE natural, and this one (for all it's claims to the contrary) did NOT taste very natural to me.

It was certainly pleasant tasting, but for the tastebuds of someone who WAS a lifelong soda drinker (diet soda for the past 12 years), and gave up soda completely last May (I don't miss it at all!), this is not the drink for me. I mostly drink water, iced tea, milk, hot tea & coffee now, and this sweet tasting, sugary tasting beverage, had way too much carbonation & flavoring for me! The watermelon & strawberry flavors were nice, but TASTED fake, which is weird for a drink that touts things like '100% juice' and a label that says 'natural flavors'...I would recommend this product only to those who like super-sweet carbonated beverages, and aren't worried about calories, etc.

So once i got home, I ordered a variety of the Douwe Egberts coffee and loved them all, but the Intense is the closest to the Bellagio joe. Discerning coffee friends also loved it when they had some, and asked what it was. You have to try this. Yes, it's ground, but it is vacuum sealed into a hard block, and seems to lose no aroma or flavor being pre-ground.

review_title: A CHRISTMAS Basket for MOTHER'S Day = Unhappy Customer
review_text:

If I had known, I would have opted for a more appropriate gift for the occasion. I wanted to return it to resend a different basket but later realized that the item was ineligible to be returned.

In addition, although I indicated that this was a gift and wrote a note to be included with the package, Amazon did not include the note and my grandmother had no idea who it was from. I am very unsatisfied with this product and am very unhappy that Mother's Day was somewhat ruined thanks to Amazon's inability to describe this as a Christmas gift or to include the note that I requested.

review_title: Honestly, If you like coffee and need more protein, this is it
review_text: I don't write too many reviews, but after trying Click I thought I would share a review in the hopes that it sways some people to try it. I first must admit I'm a 20-something guy who has tried a number of protein products in my day, some good, most bad to terrible. My personal favorite is muscle milk light due to its flavor and ease of mixing. After trying Click, I can't imagine a better protein drink early in the morning. The flavor is excellent (I prefer Vanilla Latte over Mocha personally) and it mixes with ease. Great in water or milk. Recently I've been blending it in the morning with Almond Milk and a few ice cubes and it's awesome. I usually don't like Vanilla Latte drinks at Starbucks because the vanilla is overpowering but not with this mix. It's really not even accurate to call it "Vanilla Latte". I think its just an improved Mocha flavor, which is what I'd prefer. 15 grams of protein in the morning helps curb your appetite and its also low in sugar in fat which is usually a problem with protein drinks. Biggest problem I can find is that Click is clearly being marketed towards women given the bright pink/purple canister and female stick figure on the label. Honestly, this is a product that both health conscience men and women should be happy to use in the morning. Right now I don't know another company that is offering a product like this (Muscle Milk has a coffee flavor but it's not as good) and given how important breakfast is to a healthy diet and a weight loss plan, Click is a great addition to your pantry. The price is fair, especially since you'll get a discount through Amazon. If your interested in losing weight, give Click a try. It's less than you'll pay for a week of bagels and coffee at Starbucks and a whole lot healthier for you.
